questions.question: Fibres are:
questions.explanation: Fibres may be synthetic like nylon, natural like cotton and wool or artificial fibre like rayon. These fibres are used to make different types of fabrics and number of household articles.

questions.question: PVC is:
questions.explanation: PVC stands for Polyvinyl Chloride. It is a thermoplastic and most widely used for the manufacture of sewage pipes.

questions.question: Which of the following set of features belong to thermoplastics?
questions.explanation: Thermoplastics are soft and less brittle in nature. Thermoplastics get deformed easily on heating and can be bent and reshaped into desired products easily. Example: polythene and PVC.

questions.question: Cellulose is used to prepare:
questions.explanation: Rayon is also called cellulosic fibre. Cellulose of wood pulp is used to prepare rayon which is also called artificial silk.

questions.question: Best alternative to wool is:
questions.explanation: Acrylic is best alternative to wool. Acrylic is soft, lightweight and warm. Acrylic fabrics are cheaper than wool and resistant to weathering which makes them more popular than natural fibres.

questions.question: Statement incorrect for synthetic fibres is/are:
questions.explanation: Synthetic fibres are long lasting and less expensive than natural fibres. They are prepared by a number of processes using raw materials of petroleum origin.

questions.question: First synthetic fibre formed from natural material is:
questions.explanation: First synthetic fibre formed from natural material is Rayon. It is prepared by chemical treatment of cellulose of wood pulp.

questions.question: First man-made fibre produced chemically is:
questions.explanation: Nylon is first man-made fibre produced chemically using coal, water, and air, no plant or animal raw material are used to make nylon. Nylon fibre is strong, elastic and light. It is lustrous and easy to wash.

questions.question: The lightest plastic material is:
questions.explanation: Polypropylene is the lightest plastic material which is used for packing and labelling. Laboratory equipment are also made from polypropylene.

questions.question: Which of the following fibres are made from petroleum products?
questions.explanation: All the synthetic fibres are made from petrochemicals, materials of petroleum origin. It includes terylene, decron, and acrylon, etc.

questions.question: A strand of a substance is called:
questions.explanation: A thin strand of a substance which can be changed into fabrics is called fibre. Fibre may be synthetic, artificial, or natural depending upon source of origin.

questions.question: Which one is artificial silk?
questions.explanation: Rayon is called as artificial silk because it is shiny and lustrous like silk. It is obtained from wood pulp containing cellulose.

questions.question: When plastics are burnt they release:
questions.explanation: When plastics are burnt, they release toxic gases that includes carbon monoxide, sulphur dioxide, etc into the environment, leading to increased air pollution.

questions.question: Which of the following is incorrect regarding plastics:
questions.explanation: Plastics can be recycled. Thermoplastics can be melted and reshaped into other products.

questions.question: A polymer is a large molecule composed of many repeated units. What is a polyester composed of?
questions.explanation: Polyester (Poly+ester) is a category of polymers, made up of the repeating units of a type of chemical called ester. Polyester fibres are strong, resistant to stretching, and shrinking. They dry quickly, are wrinkle resistant, and can be easily washed.

questions.question: We should never dispose off polythenes in the sewage system because:

options.option: it chokes drains and pollutes the soil.

questions.explanation: We should never dispose off polythene in the sewage system because polythene chokes drains leading to overflowing on roads and streets and also pollutes the soil since it takes long time to decompose.

questions.question: The 4th R added to 3R concept is:
questions.explanation: The only way to manage plastic is to follow the 3R's principle of reduce, reuse, and recycle. Later 4th R  i e Recover is also added to environmental friendly habits.

questions.question: The scratch proof and corrosion resistant low friction protective coating on metal surface is:
questions.explanation: Teflon is scratch proof and corrosion resistant low friction protective coating on metal surface. It is coated on cookware to make them scratch resistant.

questions.question: All kinds of fibres are:
questions.explanation: All kinds of fibres are polymers. Polymers are made up of number of repeated units of monomers all join together.

questions.question: A regenerated synthetic fibre is one which is made up of:
questions.explanation: Regenerated synthetic fibre is one which is made by chemical treatment of natural material. Rayon is obtained from chemical treatment of cellulose present in wood pulp.

questions.question: A nylon fibre is made entirely from chemicals hence it is:
questions.explanation: A nylon fibre is made entirely from chemicals hence it is true synthetic fibre. It is the first non-cellulosic polymer and does not used any plant or animal raw material. It is made from coal.air and water. Most of the synthetic fibres are petroleum products and formed by chemical treatment.

questions.question: We can control the physical properties of synthetic fibres by:
questions.explanation: The physical properties of fibre can be changed by rearranging the monomers, the inter and intrachain bonding etc.

questions.question: Bakelite do not melt on heating because such:

options.option: polymers are attached by strong linear chains
polymers are joined by strong cross links

questions.explanation: Bakelite is a thermosetting plastic. And thermosetting plastics does not melt on heating because such polymers are joined by strong cross links.

questions.question: _________ is the first fully synthetic fibre.
questions.explanation: Nylon was the first fully synthetic fibre made in 1931, without using any natural raw material. It was prepared from chemicals. Nylon fibres are strong, lustrous, elastic and used for making stockings, car belts, parachutes, ropes, etc.

questions.question: Nylon absorbs very little water hence it is most suitable for making:
questions.explanation: Nylon absorbs very little water hence it is most suitable for making fishing net, parachute, and tyre cords. Nylon is resistant to water, light, elastic, dries out quickly, and very strong.

questions.question: Plastics are classified as thermoplastic and thermosetting plastic based on their behaviour:
questions.explanation: Plastics are classified as thermoplastic and thermosetting plastic based on their behaviour on heating. Thermoplastic can be melted heated and harden again when cooled but thermosetting plastic does not melt when heated and cannot be moulded. Once moulded into a shape they are permanently shaped.

questions.question: Polythene is obtained by polymerization of:
questions.explanation: Polythene as the name suggests is polyethylene. Polythene is obtained by polymerization of ethylene monomers. Repeated units of ethylene join in a linear fashion to form polythene.

questions.question: Thermoplastics can be:
questions.explanation: Thermoplastics can be destroyed by heating. Thermoplastics are bad conductor of heat and electricity. It can be recycled since it can be melted on heating and reshaped to other products.

questions.question: Nylon does not contain:
questions.explanation: Nylon is prepared by using coal, water, and air. It is first true synthetic fibre. Nylon is the first non-cellulosic polymer.

questions.question: Which of the following is not included in 3R concept:
questions.explanation: 3R’s are reduce, recycle, and reuse to manage plastics and develop the environ friendly habit. React is not included in 3R that creates good environmental practice.

questions.question: It is dangerous to burn plastics because:
questions.explanation: Burning of plastics release a number of gases that includes carcinogenic substance that can cause cancer. So, burning of plastic is dangerous for health and also to the environment.

questions.question: To overcome disadvantages of synthetic fibres:
questions.explanation: To overcome disadvantages of synthetic fibres, they are mixed with natural fibres. This mixing of natural and synthetic fibre is called blending. Terycotton and polywool are examples of blended fibres.

questions.question: Sooraj threw away plastic bags near his house. Next day he saw his cow dead close by those bags:

options.option: she had swallowed those bags and the bags had choked her throat. The cow died due to chokking and suffocation.
she had eaten food kept inside those bags

questions.explanation: she had swallowed those bags

questions.question: Atul was climbing mountain and he wondered about the strength of rope in his hand which was stronger than a steel wire. This rope would have been made up of:
questions.explanation: Nylon is synthetic fibre which is used to make rope and other household articles. The strength of rope made of nylon is stronger than a steel wire and it is also elastic and light, which makes it easier to carry. Strength is due to cross linking polymerisation.

questions.question: Littering of plastic materials leads to:
questions.explanation: Littering of plastic materials leads to solid waste pollution. Plastic is non-biodegradable material and remain undecomposed for long duration. Accumulation of plastics material on earth surface leads to solid waste pollution and cause a lot of environmental damage.

questions.question: Bakelite is formed by condensation and polymerisation of:
questions.explanation: Bakelite is a thermosetting plastic. Bakelite is formed by condensation and polymerisation of phenol and formaldehyde. It is a poor conductor of heat and electricity. It is used for making electrical switches and handles of utensils.

questions.question: Cellulose of wood pulp is chemically treated to produce:
questions.explanation: Rayon is obtained by chemical treatment of cellulose of wood pulp. Rayon is also called as artificial silk.

questions.question: Making of base of magnetic recording tape needs:
questions.explanation: Base of magnetic recording tape is manufactured from polyester. Polyester is made up of the repeating units of a chemical called ester. PET and terylene are common polyesters.

questions.question: We should not wear synthetic clothes in kitchen because:

options.option: they are non conductors of heat and electricity

questions.explanation: Synthetic fibres catches fire easily, therefore, we should not wear synthetic clothes in kitchen. On burning they melt easily and stick to the body surface to make burns more severe.

questions.question: Dyeing refers to:
questions.explanation: Dying refers to applying colour on the surface of a fabric. Artificial or manmade fibres can be dye into different colours.

questions.question: A user of synthetic fibres feels uncomfortable in warm weather:

options.option: synthetic fibres provide resistance to fungal attack

questions.explanation: Synthetic fibres are more comfortable in warm weather as synthetic fibres do not breathe well. Air space is absent in synthetic fibre which make them unfit for summer and also they do not absorb sweat. They are water resistant.

questions.question: Nylon is a:
questions.explanation: Nylon is a true synthetic fibre because nylon is prepared from coal, water, and air. It is made from non-cellulosic polymer. No natural substance obtained from plants and animals are used in preparation of nylon.

questions.question: Yarn is twisted to form:
questions.explanation: Yarn is twisted to form thread. Thread is a type of yarn intended for sewing by hand or machine. Yarn is a long continuous length of interlocked fibre.

questions.question: Recycled plastic can be produced from:
questions.explanation: Recycled plastic can be produced from used thermoplastic. Thermoplastic can be melted by heating and can be moulded into other desired products. This plastic can be used to produce recycled plastic articles.

questions.question: Sunita found that a plastic can be moulded into any shape one wants and a number of times, such plastic is:
questions.explanation: A plastic that can be moulded into any shape one wants and a number of times is polythene. Polythene is a thermoplastics and they can be melted by heating and changed into new products.

questions.question: Crushing the plastic bottles and containers initiates its:
questions.explanation: Crushing the plastic bottles and containers initiates its recycling. Plastic bottles and other containers should be crushed to avoid reuse without sterilisation.

questions.question: _____ of plastics releases foul smell and toxic gases:
questions.explanation: Incineration is a waste treatment process by burning. Incineration of plastics release foul smell and toxic gases into the environment that leads to air pollution.

questions.question: Which of the following can be beaten into thin sheets?
questions.explanation: Generally metals are malleable.

questions.question: Rayon is different from synthetic fibers because

options.option: Its fibers can also be woven like those of natural fibers.
Its fibers are stronger then other fibers

questions.explanation: It is obtained from wood pulp.

questions.question: Which of the following is a source of rayon?
questions.explanation: Wood pulp

questions.question: Polycot is obtained by mixing
questions.explanation: Polyester and cotton

questions.question: Which is a thermosetting plastic?
questions.explanation: Melamine

questions.question: The material similar to silk in appearance is
questions.explanation: Rayon

questions.question: Which of the following is not a common property of plastics?
questions.explanation: Good conductor of electricity

questions.question: The most suitable material for the preparation of handles of cooking utensils is
questions.explanation: Bakelite

questions.question: Which of the following groups contain all synthetic substances?
questions.explanation: PVC, Polythene, Bakelite

questions.question: The material which is commonly used for making kitchen containers is
questions.explanation: PET

questions.question: Pick the synthetic fibre out of the following?
questions.explanation: Nylon is a synthetic fibre. It is made without using any natural raw material. Cotton, jute, and wool are natural fibres.

questions.question: Which natural polymer makes cotton fibre?
questions.explanation: Cotton is made up of a natural polymer called cellulose in which glucose molecules are joined together one after the other.

questions.question: Which synthetic fibre is obtained by chemical treatment of wood pulp?
questions.explanation: Rayon is a synthetic fibre obtained by the chemical treatment of wood pulp.

questions.question: Which is the first fully synthetic fibre made from coal, water, and air?
questions.explanation: Nylon is a synthetic fibre. It is made in 1931 without using any natural raw materials. It is made from coal, water, and air. It was the first fully synthetic fibre.

questions.question: Which synthetic fibre is made up of many repeating units of esters?
questions.explanation: Polyester is a synthetic fibre made up of many repeating units of esters.

questions.question: Which synthetic fibre offers a less expensive alternate to natural wool?
questions.explanation: Acrylic is a synthetic fibre which is often used as a substitute for wool due to its wool-like feel. The wool obtained from natural sources is very costly. Acrylic offers a less expensive alternative to natural wool.

questions.question: Which synthetic material can be moulded into the desired shape when soft and hardened to produce a durable article?
questions.explanation: Plastic is a synthetic material that can be moulded into the desired shape when soft and then hardened to produce a durable article.

questions.question: Which of the following articles are made out of thermosetting plastic?
questions.explanation: Cooking utensils are made up of thermosetting plastics which when heated for the first time becomes soft and can be moulded to make the utensil of any desired shape and on cooling, becomes hard and rigid.

questions.question: Which kind of articles are made up of thermoplastics?

options.option: They should be flexible and not get too hot.

questions.explanation: Thermoplastics are used in making those articles which do not get too hot and are flexible.

questions.question: Which synthetic fibre is used as artificial silk?
questions.explanation: Rayon is a synthetic fibre having similar properties to that of silk. So, it is used as artificial silk.

questions.question: What is the reason for the durability of nylon fibre?
questions.explanation: Nylon has high abrasion resistance so it is very durable.

questions.question: Why the nylon cloth dries quickly?
questions.explanation: Nylon absorbs very little water, so clothes made from nylon are easy to wash and quick to dry.

questions.question: Which synthetic fibre is used for making ropes for mountain climbing?
questions.explanation: Nylon is used in making ropes for mountain climbing.

questions.question: Why polyester is used in making fabrics for sarees, dress materials, and curtains.

options.option: Both easy to wash and wrinkle resistance

questions.explanation: Nylon is wrinkle-resistant and is easy to wash and dry. So, it is used in making fabrics for sarees, dress materials, and curtains.

questions.question: Which synthetic fibre is often used as a substitute for wool?
questions.explanation: Acrylic is a synthetic fibre which is often used as a substitute for wool due to its wool-like feel.

questions.question: Why electrical wires have plastic covering?
questions.explanation: Plastics are poor conductors of heat and electricity. That is why electrical wires have plastic coverings.

questions.question: Why we use plastic containers at home for storing various materials?
questions.explanation: Plastics do not corrode easily. They are non-reactive so they do not react with air, water, or any chemical so it is safe to store many kinds of materials including chemicals and hence, are used as plastic containers at home for storing various materials.

questions.question: Which synthetic fibre is used in making sweaters, shawls, jackets, and blankets?
questions.explanation: Acrylic is used in making sweaters, shawls, jackets, and blankets as it gives wool-like feel.

questions.question: What is nylon?
questions.explanation: Nylon is a generic designation for a family of synthetic polymers composed of polyamides. Nylon is a thermoplastic silky material, generally made from petroleum, that can be melt-processed into fibers, films, or shapes.

questions.question: What type of polymer is bakelite?
questions.explanation: Bakelite or polyoxybenzylmethylenglycolanhydride was the first plastic made from synthetic components. It is a thermosetting phenol formaldehyde resin, formed from a condensation reaction of phenol with formaldehyde. It is a type of hybrid polymer.

questions.question: Who had propounded the structure of Benzene?
questions.explanation: German chemist August Kekule visualized the ring structure of benzene in 1865. Most organic chemical compounds contain loops of six carbon atoms called benzene rings.

questions.question: Polythene is a polymer, which is obtained from the polymerisation of the following-
questions.explanation: Polythene is obtained by the polymerisation of ethylene.

questions.explanation: Cloth material similar in appearance as silk but is not silk, cloth material is rayon, it is a semi-synthetic fiber, made from natural sources of regenerated cellulose, such as wood and related agricultural products.

questions.question:
Which one is biodegradable?

  1. PVC bag
  2. Aluminium
  3. Bakelite
  4. Paper
questions.explanation: Paper is biodegradable a material which gets decomposed through natural processes such as the action of bacteria is called biodegradable material.

questions.question: Which set of fibres is of natural fibre only?
questions.explanation: Cotton, silk and wool set of fibres is natural fibre, fibers that are produced by geological processes or from the bodies of plants or animals.

questions.question: Teflon is the polymer of-
questions.explanation: PTFE is a fluorocarbon solid (at room temperature) as it is a high molecular weight polymer consisting wholly of carbon and fluorine. Other names Fluon, Poly (tetrafluroethene), Poly (difluoromethylene), Poly (tetrafluoroethylene).

questions.question: Which of the following is the polymer of polythene?
questions.explanation: Ethylene is the polymer of polythene. It is a hydrocarbon whose formula is C2H4. It is widely used in the chemical industry.

questions.question: In which stage of its life cycle the silkworm produces commercial fibre?
questions.explanation: A weave is netted around by the silkworm in the pupa process to hold itself. After that, it moves to and fro its head and spins a protein- based fiber and becomes a silk fiber.

questions.question: Synthetic fibre is-
questions.explanation: Synthetic fabrics and textiles made from man-made rather than natural fibres. Some examples of synthetic fabrics are polyester, acrylic, nylon, rayon, acetate, spandex, latex and Kevlar. Synthetic fibres are made by the joining of monomers into polymers by the process of polymerization.

questions.question: Rayon is obtained from ________
questions.explanation: Rayon is described as a regenerated fibre because the cellulose, obtained from soft wood-pulp which is converted into soluble compound which is chemically solidified to form synthetic fiber.

questions.question:
Which of the following is man made fibre?

  1. Silk
  2. Polyester
  3. Nylon
  4. Wool
questions.explanation: Manmade fibres are made from various chemicals or are regenerated from plant fibres. Examples of manmade fibres are: polyester; polyamide - (nylon); acrylics; viscose, made from wood bark; Kevlar, a high-performance fibre; and Nomex, a high-performance fibre.

questions.question: Which of the following is different from the others?
questions.explanation: Teflon is a synthetic polymer containing carbon and fluorine called polytetrafluoroethylene. PTFE has one of the lowest coefficients of friction of any solid. PTFE is used as a non-stick coating for pans and other cookware. It is non-reactive, partly because of the strength of carbon-fluorine bonds, and so it is often used in containers and pipe work for reactive and corrosive chemicals.

questions.question:
Which one of the following is not a natural resource?

  1. Water
  2. Minerals
  3. Soil
  4. Rayon
questions.explanation: Rayon is not a natural resource. Rayon is a man-made redeveloped cellulose fiber. It is made from natural sources such as wood and agricultural products. Rayon fiber is a synthetic textile material.

questions.question: Which is a natural fibre?
questions.explanation: Natural fibres are fibres that come from animals or plants. Wool is entirely a natural fibre grown by Australian Merino sheep. Merino sheep are free-range animals managed by woolgrowers using modern farming practices that protect the environment while ensuring the sheep are healthy. Wool is a completely renewable fibre resource.

questions.question: Which of the following is true for thermosetting polymers?
questions.explanation: All of these

questions.explanation: Polythene is a thermoplastic. Not all plastics but only thermoplastics can be softened on heating. Plastics are poor conductors of heat and electricity.

questions.explanation: Artificial silk being semisynthetic is not biodegradable. Both cotton and artificial silk are not polyesters and also cotton is not crease resistant. Both cotton and artificial silk have cellulose as the monomer.

questions.question:
Rubina took four pieces of clothes of equal size made of different type of fibres and weighed them. She soaked all the pieces in a beaker filled with water and kept them for 25 minutes. Then she took out these cloth pieces and weighed them one by one again. She noted down her observations in the given table.

Cloth piece Weight before soaking (g) Weight after soaking (g)
I 20 25
II 15 18
III 10 11
IV 30 45

I, II, III and IV could be respectively
questions.explanation:
Water absorption capacity of the given fibres varies as:
Wool > Cotton > Silk > Nylon
Hence, fibre IV which absorbs maximum amount of water is wool, I is cotton, II is silk and III is nylon which absorbs least amount of water.

questions.question: X is a thermoplastic polymer of polyester family. It can be recycled, is less gas permeable and produces CO2 and H2O on complete combustion. Identify X
questions.explanation: PET (polyethylene terephthalate) can be made into a fibre or a plastic. It is less gas permeable as compared to other low cost plastics hence is used to store carbonated drinks, acidic drinks and juices etc. It can be recycled. It contains carbon, hydrogen and oxygen hence on complete combustion produces CO2and H2O.

questions.question: Mineral is:
questions.explanation: Mineral is a naturally occurring material obtained from earth crust. Mineral contain one or more element.

questions.question: Non metal that conducts electricity is:
questions.explanation: Non-metal are generally bad conductor of electricity but Graphite is an allotropes of carbon that is good conductor of electricity.

questions.question: Metals are:
questions.explanation: Metals are electropositive because metals contain 1,2 or 3 electron in outermost shell and have a tendency to lose electron and form a cation. Metals form electrovalent bond by loosing electron and become electropositive.

questions.question: A metal is characterised by:
questions.explanation: Metal conducts electricity because they contain a free electron which can move through the metal and conduct electric current.

questions.question: Non metals occur as:
questions.explanation: Non-metals are found in nature in solid, liquid, and gaseous state. On the other hand, metals are found in solid state except mercury that is liquid at room temperature.

questions.question: Metals that react with both acids and bases are called:
questions.explanation: Metals that reacts with both acids and bases are called amphoteric. Amphoteric oxides are acidic as well as basic in nature.e.g zinc oxide

questions.question: Metalloids are also known as:
questions.explanation: Those elements that show the property of both metal and non-metal are called metalloid or semi metals. Silicon, germanium, etc are common metalloid.

questions.question: Metals are solid except:
questions.explanation: All metals are found in solid state at normal room temperature except mercury, which is liquid at room temperature.

questions.question: Metal oxides are:
questions.explanation: Metal oxides are basic in nature. When metal oxides react with water, they form bases.

questions.question: Bases turn:
questions.explanation: Bases turns red litmus blue but acids turn blue litmus red. Litmus is extracted from lichens that give different colour with different pH.

questions.question: The metal which can form a barrier layer of oxide on its surface is:
questions.explanation: Aluminium form a barrier layer of oxide on its surface which prevents the further corrosion of aluminium on heating in presence of water.

questions.question: Common salt when react with sulphuric acid produces:
questions.explanation:
When common salt reacts with sulphuric acid, hydrochloride gas is produced along with sodium bisulphate.
NaCl + H2SO4 {tex}\to{/tex} NaHSO4 + HCl

questions.question: Displacement reactions are not shown by:
questions.explanation: The reaction in which more reactive element displaces the less reactive metal from its compound is called displacement reaction. Oxygen never shows displacement reaction.

questions.question: Noble metals are different from base metals because:

options.option: they are non resistant to corrosion and oxidation
they are resistant to corrosion and oxidation
their economic value is low so not used nobly

questions.explanation: Noble metals are different from base metals because noble metals are resistant to corrosion and oxidation. These metals are lustrous having shiny surface.

questions.question: High density of most metals is due to:
questions.explanation: High density of most metals is due to tightly packed crystals lattice. Lattice of metals are closely packed due to high electrostatic force of attraction.

questions.question: The molecular formula of Sodium oxide should be:
questions.explanation: Molecular formula of sodium oxide is Na2O. The valency of sodium is one and that of oxygen is 2 that results into formation of compound Na2O.

questions.question: Mercury is used in thermometers due to:
questions.explanation: Mercury is used in thermometer due to its liquid nature and shiny color. It expands easily with an increase in temperature.

questions.question: Sodium and Potassium can even float on water because:
questions.explanation: Sodium and Potassium floats on water surface due to very low density. The density of sodium and potassium metal is less than that of water.

questions.question: The acidic oxides react with water to form:
questions.explanation: Non-metal oxides react with water to form acids as non-metal oxides are acidic in nature.

questions.question: Metals are used for making wires of musical instruments due to:
questions.explanation: Metals are used for making wires of musical instruments because metals are sonorous in nature and produce specific sound when strike with hard object.

questions.question: Sodium and Potassium are always kept in kerosene but Magnesium is not because:
questions.explanation: The ignition temperature of sodium and potassium is very low and catch fire if come in contact of oxygen. To prevent accidental fire, sodium and potassium is kept immersed under kerosene oil. Magnesium have high ignition temperature.

questions.question: At a higher temperature, the metal is:
questions.explanation: The ductility of metal increase with increase in temperature, so metal is more ductile at higher temperature than at room temperature.

questions.question: Tungsten is used in incandescent bulb because tungsten:
questions.explanation: Tungsten is used in incandescent bulb because tungsten has very high melting point and it has very high resistivity. It converts electrical energy into light energy.

questions.question: The non-metal used in food packaging and canning is:
questions.explanation: Nitrogen gas is very less reactive and prevents oxidation of food. Nitrogen gas is used in food packaging and canning.

questions.question: Which reaction will not release hydrogen gas?
questions.explanation: Magnesium oxide will not release hydrogen gas when react with water. Magnesium hydroxide is formed in this reaction.

questions.question: Steel is an alloy of:
questions.explanation: Alloys are homogenous mixture of two or more metal or non metal in a fixed ration. Steel is an alloy of iron and carbon.

questions.question: Two non-metals of greatest significance to life on earth are:
questions.explanation: Water the basic need of life is made up of hydrogen and oxygen. So, hydrogen and oxygen are two non metals which are the gretest significance of life on earth.

questions.question: The process of coating a thin layer of Zinc on surface of iron object can be called:
questions.explanation: The process of coating a thin layer of zinc on surface of iron object is called galvanisation. Zinc coating provide high resistant to corrosion.

questions.question: Nowadays base metal is used for making:
questions.explanation: Base metals like silver and copper is used for making coins. These metals have characteristic look and remain same for longer duration.

questions.question: Which non metal is required for proper functioning of thyroid:
questions.explanation: Thyroid gland is important gland that produce thyroxin hormone essential for metabolism of carbohydrates, proteins and fats. Iodine is essential for functioning of thyroid.

questions.question: Copper and Zinc mixture forms:
questions.explanation: The homogenous mixture of copper and zinc form an alloy called as brass. Brass is used to make utensils and statues.

questions.question: Which of the following metals catch fire on reaction with air?
questions.explanation: Sodium and potassium both are extremely reactive and react with air as well as water vigorously. The reactions are highly exothermic and hence, the hydrogen gas evolved as byproduct catches fire.

questions.question: Bronze alloy is made with:
questions.explanation: Bronze alloy is made with copper and tin. Alloy is homogenous mixture of two metal or a metal and a non metal.

questions.question: Which reaction is not feasible?
questions.explanation:
Any metal will displace another metal only if it is more reactive than the other metal. The reactivity of different metals is given by the reactivity series. Following is the reactivity series.

As can be seen from above series, iron is less reactive than zinc, because of which it is enabled to displace zinc from ZnSO4.

questions.question: Wrought iron is the:
questions.explanation: Wrought iron is the pure form of iron. It is converted into steel by adding carbon in it. Wrought iron is used to make agricultural implements.

questions.question: The reaction X + Y {tex}\to{/tex} XY indicates that:
questions.explanation:
The reaction in which two or more substances combine together to form single compound is called combination reaction.
2 Na + Cl2 {tex}\to{/tex} 2NaCl

questions.question: Stainless steel has composition that includes:
questions.explanation: Stainless steel is an alloy of iron, chromium, and nickel. It does not corrode in the presence of air and water. It is used to make surgical instruments and other utensils.

questions.question: Which of the following can be beaten into thin sheets?
questions.explanation: Zinc is metal. All the metals can be beaten into thin sheets with a hammer. So, Zinc can be beaten into thin sheets

questions.question: Which of the following is not a metal?
questions.explanation: Sulphur

questions.question: The substance that will be flattened on beating with a hammer is
questions.explanation: Zinc granule

questions.question: Materials which can be drawn into wires are called ductile. Which of the following is not a ductile material?
questions.explanation: Sulphur is not ductile because it's a non-metal.

questions.question: Metals are generally hard. Which of the following metals is an exception and can be cut with a knife?
questions.explanation: Metals are generally hard. This means that most of the metals cannot be cut easily. But sodium metal is soft and can be easily cut with a knife.

questions.question: Metals are generally solid. Which of the following metals is in the liquid state at room temperature?
questions.explanation: Mercury has a melting point of -38.9oC, a boiling point of 356.7oC, and is the only metal to remain in liquid form at room temperature.

questions.question: Metals generally react with dilute acids to produce hydrogen gas. Which one of the following metals does not react with dilute hydrochloric acid?
questions.explanation: Copper

questions.question: Which of the following reacts with cold water vigorously?
questions.explanation: Sodium

questions.question: The metal which produces hydrogen gas on reaction with dilute hydrochloric acid as well as sodium hydroxide solution is
questions.explanation: Aluminium

questions.question: Which of the following non-metals reacts and catches fire on exposure to air?
questions.explanation: Phosphorus

questions.question: Generally metallic oxides are basic and non-metallic oxides are acidic in nature. Solution of which of the following oxides in water will change the colour of blue litmus to red?
questions.explanation: Sulphur is a non-metal and it forms an acidic oxide, i.e. sulphur dioxide (SO2) which turns blue litmus red.

questions.question: Which of the following property is not responsible for copper to be used as electrical conduction wires?
questions.explanation: Colour

questions.question: Boojho has learnt that non-metals on beating with a hammer are generally broken into pieces. Which of the following is a nonmetal?
questions.explanation: Piece of coal, when stroked with a hammer, breaks into pieces. So it's a non-metal.

questions.question: Which property of metal is used in making aluminium foils?
questions.explanation: Metals are malleable which means they can be beaten into thin sheets with a hammer. Hence, malleability is used in making aluminum foils.

questions.question: Which non-metal has a shiny appearance?
questions.explanation: Non-metals have a dull appearance. Only iodine has a shiny appearance.

questions.question: Which of the following substance does not conduct heat and electricity?
questions.explanation: Sulphur is a non-metal which do not conduct heat and electricity.

questions.question: Which of the following substance, when stretched breaks into small pieces?
questions.explanation: Non-metals are not ductile. Phosphorous is a non-metal which when stretched, breaks into small pieces.

questions.question: Which non-metal is a good conductor of electricity?
questions.explanation: Non-metals are poor conductors of heat and electricity. However, graphite is a non-metal which is a good conductor of electricity.

questions.question: During rusting, which component of air combines with iron in the presence of water?
questions.explanation: During rusting of iron, iron metal combines slowly with the oxygen of air in the presence of water to form a compound called iron oxide, which is called rust.

questions.question: What is the product formed when a metal reacts with water?
questions.explanation: When a metal reacts with water, then a metal hydroxide and hydrogen gas are formed.

questions.question: Which type of reaction takes place when a strip of zinc metal is placed in an iron sulphate solution?
questions.explanation: When a strip of zinc metal is placed in copper sulphate solution for some time, then the blue colour of copper sulphate solution fades gradually and a red-brown coating of copper metal is deposited on zinc strip. In this reaction, a more reactive metal ‘zinc’ displaces a less reactive metal ‘copper’ from its salt solution. Hence, it is a displacement reaction.

questions.question: What is the product formed when metals react with dilute acids?
questions.explanation: Most of the metals react with dilute acids to form salts and hydrogen gas that burns with a ‘pop’ sound.

questions.question: Which metal is placed at the top of the reactivity series of metals?
questions.explanation: In the reactivity series of metals, potassium is the most reactive metal, so it has been placed at the top.

questions.question: Which property of metals allows us to make jewellery?
questions.explanation: The metals are lustrous which means that they have shiny appearance due to which they are used in making jewellery.

questions.question: Which non-metal is used in making crackers?
questions.explanation: Sulphur and phosphorous are used in making crackers.

questions.question: Which non-metal is used in the production of fertilizer and thus enhance the fertility of soil?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen is used in making fertilizers to enhance the growth of plants.

questions.question: Which gas is taken up by plants and animals for breathing?
questions.explanation: Oxygen is used by plants and animals including human beings for breathing.

questions.question: Aluminium foils are used for packaging
questions.explanation: Aluminium foils are used for packaging medicines, chocolates, food items, and many other materials.

questions.question: Which of the following metal/s is/are used for making cooking utensils?
questions.explanation: All these metals - Iron, copper, and aluminium metals are used for making cooking utensils.

questions.question: Which metal is used in making electrical wires for household wiring and electric motors?
questions.explanation: Copper is highly ductile metal which is used in making electrical wires for household wiring and electric motors.

questions.question: Which non-metal is highly reactive and is used in making matchsticks?
questions.explanation: Phosphorous is a highly reactive non-metal and is used in making matchsticks.

questions.question: Which property of metals is used in making bells?
questions.explanation: Metals are sonorous which means that they produce a ringing sound when we strike them. That is why they are used to make bells.

questions.explanation: electroplating

questions.question: Stainless steel is corrosion resistant because of
questions.explanation: alloying

questions.question: Which of the following elements is highly resistant to corrosion?
questions.explanation: Nickel

questions.explanation: Cu

questions.question:
Pick the odd one out
titanium, bronze, solder, gun metal
questions.explanation: titanium

questions.explanation: Duralumin

questions.question: Choose the incorrect information about metals
questions.explanation: metals are brittle

questions.explanation: Mercury

questions.explanation: Copper

questions.question: Which metal is suitable to store acid in its vessel?
questions.explanation: Copper

questions.explanation: Both Silver and Gold

questions.explanation: No reaction takes place

questions.explanation: Na

questions.question:
Complete the following equation
Zn + CuSO4 {tex}\longrightarrow{/tex} ________ + Cu
questions.explanation: ZnSO4

questions.question: A non-metal having lusture is

questions.question: Which of the following elements is a metalloid?
questions.explanation: Antimony

questions.explanation: Diamond

questions.explanation: CO2

questions.explanation: Mercury

questions.question: Chlorine is used in

questions.explanation: exposed to moist air

questions.question: Which of the following metals is used in galvanisation of iron?
questions.explanation: Zinc

questions.question: Which of the following is not suggested to prevent metals from corrosion?
questions.explanation: Dipping metal in salt solution

questions.question: Which is the hardest matter?
questions.explanation: Diamond is regarded to be the hardest known material in the world. But by considering large compressive pressures under indenters, scientists have calculated that a material called wurtzite boron nitride (w-BN) has greater indentation strength than diamond.

questions.question: The ore of aluminium metal is-
questions.explanation: Bauxite ore is the world’s primary source of aluminum. The ore must first be chemically processed to produce alumina (aluminum oxide). Alumina is then smelted using an electrolysis process to produce pure aluminum metal.

questions.question: Which of the following metal burns quickly when comes in the contact of water and air?
questions.explanation: The heavier alkali metals (rubidium and cesium) will spontaneously ignite upon exposure to air at room temperature. Alkali metals react with water to produce heat, hydrogen gas, and the corresponding metal hydroxide. Elements such as Lithium; Potassium; Rubidium; Sodium, etc.

questions.question: Which of the following metal is known as the metal of future?
questions.explanation: Titanium is being increasingly used in our modern society. It is light, strong and corrosion-resistant. These properties allow it to be used in the aerospace industry, building industry, sports goods industry and as implants in a number of surgical procedures.

questions.question: Every heavy radioactive elements, converts finally into-
questions.explanation: All the radioactive elements are ultimately converted to lead.

questions.question: The purest form of iron is-
questions.explanation: The purest form of iron is Wrought iron.

questions.question: What is present in the nucleus of an atom-
questions.explanation: The nucleus is a collection of particles called protons, which are positively charged, and neutrons, which are electrically neutral. Protons and neutrons are in turn made up of particles called quarks. The chemical element of an atom is determined by the number of protons, or the atomic number, Z, of the nucleus.

questions.question: Which of the following is a temporary particle?
questions.explanation: The neutron is classified as a hadron, because it is a composite particle made of quarks. The neutron is also classified as a baryon, because it is composed of three valence quarks. The finite size of the neutron and its magnetic moment both indicate that the neutron is a composite, rather than elementary, particle.

questions.question: The penetrating power of proton is
questions.explanation: Beta particles are high energy electrons and very light as compared to the atom and tend to scatter and bounce a lot as compared to a proton. Protons are about 8,000 times heavier than electrons. So, protons have lesser penetration power than electron.

questions.question: The size of nucleus is-
questions.explanation: It is found that nuclear radii range from 1-10 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-15 m. This radius is much smaller than that of the atom, which is typically 10-10 m.

questions.question: What has been invented by Chadwick?
questions.explanation: Sir James Chadwick was a British physicist who was awarded the 1935 Nobel Prize in Physics for his discovery of the neutron in 1932.

questions.question: In flash bulbs, the magnesium wire is kept in the following atmosphere-
questions.explanation: Flash bulbs contain a foil of magnesium packed in the atmosphere of nitrogen as it provides the inert atmosphere.

questions.question: Calamine is-
questions.explanation: Calamine is an ore of zinc. It is zinc carbonate ZnCO3.

questions.question: Which pair of elements contain metal and non-metal both present in liquid state at room temperature?
questions.explanation: Bromine is the only non-metal to exist in liquid form at room temperature. Mercury is the metal to exist in liquid form at room temperature. It has a relatively high vapour pressure and the highest volatility of any metal vapourising to become a colourless, odourless gas. Hence, Bromine, Mercury is correct.

questions.question:
From the following correct statement is

  1. Every ore is a mineral
  2. Every mineral is an ore
  3. Minerals can't be an ore
  4. An ore can't be a mineral
questions.explanation: All ores are minerals while all minerals are not ores as some of them might have a large amount of unwanted substances such as sand, stones and earthly impurities.

questions.question: Bauxite is the ore of which metal?
questions.explanation: Bauxite ore is the world’s primary source of aluminum. The ore must first be chemically processed to produce alumina (aluminum oxide). Alumina is then smelted using an electrolysis process to produce pure aluminum metal. Bauxite is typically found in topsoil located in various tropical and subtropical regions.

questions.question: The glass used in making lab apparatus is?
questions.explanation: The glass used in making lab apparatus is: Pyrex glass {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} borosilicate (resistant)

questions.question: In which of the following matter gold dissolves?
questions.explanation: Aqua regia is a yellow-orange (sometimes red) fuming liquid, so named by alchemists because it can dissolve the noble metals gold and platinum, though not all metals.

questions.question: The hardest metal from the following is:
questions.explanation: Tungsten is one of the hardest metals you will find in nature. Also known as Wolfram, the rare chemical element exhibits a high density (19.25 g/cm3) as well as a high melting point (3422 °C/ 6192 °F). In its rare form, tungsten is hard to work with due to its brittleness which can be changed when turned pure.

questions.question: The main element to be used in manufacturing match box-
questions.explanation: Chemical formula of red phosphorus is P4, which is used in the match box for making the striking surface where matchsticks will strike to generate flame or smoke. -Glass powder is also used in the making of a striking surface of a match box with red phosphorus, but in a minor quantity.

questions.question: The permanent magnet is made up of-
questions.explanation: Permanent magnets are made from special alloys (ferromagnetic materials) such as iron, nickel and cobalt, several alloys of rare-earth metals and minerals such as lodestone.

questions.question: Which gas is filled in balloon?
questions.explanation: Helium gas is used to fill balloons. Helium (density = 0.178 kg/m3) is lighter than air (density = 1.27 kg/m3).

questions.question: The presence of which gives the green colour to crackers?
questions.explanation: Mineral elements provide the color in fireworks: Barium produces bright greens. Strontium yields deep reds.

questions.question: The lightest element is -
questions.explanation: Hydrogen is the lightest element and exhibits the simplest atomic structure. In addition, it is the most abundant element in the universe and can form bonds to nearly every element, such as s- and p-block elements.

questions.question: The reason that murcury does not stick to the rod even after pulling the rod out of the glass of mercury is-
questions.explanation: Cohesive force is the intermolecular attraction between like molecules, whereas adhesive force is the intermolecular force between unlike molecules. In the given case cohesive force between mercury molecules is more than adhesive force between mercury molecules and glass rod, hence mercury does not stick to the rod.

questions.question: Haematite is an ore of which metal?
questions.explanation: Hematite, also spelled haematite, heavy and relatively hard oxide mineral, ferric oxide (Fe2O3), that constitutes the most important iron ore because of its high iron content (70 percent) and its abundance.

questions.question: German silver is a mixture of-
questions.explanation: German silver is a mixture of copper, zinc and nickel. The constituents of copper is 60%, zinc is 20% and nickel is 20%

questions.question: Simple glass is a mixture of which of the following?
questions.explanation: Glass is the homogenous mixture of the silicates of various alkaline metals of non-crystalline and transparent or less transparent substances. Ordinary glass is manufactured by the composition of various substances like silica, bleaching powder, oxides of alkaline metals, calcium oxide (lime) etc.

questions.question: Which of the following gases is filled in balloon?
questions.explanation: Helium is a special gas called a Noble Gas, which means it doesn’t burn. When a match is held near a helium-filled balloon, the balloon pops.

questions.question: Which gas is filled in the tyre tubes of Aeroplane?
questions.explanation: Helium is used for inflating aeroplane tyres & filling balloons for metrological observations.

questions.question: The property of metals by which they can be beaten into thin sheets is called
questions.explanation: Malleability is the property of metals by which they can be beaten into thin sheets. Gold and silver are the most malleable and ductile metals.

questions.question: When a copper vessel is exposed to moist air for a long time, if acquires a dull green coating on its surface. The material of ths green coating is a mixture of
questions.explanation:
Copper vessel is exposed to moist air for a long time, it acquires a dull green coating on its surface the materials of this green coating is a mixture of copper carbonate and copper hydroxide.
2Cu(s) + H2O + CO2 + O2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\underset{Copper\;hydroxide}{Cu{(OH)}_2}{/tex} + {tex}\underset{Copper\;carbonate}{Cu{CO}_3(s)}{/tex}

questions.question: The chemical formula of cryolite is-
questions.explanation: The chemical formula of cryolite is Na3AlF6.

questions.question: The most abundant non-metal found in the Earth's crust is
questions.explanation: The most abundant non-metal found in the Earth's crust is oxygen. Hence, oxygen is correct.

questions.question: Which one is a non-metal which conducts electricity?
questions.explanation: Carbon is the only non-metal which conducts electricity. When carbon in the form of graphite one valence electron of each carbon atom remains free, thus making graphite a good conductor of electricity.

questions.question:
Choose the higher form of Carbon from the following:

  1. Graphite
  2. Diamond
  3. Buckminster fullerine
  4. Bauxite
questions.explanation: Higher forms of carbon are Graphite, Diamond and Buckminster fullerene.

questions.question:
Choose the coorect option related to carbon from the following:

  1. The melting point and boiling point of most carbon compounds are higher
  2. Mostly carbon compounds are bad conductor of electricity
  3. One higher form of carbon is a good conductor of electricity
  4. Compressed natural gas is a fuel in which there is no carbon
questions.explanation: The melting point and boiling point of most carbon compounds are higher, bad conductor of electricity and one higher form of compound is a good conductor of electricity.

subtopics.name: Chemical Properties of Metals and Non-metals

questions.question:
Choose those metals from the following which are found in their natural state:

  1. Copper
  2. Zinc
  3. Silver
  4. Gold
questions.explanation: A native metal is any metal that is found pure in its metallic form in nature. Metals that can be found as native deposits singly or in alloys include aluminium, antimony, arsenic, bismuth, cadmium, chromium, cobalt, indium, iron, manganese, molybdenum, nickel, niobium, rhenium, selenium, tantalum, tellurium, tin, titanium, tungsten, vanadium, and zinc, as well as the gold group (gold, copper, lead, aluminium, mercury, silver) and the platinum group (platinum, iridium, osmium, palladium, rhodium, ruthenium).

questions.question:
There are many methods for processing of metals. Out of the following which metals are processed by electrolytic decomposition method?

  1. Gold
  2. Copper
  3. Potassium
  4. Sodium
questions.explanation: The metals produced by various reduction processes described above are not very pure. They contain impurities, which must be removed to get pure metals. The most widely used method for refining impure metals is electrolytic refining. Many metals, such as copper and silver are refined electrolytically.

questions.question:
Important steps for obtaining of metals from their ores are given below:

  1. Processing
  2. Sanding and Grinding
  3. Enrichment
  4. Extraction
questions.explanation:
Important steps for obtaining of metals from their ores are

  1. Sanding and Grinding
  2. Extraction
  3. Enrichment
  4. Processing

questions.question:
Metals generally shows which of the following property?

  1. Elasticity
  2. Electric conductivity
  3. Lack of lustre
  4. Sounding nature
questions.explanation:
Metals generally shows the following properties:

  1. Elasticity
  2. Electric conductivity
  3. Sounding nature

questions.question:
The usage of Aluminium is into the making of food making utensils. Which of the following properties are responsible for it?

  1. Higher reactibility
  2. Higher boiling point
  3. Higher electric conductivity
  4. Higher heat conductivity
questions.explanation: Cooking utensils, such as pots, pans and menu trays, are often made from aluminium because it is lightweight and conducts heat well, making it energy-efficient for heating and cooling. These properties also make it a preferred material for packaging.

questions.question: Monozyte is the ore of-
questions.explanation: Monazite is an important ore for thorium, lanthanum, and cerium. It is often found in placer deposits. India, Madagascar, and South Africa have large deposits of monazite sands. The deposits in India are particularly rich in monazite.

questions.question: Which is easily soluble in water-
questions.explanation: Nitrogen gas does not react with water. It dissolves in water. Nitrogen (N2) soluble at 20°C and 1 bar atmospheric pressure is approximately 20 mg/L. Nitrogen solubility differs between compounds.

questions.question: Which of the following metal is found in free stage?
questions.explanation: Lead is found in free state.

questions.question: By which copper is purified?
questions.explanation: Copper is purified by electrolysis. Electricity is passed through solutions containing copper compounds, such as copper (II) sulfate. The anode (positive electrode) is made from contaminated copper and the cathode (negative electrode) is made from uncontaminated copper.

questions.question: Which of the following metal is a liquid?
questions.explanation: Mercury is the only metal which is in liquid state at room temperature.

questions.question: First of all who discovered radioactivity?
questions.explanation: It was Henri Becquerel who discovered the phenomenon, it was his doctoral student, Marie Curie, who named it as radioactivity. She would go on to do much more revolutionary work with radioactive materials including the discovery of additional radioactive elements: thorium, polonium, and radium.

questions.question: Which property of copper is used for making electric wires?
questions.explanation: Copper is a good conductor, it is used for making electric wires. Copper is a soft malleable and ductile metal with very high thermal and electrical conductivity.

questions.question: Which of the following metal is highly elastic?
questions.explanation: Silver is more tensile than copper, aluminum and gold. Therefore, silver is highly reactive metal among them.

questions.question: Brass is a mixture of-
questions.explanation: Brass is a mixture of copper and zinc, which has a harder finish than copper alone. We supply brass to customers for a variety of applications due to its durabil ty and looks. Brass is a solid material, making it strong, hard, and formable.

questions.question: Which of the following element is not present in stainless steel?
questions.explanation: Stainless steels are steels containing at least 10.5% chromium, less than 1.2% carbon and other alloying elements. Stainless steel’s corrosion resistance and mechanical properties can be further enhanced by adding other elements, such as nickel, molybdenum, titanium, niobium, manganese, etc.

questions.question: Iron is painted to _______
questions.explanation: iron is painted to save it from corrosion. after painting iron surface does not come in contact with moisture and air, which causes corrosion.

questions.question: Aqueous solution of which of the following oxides turns blue litmus red?
questions.explanation: Sulphur is a non-metal and it forms an acidic oxide, i.e. sulphur dioxide (SO2) whose aqueous solution which turns blue litmus red.

questions.question: Which one of the following is used for making crackers?
questions.explanation: Copper is used for making crackers.

questions.question: Metal M occurs in earth’s crust as its oxide M2O3 An alloy of this metal is used in making aircrafts. Metal M and the composition of alloy are respectively
questions.explanation: Aluminium, Al (95.5%) + Cu (3% ) + Mn (1%) + Mg (5%)

questions.question: Select the odd one out from the given alloys on the basis of the major constituent.
questions.explanation:
Solder does not contain copper which is the major constituent of all other given alloys.
Gun metal - Cu (90%) + Sn (10%)
Bell metal - Cu (78%) + Sn (22%)
German silver - Cu (60%) + Zn (20%) + Ni (20%)
Solder - Sn (50%) + Pb (50%)

questions.question:
Ryma took a substance (X) in a deflagrating spoon and burned it. She carefully collected the evolved vapours (Y) in a test tube and added a small amount of water to the test tube. Then she tested the solution obtained (Z) with litmus paper. The solution turned blue litmus red.
X, Y and Z could be respectively
questions.explanation:
Solution of the oxide of substance X is acidic in nature as it turns blue litmus red. Hence, element X must be a non-metal.
{tex}\underset{\mathrm X}{\mathrm{Sulphur}\;}+\;\mathrm{Oxygen}\;\rightarrow\underset{\mathrm Y}{\mathrm{Sulphur}\;\mathrm{Dioxide}}{/tex}
{tex}\mathrm{Sulphur}\;\mathrm{dioxide}\;+\;\mathrm{Water}\;\rightarrow\underset{\mathrm Z}{\mathrm{Sulphurous}\;\mathrm{acid}}{/tex}

questions.question:
Observe the given figure carefully.

The nature of suspension, the colour change in red litmus paper and the formula of rust are respectively.
questions.explanation: Basic, blue and Fe2O3. xH2O

questions.question:
Saumya set up the following apparatus to study the conditions under which iron rusts.

If rusting of iron nails occurs only in test tube I, then which of the following is correct?

  1. Test tube I has both air and water, hence rusting occurs.
  2. X is layer of oil which prevents water from evaporating.
  3. Y is anhydrous calcium chloride which prevents the reaction of iron with air.
  4. X makes the air moist to increase the process of rusting.
questions.explanation: Layer of oil prevents the contact of air with iron nails, anhydrous calcium chloride absorbs moisture to make the air completely dry.

questions.question:
Element X is added to two different salt solutions as shown in the figure.

If reaction occurs in beaker II only, then the order of reactivity for these elements is
questions.explanation: Element X is less reactive than sodium and more reactive than copper.

Substance M.pt. (oC) Electrical conductivity
    Solid form Molten form
P 1100 Good Good

R 2027 Poor Poor

questions.explanation: Metals generally have high melting points and conduct electricity both in molten and solid form.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is incorrect?

options.option: Gold and silver are the most malleable and ductile metals.
Most of the metals have high specific gravities.
Mercury and gallium are metals which exist in liquid state at room temperature.

questions.explanation: Sodium and potassium are soft metals and can easily be cut with a knife.

questions.question:
Observe the given figure carefully.

Select the correct statement(s) from the following.

  1. In beaker P, blue colour of solution changes to green colour.
  2. In beaker Q, no reaction takes place.
  3. In beaker R, if Y is aluminium then reaction will take place.
  4. In beaker R, if Y is tin then reaction will take place.
questions.explanation:
In beaker ‘Q’, reaction will take place as zinc being more reactive than iron displaces iron from aqueous solution of iron (II) ions.
In beaker ‘R', if ‘Y is tin then no reaction will take place as tin being less reactive than zinc, cannot displace zinc from aqueous solution of zinc (II) ions.

questions.question:
Savita had four metals : iron, zinc, X and Y. She arranged the following experimental set-ups and observed that displacement reaction took place in each case.

Metals X and Y are respectively
questions.explanation: If reaction took place in all the set-ups then, X is more reactive than Y. Hence, X and Y can respectively be Cu and Ag. As Zn is more reactive than X, hence X cannot be Al.

questions.question: Moist air is the combination of ________.
questions.explanation: H2O + CO2 + O2

questions.question:
What happens when calcium is treated with water?

  1. It does not react with water.
  2. It reacts violently with water.
  3. It reacts less violently with water.
  4. Bubbles of hydrogen gas form and stick to the surface of calcium.
questions.explanation:
(iii) It reacts less violently with water
(iv)Bubbles of hydrogen gas form and stick to the surface of calcium.

questions.question: ________ is used as antiseptic.
questions.explanation: Iodine

questions.question:
Ms. Rashmi, a science teacher set up the given apparatus to demonstrate displacement reactions where X and Y are two unknown metals.

The observations are summarised in the given table.

Beaker Observation
I Blue colour solution changes to green.
II No reaction takes place.
III No reaction takes place.
IV. Blue colour solution change to colourless.

X and Y could be ________ and ________ respectively.
questions.explanation:
From the given observations, it can be concluded that Fe is more reactive than X but less reactive than Y and Y is more reactive than X.
Hence, X and Y could be respectively Cu and Zn or Cu and Al.

questions.question: Which of the following elements produces basic oxide on heating?
questions.explanation: Magnesium is a metal hence, it will produce basic oxide on heating.

questions.question: The second most abundant metal in the Earth's crust is ________.
questions.explanation: iron

questions.question: Which of the following metals reacts vigorously with oxygen?
questions.explanation: Sodium reacts vigorously with oxygen and water hence, it is stored under kerosene.

questions.question: Which of the following materials cannot be used to make container for storing copper sulphate solution?
questions.explanation: Aluminium, zinc and iron all are more reactive than copper hence will displace copper from its solution.

questions.question:
Match the columns and select the correct option from the given codes.

Column I Column II
P. The most abundant element in universe (i) Oxygen
Q. Largest constituent of atmosphere (ii) Aluminium
R. Most abundant non-metal in earth's crust (iii) Nitrogen
S. Most abundant metal in earth's crust (iv) Hydrogen
  (v) Iron
questions.explanation:
The most abundant element in universe - Hydrogen
Largest constituent of atmosphere - Nitrogen
Most abundant non-metal in earth’s crust - Oxygen
Most abundant metal in earth’s crust - Aluminium

questions.question: Which of the following metals will not liberate hydrogen when kept in HCl solution?
questions.explanation: Ag, Cu and Hg will not liberate hydrogen when kept in HCl solution as these metals lie below hydrogen in the reactivity series.

questions.question: In displacement reactions, a more reactive metal displaces a less reactive metal from its solution. In which of the following mixtures displacement reaction will take place?
questions.explanation: Aluminium being more reactive than copper will displace copper.

questions.question: Silver objects become blackened and lose their shine with the passage of time due to

options.option: Reaction of silver with moisture present in air
Reaction of silver with carbon dioxide present in air.
Reaction of silver with hydrogen sulphide present in air
Reaction of silver with oxygen present in air

questions.explanation: Silver reacts with hydrogen sulphide present in air to form black coloured silver sulphide.

questions.question: While making the heating element in electrical equipments, which of the following properties is considered?
questions.explanation: The heating element in electrical equipments should have high melting point to withstand high temperature.

questions.question: Which of the following properties make mercury an ideal liquid for making thermometers?
questions.explanation: All of these

questions.question: Which of the following metals is used in car batteries?
questions.explanation: Lead

questions.question: Which of the following metals is not present in German silver?
questions.explanation: German silver contains copper, zinc and nickel.

questions.question:
W is a lustrous non-metal, X is a metal having low melting and boiling point, Y is a solid non-metal while Z is a metal less reactive than copper. 
W, X, Y and Z could be respectively
questions.explanation:
Iodine - Lustrous non-metal
Sodium - Metal having low melting and boiling point
Sulphur - Solid non-metal
Silver - Metal less reactive than copper

Q. Solder (ii) Al + Cu + Mn + Mg

questions.question: Which of the following represents the incorrect match of the alloy with its uses?
questions.explanation: Magnalium is used to make balance beams, light instruments. Gears and castings are made from gun metal.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements is/are correct?

  1. Among metals lead is the best conductor of heat.
  2. Metals are more abundant than non-metals.
  3. Bromine is a liquid non-metal.
  4. Sodium and potassium have lower densities
questions.explanation: Among metals, gold is the best conductor of heat.

questions.question: The metal present in the hormone insulin is:
questions.explanation: Ag

questions.question: A metal X does not liberate hydrogen from acids but reacts with oxygen to give a black coloured product. Metal X and the black coloured product could be respectively.
questions.explanation: Copper does not liberate hydrogen from acids but reacts with oxygen to form black coloured cupric oxide.

questions.question: X is a non-metal which is used in a purple colour solution considered as an antiseptic solution. Here X is:
questions.explanation: Iodine

questions.question:
Five beakers are arranged as shown in the figure.


Study the given set-up carefully and select the correct statements.

  1. Reaction will take place in beakers (ii) and (v).
  2. A colour change will take place in beakers (i) and (v).
  3. A white deposition will be formed on copper turnings in beaker (iv).
  4. A brown deposition will be formed on iron filings in beaker (i) and on zinc granules in beaker (v).
  5. Reaction will take place in all the beakers but colour change will be observed only in beakers (i) and (iii).
questions.explanation:
Beaker (i): Reaction will take place and colour of solution will change from blue to green.
{tex}\mathrm{Fe}{(\mathrm s)}\;+\;\underset{(\mathrm{Blue})}{{\mathrm{CuSO}}_4{(\mathrm{aq})}}\rightarrow\underset{(\mathrm{green})}{{\mathrm{FeSO}}_4(\mathrm{aq})}\;+\;\mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm s){/tex}
Beaker (ii): No reaction as Fe is less reactive than Zn
Beaker (iii): No reaction as Cu is less reactive than Fe
Beaker (iv): No reaction as Cu is less reactive than Mg
Beaker (v): Reaction will take place and colour of solution will change from blue to colourless.
{tex}\mathrm{Zn}{(\mathrm s)}\;+\;\underset{(\mathrm{Blue})}{{\mathrm{CuSO}}_4{(\mathrm{aq})}}\rightarrow\underset{(\mathrm{Colourless})}{{\mathrm{ZnSO}}_4(\mathrm{aq})}\;+\;\mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm s){/tex}

questions.question: Which of the following correctly represents decreasing order of the reactivity of the metals?
questions.explanation: Potassium, calcium, zinc, lead

questions.question:
Select the incorrect statement.

  1. Copper does not react with dilute hydrochloric acid but it reacts with dilute sulphuric acid.
  2. Non-metals react with acids to form hydrogen gas which burns with a pop sound.
  3. Sodium and potassium are brittle in nature.
  4. Mercury is a liquid non-metal
questions.explanation:
Copper does not react with dilute hydrochloric acid or dilute sulphuric acid but it reacts with concentrated sulphuric acid.
Non-metals do not react with acids to evolve hydrogen gas. Mercury is a liquid metal.

questions.question: The density of the metals is very high because they:
questions.explanation: Have closely packed atoms

questions.question: Which of the following pairs will give displacement reactions?
questions.explanation: Silver nitrate solution and copper metal.

questions.question: Potassium carbonate is also known as
questions.explanation: Pearl ash

questions.question: The metal preferred for making jewellery is ________ .

questions.question: The property of the metal that is used to make musical instruments is:
questions.explanation: Sonority

questions.question: Metals react with acids to produce a gas known as ________ .
questions.explanation: hydrogen

questions.question: This non-metal is a good conductor of electricity.
questions.explanation: Graphite

questions.question: The correct order of reactivity between iron, zinc and copper is ________ .
questions.explanation: Zn > Fe > Cu

questions.question: The metal that is liquid at room temperature is ________.
questions.explanation: Mercury is liquid at room temperature.
