questions.explanation: Zn + Cu2+ {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} Zn2+ + Cu

questions.question: Which one of the following can be beaten into sheets?
questions.explanation: Aluminium being a soft malleable metal can be beaten into sheets.

questions.question: Which of the following metals will not react completely with dilute sulfuric acid to form a salt and hydrogen gas?
questions.explanation: Lead

questions.question: Which one of these is 'natural oxide'?
questions.explanation: Carbon monoxide

questions.question: Which of the following statement is incorrect about aluminium?

options.option: Aluminium is a chemical element in the Boron group.
Aluminum is extracted from its ore bauxite.

questions.explanation: It is soft and brittle.

questions.question: Which of these is most malleable?
questions.explanation: Aluminium

questions.question: Tooth cavities are filled with alloys of:
questions.explanation: Silver

questions.question: The non-metal which is not affected by caustic soda is:
questions.explanation: Phosphorus

questions.question: The metal which is stored in kerosene is ________.
questions.explanation: sodium

questions.question: Which of the following is the electronic configuration of metal with a high melting point?
Which of the following oxides cannot be reduced by both hydrogen gas and carbon into its metals?
questions.explanation: CaO

questions.question: Aluminium is used to make cooking utensils even though it is very high in the reactivity series. Which of the following statements justifies its use as a cooking utensil?

options.option: Aluminium loses three valence electrons readily during a chemical reaction
Aluminium has a much lower density compared to iron
Aluminium can react readily with oxygen in the air to form aluminium oxide
Aluminium has a relatively high melting point compared to other metals

questions.explanation: Aluminium can react readily with oxygen in the air to form aluminium oxide

questions.question: When non-metals react with water then
questions.explanation: Hydrogen gas is formed

questions.question: Which of the following statement explains why 99.99% copper is used in manufacturing high quality electrical wires for audio equipment?

options.option: 99.99% copper is almost free of impurities.
Copper which is of a high purity can conduct electric current better.
Copper is a good conductor of electricity.
99.99% copper is less ductile and cannot be stretched easily.

questions.explanation: Copper which is of a high purity can conduct electric current better.

questions.question: Which one of the following statements is CORRECT?
questions.explanation: Generally metals are ductile

questions.question: Which one of the following is used for decorating sweets?
questions.explanation: Silver foils are made from real silver, but they are edible and are used for decorating sweets.

questions.question:
Stainless steel is used to make utensils rather than iron metal. Which of the following properties of stainless steel make it better than that of iron?

  1. Stainless steel is a good conductor and resistant to chemicals.
  2. Stainless steel is corrosion free and resistant to chemicals.
  3. Stainless steel is durable than iron metal.
  4. Both II and III
questions.explanation: Both II and III

questions.question:
Which of the following materials will be sonorous in nature?

  1. Copper vessel
  2. Steel spoon
  3. Wooden chair
  4. Glass vase
  5. Iron nail
questions.explanation: Copper vessel, steel spoon and iron nail being metallic in nature will be sonorous in nature.

questions.question: Which of the following materials will break into pieces on hammering?
questions.explanation: Non-metals being brittle break into pieces on hammering.

questions.question: Aluminium and its alloys are used to build aircrafts, but not iron. Why?

options.option: Aluminium alloys are used in aircrafts because they are light weight and corrosion free.

questions.explanation: Aluminium alloys are used in aircrafts because they are light weight and corrosion free.

questions.question: Which of the following represents an incorrect match?
questions.explanation: Mercury is a liquid metal.

questions.question: Which of the following reactions will not take place easily?
questions.explanation: Mg(s)+Pb(NO3)2(aq) {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} Pb(s) + Mg(NO3)2(aq)

questions.question:
Study the given figures

What are X and Y respectively?
questions.explanation: Cu and Fe

questions.question: Coke is obtained from:
questions.explanation: Coke is obtained by heating coal to a high temperature in the absence of oxygen or in limited supply of oxygen. It contains less impurities and high carbon content.

questions.question: Gaseous mixture containing more volatile components of petroleum is called:
questions.explanation: Natural gas is gaseous mixture containing more volatile components of petroleum. It is used in vehicle as compressed natural gas (CNG).

questions.question: Fossil fuels are:
questions.explanation: Dead remains of plants and animals are called fossils. Fossils used as source of energy are called fossil fuels. Fossil fuels are formed naturally.

questions.question: A thick black liquid formed by heating coal in the absence of air:
questions.explanation: Coal tar is a thick black liquid formed by heating coal in the absence of air. Coal tar is used to construct road and water proofing.

questions.question: Identify the gas that is generally produced when coal is burnt in the presence of a lot of air.
questions.explanation: Coal is mainly composed of carbon. When coal is burnt in the presence of air, carbon reacts with oxygen gas that is present in air (atmosphere) and mainly produces carbon dioxide gas (CO2).

questions.question: Such resources which can be continually replenished are called:
questions.explanation: Natural resources are either exhaustible or inexhaustible. Those resources which can be continually replenished are called inexhaustible resources.

questions.question: What is absent in coal?
questions.explanation: Coal mainly consists of carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen besides sulphur. Phosphorus is absent in coal.

questions.question: Coal type that contains lowest amount of carbon is:
questions.explanation: Carbon content in lignite coal is less than 50%, so, lignite coal is the inferior quality of coal. Anthracite coal is best quality of coal.

questions.question: Conversion of dead trees and other plants into coal is called:
questions.explanation: Conversion of dead trees and other plants into coal inside the earth crust due to high temperature and pressure is called carbonisation.

questions.question: Coal and petroleum can be categorised as:
questions.explanation: Coal and petroleum are nonliving natural resources. They are present in earth crust in limited quantity. Coal and petroleum is non replenishable natural resources.

questions.question: Type of coal that can serve as good heating fuel for domestic purpose is:
questions.explanation: Anthracite coal is used as fuel in domestic purpose for cooking food. It contain about 70% carbon content and provide sufficient heat for cooking.

questions.question: Coal was formed during:
questions.explanation: Coal was formed during carboniferous period. Lots of plants are present on earth that get buried inside the earth crust due to volcanic eruption.

questions.question: Coke is used in the manufacture of ______.
questions.explanation: Coke is a pure form or allotrope of carbon. It is used in the manufacture of steel, as a fuel and as a reducing agent in the extraction of various metals. Steel is an alloy of carbon and iron. Presence of carbon in the steel makes it harder and more durable than pure iron.

questions.question: The slow process of conversion of dead vegetation into coal is called _____.
questions.explanation: Plants contain mainly carbon, which gets converted into coal. Hence, the slow process of conversion of dead vegetation into coal is called carbonisation.

questions.question: That category of coal which has high heat value but very little moisture is:
questions.explanation: Bituminous coal has high calorific value to produce more heat per gram. It contains very less moisture content.

questions.question: The type of coal that can be used to produce coke is:
questions.explanation: Coke is produced by destructive distillation of bituminous coal. Bituminous coal contain very less amount of moisture in it.

questions.question: Coke is used to manufacture:
questions.explanation: Producer gas is mixture of carbon monoxide, hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and nitrogen. Producer gas is manufactured from coke.

questions.question: Petroleum is a mixture of:
questions.explanation: Petroleum is a mixture of different hydrocarbons starting from methane (CH4) to higher hydrocarbons. Compounds of carbon and hydrogen are called as hydrocarbon.

questions.question: Coal is regarded as backbone of our national economy because:

options.option: supports industries by providing raw material in its different forms
supports our agriculture and crop production as manure
is exported on large scale since our country has maximum reserves
indirectly supports many industries and thermal power plants

questions.explanation: Coal is regarded as backbone of our national economy because coal indirectly supports many industries and thermal power plants to obtain energy.

questions.question: Lead added to petrol engines works as:
questions.explanation: Lead is added to petrol engines as lead works as anti knock agent. It is used to engine knocking and increase the fuels octane rating.

questions.question: _______ becomes coal when it is been buried long and deep enough.
questions.explanation: peat is an accumulation of partially decayed vegetation or organic matter that is unique to natural areas. Peat becomes coal when it is been buried long and deep enough.

questions.question: Coke is used for:
questions.explanation: Coke is used for extraction of metals as it acts as excellent reducing agent as well as heat giving substance. It is obtained from coal.

questions.question: LPG finds its use as:
questions.explanation: LPG is also used as refrigerant in cooling devices such as air conditioner and refrigerator due to low boiling point.

questions.question: Nowadays, addition of lead tetraethyl to petrol engines is banned because:

options.option: lead works as stimulating agent for engine

questions.explanation: Lead was used in petrol as anti knocking agent. Nowadays, addition of lead tetraethyl to petrol engines is banned due to toxic nature of lead.

questions.question: Excessive use of coal & petroleum may lead to:
questions.explanation: Excessive use of coal and petroleum causes green house effect, acid rain, and energy crisis in future. Burning of fossil fuels release carbon dioxide gas. Carbon dioxide gas causes green house effect.

questions.question: That gas produced by burning of fossil fuels which is itself highly poisonous:
questions.explanation: Incomplete burning of fossil fuels produces carbon monoxide gas in place of carbon dioxide. Carbon monoxide gas is a poisonous gas that bind with haemoglobin to reduce oxygen carrying capacity.

questions.question: Naphthalene balls used to repel insects and moths are obtained from:
questions.explanation: Naphthalene balls used to repel insects and moths from cloths are obtained from coal tar. Coal tar is obtained as by product of fractional distillation of petroleum.

questions.question: Process of heating coal in the absence of oxygen is called:
questions.explanation: The process of heating coal in the absence of oxygen is called destructive distillation of coal. Coke and coal gas are obtained from destructive distillation of coal.

questions.question: Fraction of petroleum used for surface roads is:
questions.explanation: Bitumen is used for construction of surface roads. Bitumen is obtained as residual product of the fractional distillation of petroleum.

questions.question: Nowadays, a petroleum product is used for medalling roads in place of previously used material, they are:
questions.explanation: Bitumen and coal tar is used for medalling roads in place of previously used materials. These materials make roads more water resistant and durable.

questions.question: Natural gas contains only traces of:
questions.explanation: The main constituent of natural gas is methane, only traces of ethane and propane is present in natural gas. Natural gas is used as excellent fuel for vehicles.

questions.question: Natural gas is formed by decomposition of vegetation by:
questions.explanation: Natural gas is formed by decomposition of vegetation by anaerobic bacteria in absence of oxygen. During this anaerobic decomposition methane gas is produced.

questions.question: To save petrol/diesel while driving we must:

options.option: drive for a longer period of time

questions.explanation: To save petrol/diesel while driving, we must drive at a moderate speed and switch off the engine at red lights. Petroleum products are limited in quantity and takes millions of years for formation.

questions.question: Various materials which are obtained from nature are called natural resources. Which of the following is not a natural resource?
questions.explanation: Plastic is made in laboratory aur factories whereas other are naturally available.

questions.question: Air is a natural resource and cannot be exhausted by human activities. It is known as an inexhaustible natural resource. Which of the following is another inexhaustible natural resource?
questions.explanation: Sunlight, air and water are inexhaustible natural resources that mean which cannot be exhausted by human activities.

questions.question: Which of the following is a pair of exhaustible natural resources?
questions.explanation: Wildlife and minerals are exhaustible natural resources because these resources are present in a limited quantity in nature and can be exhausted by human activities.

questions.question: Coal is processed in industries to get some useful products. Which of the following is not obtained from coal?
questions.explanation: CNG (Compressed Natural Gas) is not obtained from coal.

questions.question: Exhaustible natural resources are:
questions.explanation: Exhaustible natural resources are present in a limited quantity in nature and can be exhausted by human activities.

questions.question: Fossil fuels are obtained from:
questions.explanation: Fossil fuels are obtained from the remains of living organisms (plants and animals) buried under the earth millions of years ago.

questions.question: Coal is formed from the remains of:
questions.explanation: Coal is formed by the decomposition of large land plants and trees buried under the earth about 300 million years ago.

questions.question: Which substance is formed by the carbonisation of dead vegetation?
questions.explanation: Coal is formed by the carbonisation of dead vegetation.

questions.question: Naphthalene balls are obtained from coal tar and are used as:
questions.explanation: Naphthalene balls are used to repel moths and other insects (in stored clothes, etc).

questions.question: Which of the following is not a constituent of petroleum?
questions.explanation: Coke is not a constituent of petroleum. Coke is prepared by heating coal in the absence of air.

questions.question: Petroleum was formed from organisms:
questions.explanation: Petroleum was formed by the decomposition of the remains of tiny plants and animals buried under the sea millions of years ago.

questions.question: Which one of the following forms of coal has the lowest percentage of carbon?
questions.explanation: Peat

questions.question: Which of the following is the common variety of coal?
questions.explanation: Bituminous

questions.question: The purest form of coal is
questions.explanation: anthracite

questions.question: The allotrope of carbon used to extract metals is
questions.explanation: coke

questions.question: Petrochemical substances are obtained from
questions.explanation: natural gas and petroleum

questions.question: Petrol is a mixture of ________ such as hexane, heptane and octane.
questions.explanation: coal gas

questions.question: Paraffin oil or kerosene is obtained from petroleum by
questions.explanation: fractional distillation

questions.question: The constituent of natural gas is
questions.explanation: methane

questions.question: Which of the following burns easily with a smokeless flame and thus does not cause air pollution?
questions.explanation: CNG

questions.question: Mortar is a mixture of-
questions.explanation: Mortar is a mixture of sand, a binder such as cement or lime, and water and is applied as a paste which then sets hard.

questions.question: Three constituents of petroleum are
questions.explanation: Three constituents of petroleum are bitumen, lubricating oil and paraffin wax. Petroleum (Crude oil) obtained from rocks inside the earth is actually a mixture of many substances it is refined in refinery to obtain separately different components.

questions.question: Dead plants slowly get converted to coal under ________
questions.explanation: Dead plants slowly get converted to coal under high pressure and high temperature. This type of fuel is known as fossil fuel.

questions.question: The purest form of coal is-
questions.explanation: High grade (HG) and ultra high grade anthracite are the highest grades of anthracite coal. They are the purest forms of coal, having the highest degree of coalification, the highest carbon count and energy content and the fewest impurities (moisture, ash and volatiles).

questions.question: The black thick liquid with unpleasant smell is known as
questions.explanation: Coal-tar is black thick liquid with unpleasant smell. It is by-product of the production of coke and coal gas from coal. It is distillation yield compounds, as benzene, anthracene and phenol.

questions.question: When coal burns in air ________ gas is mainly produced.
questions.explanation:
When coal burns in presence of air CO2 (carbon dioxide) gas is mainly produced.
{tex}\underset{(Coal)}C+\underset{(Coal)}{O_2}\rightarrow\underset{(Carbon\;dioxide)}{{\mathrm{CO}}_2+Heat}{/tex}

questions.question: Which one is not a natural resource?
questions.explanation: It is a mixture of metals and made by humans.

questions.question: Which one is an exhaustible natural resource?
questions.explanation: The amount of these forests in nature is limited. They can be exhausted by human activities.

questions.question: Which one is not an inexhaustible natural resource?
questions.explanation: The reservoir of natural gas is limited on earth and it can be used up completely.

questions.question: Why do we need to conserve petrol?
questions.explanation: Petrol is a non-renewable resource and it will exhaust after some time if we use it in an inappropriate way.

questions.question: Natural gas is a fossil fuel because

options.option: it is formed from dead remains of organisms.
it is found in the earth's crust.

questions.explanation: Natural gas is formed from fossils(dead remains) of organisms.

questions.question: The fuel which was used in railway engines to produce steam to run the engine is:
questions.explanation: Earlier, Coal was used in railway engines to produce steam to run the engine.

questions.question: Carbonation is

questions.question: Gas necessary for combustion is:
questions.explanation: Combustion takes place only in presence of oxygen. Oxygen supports the combustion of substances in which heat is evolved.

questions.question: Substance that burns in oxygen to produce heat and light is called:
questions.explanation: Those substances that burn in the presence of oxygen to produce heat and light is called a combustible substance. Fossil fuels, wood, paper etc are examples of a combustible substances.

questions.question: Substance that does not burn in air is:
questions.explanation: The substance that does not burn in air is called non combustible substance. Wood, kerosene, and diesel is example of combustible substance but sand is non combustible.

questions.question: Spontaneous combustion is a:

options.option: both fast process and used in internal combustion engines

questions.explanation: A combustion in which a material suddenly bursts into flames, without the application of any apparent cause called spontaneous combustion. Spontaneous combustion is a fast process and complete in a few seconds.

questions.question: Cutting trees at large scale leads to:
questions.explanation: Cutting of trees at large scale leads to deforestation. Deforestation may cause desertification and loss of living natural resources.

questions.question: Amount of heat energy produced on complete combustion of 1kg of fuel is called:
questions.explanation: The amount of heat energy released on complete combustion of 1 kg of fuel is called calorific value of that fuel. Higher the calorific value better the fuel is considered.

questions.question: Substance which vaporises during burning forms
questions.explanation: Substance which get vaporise during combustion or burning forms flame. Each substance forms characteristic flame having different colour and heat region.

questions.question: Combustion is also called:
questions.explanation: Combustion is also called as burning. Burning of substance requires oxygen and combustible substance and ignition temperature.

questions.question: Lowest temperature at which a substrate catches fire is called its:
questions.explanation: The lowest temperature at which substance catch fire is called ignition temperature. The fuel with moderate temperature is considered better fuel for domestic use.

questions.question: A combustible substance is :-
questions.explanation: Those substances that start burning at ignition temperature in presence of oxygen is called combustible substance. Water, glass, and sand are non combustible substance while wax is a combustible substance.

questions.question: Water works as a fire extinguisher by removing :-
questions.explanation: Water works as a fire extinguisher because it removes oxygen and lower down the temperature below the ignition temperature.

questions.question: We can extinguish fire by:-

options.option: both cutting the supply of oxygen & lowering temperature below ignition temperature

questions.explanation: Fire can be extinguished by cutting the supply of oxygen and removing combustible substances from surrounding or lowering the temperature below the ignition temperature.

questions.question: Non combustible substances do not burn in:
questions.explanation: Non combustible substance does not burn in air and oxygen . Carbon is generally absent in non-combustible substance or present in very less quantity.

questions.question: The gas which supports combustion of coal is:
questions.explanation: Oxygen gas supports the combustion of combustible substances including coal. Combustion of coal release carbon dioxide gas as well as solid residue.

questions.question: Combustion of fuels is done to obtain:
questions.explanation: Fuel is burnt to obtain energy. Energy is used to drive a number of processes like running car, producing electricity, cooking food etc.

questions.question: Temperature of water inside paper cup held above candle flame:
questions.explanation: The temperature of water inside paper cup held above candle flame rise due to heat transfer. But paper does not attain ignition temperature until all the water get vaporised.

questions.question: Some combustion reactions occur suddenly with evolution of large amount of gas. Such a combustion is:
questions.explanation: Some combustion reactions occur suddenly with evolution of large amount of gas and energy in form of heat and light. Such sudden combustion is called explosion.

questions.question: In the following cases fire can be extinguished by removing combustible substance except:
questions.explanation: Fire can be extinguished by removing combustible substance as in case of fire in wood log, a paper on fire and a firecracker but removing of combustible substance is not possible in case of fire in a building.

questions.question: Some substances do not burn with a flame such as:
questions.explanation: Substances like wax, LPG, and kerosene burns with flame but charcoal do not produce flame. Flame is formed only when combustible substance first change into vapour state.

questions.question: If Bunsen burner is closed which colour flame is produced
questions.explanation: If hole in Bunsen’s burner’s is closed incomplete combustion of fuel take place due to presence of insufficient oxygen that produce yellow flame with lots of smoke.

questions.question: A flickering flame produces smoke due to:
questions.explanation: A flickering flame produces smoke due to partial or incomplete combustion of fuel due to insufficient oxygen in immediate surroundings.

questions.question: When a fuel undergoes complete combustion it produces:
questions.explanation: When a fuel undergoes complete combustion, it produces non luminous flame without any smoke. The colour of flame is blue in place of yellow as in case of incomplete combustion.

questions.question: When a piece of rock from space enters earth, it burns because of
questions.explanation: When a piece of rock from space enters earth’s atmosphere it start burning due to friction between rock and atmospheric gases. These rocks are called meteors.

questions.question: Sunil wet a paper cup with water and then heated it over the flame:
questions.explanation: Due to presence of water in paper cup ignition temperature of wet paper cup gets increased and paper will not burn when placed over a flame until water gets evaporated.

questions.question: From the fire triangle(essential elements of fire) water removes:
questions.explanation: There are three essential requirements for producing fire. These are fuel, air (to supply oxygen), and heat (to raise the temperature of the fuel beyond the ignition temperature). Fire can be controlled by removing one or more of these requirements. Heat, oxygen and combustible substance forms the fire triangle that causes fire. Water removes the heat and oxygen when poured over burning substance that extinguishes fire.

questions.question: A person after fire casualty is wrapped with blanket to:

options.option: to increase the ignition temperature of body
to cut the supply of combustible substance
to cut the supply of carbon dioxide

questions.explanation: A person after fire casualty is wrapped with blanket to cut oxygen supply. Cutting of oxygen supply extinguish the fire due to absence of support of combustion.

questions.question: Yellow flame leaves behind black residue which is also called:
questions.explanation: Yellow flame is formed due to incomplete combustion of fuel. Yellow flame leaves behind black residue which is also called as soot.

questions.question:
Water is never used to extinguish an electrical fire since :
i) It is bad conductor of electricity.
ii) It is good conductor of electricity.

options.option: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement ii) is wrong but i) is correct

questions.explanation: Water is never used to extinguish an electric fire because water is good conductor of electricity .

questions.question: Wax vapour burn completely in the:
questions.explanation: Wax vapour burns completely in the non-luminous zone of flame. non-luminous zone is blue in colour. Luminous zone is yellow in colour with incomplete combustion of fuel.

questions.question: A fuel will be considered good if:

options.option: ignition temperature is lower than room temperature
ignition temperature is greater than room temperature
very costly and produce lot of smoke
ignites easily to produce lot of carbon monoxide

questions.explanation: A fuel will be considered good if the ignition temperature is greater than room temperature. It should have a high calorific value and easy to transport.

questions.question: Which of the following can catch fire on their own?
questions.explanation: Sodium metal catches fire on its own. The ignition temperature of sodium is less than room temperature and catches fire when come in contact of oxygen.

questions.question: The least hot part of the flame is:
questions.explanation: The least hot part of the flame is black. This part is situated at the top of flame containing unburnt particles.

questions.question: A non inflammable substance out of the following is:
questions.explanation: A non inflammable substance out of the following is carbon dioxide. Carbon dioxide gas prevents the fire. LPG, CNG, and alcohol are inflammable substances and catch fire easily.

questions.question: During combustion temperature rises considerably because burning:
questions.explanation: During combustion temperature rises considerably because burning produces heat and light due to the breaking of carbon bonds in them.

questions.question: The type of combustion which can cause lot of air pollution is:
questions.explanation: Incomplete combustion of fuel causes lot of air pollution. During incomplete combustion, carbon monoxide gas is produced which is very harmfull for health of humans.

questions.question: Combustion can be defined as a:-
questions.explanation: The process of burning of any substance in presence of oxygen is called combustion. Combustion is a chemical change as in combustion new substance is formed that cannot be reversed.

questions.question: Another name given to ignition temperature is:
questions.explanation: Ignition temperature is the temperature at which fuel starts burning. Ignition temperature is also called a kindling temperature.

questions.question: Fuels are present in:
questions.explanation: Fuels are present in all three states. In solid state, coal and wood, In liquid state kerosene and petrol and in gases state, CNG and LPG

questions.question: The contact number of a fire station or fire control room is:
questions.explanation: The contact number of a fire station or fire control room is 101 throughout India.

questions.question: In villages, many people still use coal or wood to cook food. For easy combustion of such fuels :
questions.explanation: Coal or wood is still used in a number of villages for cooking food. For easy combustion of coal and wood air is blown around to increase the supply of oxygen for easy combustion.

questions.question: If a combustible substance is present along with fire:

options.option: substance will not catch fire below its ignition point.
substance will not catch fire above its ignition point.

questions.explanation: If a combustible substance is present along with fire, the substance will not catch fire below its ignition point. Ignition temperature is necessary for combustion of fuel.

questions.question: Gas that has very high calorific value is:
questions.explanation: Hydrogen gas has the highest calorific value. Hydrogen gas is used as fuels in rockets.

questions.question: Acid rain is caused by oxides of
questions.explanation: Acid rain is caused by a chemical reaction that begins when compounds like sulfur dioxide and nitrogen oxides are released into the air. These substances can rise very high into the atmosphere, where they mix and react with water, oxygen, and other chemicals to form more acidic pollutants, known as acid rain.

questions.question: Magnesium ribbon on burning in air produces?
questions.explanation:
Magnesium ribbon on burning in air produces magnesium oxide, heat and light, Magnesium ribbon is a very reactive metal.
Magnesium + Oxygen (From air) {tex}\xrightarrow{{Combustion}}{/tex} Magnesium oxide + Heat + Light

questions.question: Which of the following is not a combustible substance?
questions.explanation: Those substances which do not burn are called a non-combustible substance. Some of the non-combustible substances are stone, glass, cement, soil, sand, iron nails, etc.

questions.question: If a person’s clothes catch fire, the best way to extinguish the fire is to:

options.option: Cover the person with a polythene sheet
Cover the person with a woolen blanket

questions.explanation: Cover the person with a woollen blanket so that the supply of air to the burning clothes is cut off and hence, the burning (or fire) stops.

questions.question: Which among the following is considered as the cleanest fuel?
questions.explanation: Hydrogen gas is considered as cleanest fuel. Unlike carbon based fuels, hydrogen produces no harmful by-products on combustion. Only energy and clean water are produced.

questions.question: Shyam was cooking potato curry on a chulha. To his surprise he observed that the copper vessel was getting blackened from outside. It may be due to:
questions.explanation: Due to incomplete combustion of fuel, copper vessel was getting blackened from outside.

questions.question: A substance which reacts with oxygen giving heat is called a combustible substance. Which one of the following is a combustible substance?
questions.explanation: Wood is a combustible substance. It is a solid fuel and produces smoke during combustion.

questions.question: In villages, people use wood as fuel because:

options.option: It is considered to be an ideal fuel.
Of its easy availability and low cost.

questions.explanation: In villages, people use wood as a fuel because of its easy availability and low cost.

questions.question: Which one of the following has the highest calorific value?
questions.explanation: LPG has the highest calorific value, i.e. 55000 kJ/kg. It means that if 1 kg of LPG is burnt completely, then it will produce 55000 kilojoules of heat energy.

questions.question: When the supply of oxygen is not sufficient, the fuels burn incompletely producing mainly
questions.explanation: yellow flame

questions.question: Which zone of the flame has the highest temperature?
questions.explanation: Outermost zone

questions.question: Which one of the following has the lowest calorific value?
questions.explanation: Coal gas

questions.question: The fuel, among the following, that has low calorific value and causes high pollution is
questions.explanation: cow-dung cake

questions.question: A gaseous fuel ________.

options.option: gives a high ash content on burning

questions.explanation: can be burnt at any moment

questions.question: What is RDX?
questions.explanation: RDX is an organic compound with the formula (0 2N2CH2)3. It is a white solid without smell or taste, widely used as an explosive. Chemically, it is classified as a nitroamine alongside HMX, which is a more energetic explosive than TNT. It was used widely in World War II and remains common in military applications.

questions.question: Which of the following gas is used in a Gas-lighter?
questions.explanation: Butane, a highly flammable, colorless, easily liquefied gas used in gas-type lighters, cigarette lighters and butane torches.

questions.question: Which of the following is known as the Marsh gas?
questions.explanation: Marsh gas, which is also called methane, is produced by the anaerobic bacterial decomposition of vegetable matter and the rumen of herbivorous animals underwater.

questions.question: What is obtained by the fractional distillation of bones?
questions.explanation: Animal charcoal is produced from bones, horn, blood, etc. Bone-black, or bone-charcoal, is obtained by the destructive distillation of bones in iron retorts, the distillate constituting bone- oil.

questions.question: A burning stick when brought into a jar, filled with nitrogen, then
questions.explanation: A burning stick when brought into a jar filled with nitrogen, then it extinguishes.

questions.question: It is mainly found in Bio-Gas-
questions.explanation: Biogas consists mainly of methane and carbon dioxide. It can also include small amounts of hydrogen sulphide, siloxanes and some moisture. The relative quantities of these vary depending on the type of waste involved in the production of the resulting biogas.

questions.question: Power alcohol is-

options.option: Ethyl alcohol in the form of fuel

questions.explanation: Ethanol fuel is ethyl alcohol, the same type of alcohol found in alcoholic beverages, used as fuel. It is most often used as a motor fuel, mainly as a biofuel additive for gasoline.

questions.question: Which of the following is the property of good fuel?
questions.explanation: An ideal fuel should have the following properties: high calorific value, moderate ignition temperature, low moisture content, low non-combustible matter, moderate velocity of combustion, products of combustion not harmful, low cost, easy to transport.

questions.question: If you burn 1 kg each of the following fuels in an efficient system, you will get maximum amount of heat when you burn
questions.explanation: Hydrogen get maximum amount of heat released during its combustion, calorific value is a measure of a fuel's energy density, and is expressed in energy (Joules) per specified amount (e.g., Kilograms).

questions.question: Which one is not a fossil fuel?
questions.explanation: Wood is not a fossil fuel where as coal, petroleum, natural gas are fossil fuels, all were formed due to decomposition of dead animals and plants million of years ago.

questions.question: For the conversion of dense forests, get buried under the soil into fossil (one) essential conditions required are
questions.explanation: For the conversion of dense forests (which get buried under the soil) into fossil (coal) essential conditions required are high pressure and high temperature. The formation of coal occurs over millions of years via a process known as carbonation. In this process, dead vegetation is converted into carbon-rich coal under very high temperature and pressure.

questions.question: Which fuel has highest calorific value?
questions.explanation:
Hydrogen fuel has highest calorific value

Fuel Calorific Value of fuel
Methane 50,000
CNG 50,000
LPG 55,000
Hydrogen 150,000

Hence, hydrogen is correct.

questions.question: Which of the following gases has the blue flame?
questions.explanation: In the presence of oxygen (O2) including atmospheric concentrations carbon monoxide (CO) burns with a blue flame producing carbon dioxide (CO2).

questions.question: The main component of natural gas is:
questions.explanation: The largest component of natural gas is methane, a compound with one carbon atom and four hydrogen atoms (CH4). Natural gas also contains smaller amounts of natural gas liquids (NGL, which are also hydrocarbon gas liquids), and nonhydrocarbon gases, such as carbon dioxide and water vapour.

questions.question: Which of the gases is found in bio-gas?
questions.explanation: Biogas is composed mostly of methane (CH4), the same compound in natural gas, and carbon dioxide (CO2). The methane content of raw (untreated) biogas may vary from 40%-60%, with CO2 making up most of the remainder along with small amounts of water vapor and other gases.

questions.question:
Choose the correct statement from the following:

  1. CNG is an ideal fuel.
  2. CNG on burning creates oxides of Nitrogen and sulphur in less quantity.
  3. The calorie value of CNG is more than hydrogen.
  4. CNG is comparatively a clean fuel.
questions.explanation: CNG on burning creates oxides of Nitrogen and sulphur in less quantity, the calorie value of CNG is more than hydrogen and it is comparatively a clean fuel.

questions.question: If we completely burn the same amount of the following fuels, the maximum amount of heat will be obtained on burning
questions.explanation:
Fuels Calorific value (kJ/kg)
LPG 55,000
CNG 50,000
Petrol 45,000
Diesel 45,000

questions.question: The main components of Bio-gas are-
questions.explanation: Biogas is an emerging renewable eiergy source which is obtained through degradation of orgaric matter by bacteria under anaerobic conditions. Raw biogas contains about 55-65% methane (CH4), 30-45% carbon dioxide CO2), traces of hydrogen sulphide (H2S), and fractions of water vapour.

questions.question:
Which of the forms of energy mentioned below have lastly originated from solar energy?

  1. Bio gas
  2. C. N. G. and L. P. G.
  3. Wind energy
  4. Geothermal Energy
questions.explanation: Geothermal energy, wind energy, and biomass are all ultimately derived from solar energy.

questions.question: Water boils at low temperature on mountain because-

options.option: There is less air pressure on mountain.

questions.explanation: At elevated altitudes, any cooking that involves boiling or steaming generally requires compensation for lower temperatures because the boiling point of water is lower at higher altitudes due to the decreased atmospheric pressure.

questions.question:
Manan, a class 8 student took a glass tube with a pair of tongs and introduced its one end in the dark zone of the candle flame as shown in the figure.

Which of the following is correct regarding this activity?

options.option: Carbon dioxide is released in the innermost dark zone which bursts into flames when burning match stick is brought near it.
The wax vapours set down the glass tube and burst into flame when burning match stick is brought near it.
The burning match stick burns the candle wick which is made of highly inflammable substance.
The wax vapours rise up the glass tube and burst into flame when burning match stick is brought near it.

questions.explanation: The wax vapours rise up the glass tube and burst into flame when burning match stick is brought near it.

questions.question: Complete combustion of most of the fuels produces (i) ________ which is responsible for (ii) ________. (i) and (ii) are respectively
questions.explanation: Carbon dioxide and global warming

questions.question:
Select the incorrect matches regarding the type of fire and fire extinguishers which can be used for that particular type of fire.

I. Flammable gases Carbon dioxide, dry chemicals
II. Cooking oil and fat Water
III. Electrical equipments Water, chemical foam
IV. Combustible metal Carbon dioxide
questions.explanation: II, III and IV only

questions.question:
Match the following columns for the flame of a candle.

Column I
(Part)		 Column II
(Zone)		 Column III
(Colour)
P. Hottest part (i) Innermost zone of unburnt wax vapours (x) Blue
Q. Moderately hot part (ii) Middle zone of partial combustion (y) Black

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

  1. LPG is non-flammable substance, that is why it is used for cooking at our homes.
  2. On burning, camphor produces flame while charcoal does not produce flame.
  3. Sulphur is a non-combustible non-metal.
  4. Combustion of phosphorus is an example of rapid combustion.
questions.explanation: LPG is an inflammable substance. Sulphur is combustible and combustion of phosphorus is an example of spontaneous combustion.

questions.question:
Read the given statements and select the correct option.
Statement 1: Kerosene oil and wood do not catch fire on their own at room temperature.
Statement 2: A combustible material cannot catch fire as long as its temperature is lower than its ignition temperature.

options.option: Both statements 1 and 2 are true but statement 2 is not the correct explanation of statement 1.
Statement 1 is true and statement 2 is false.
Both statements 1 and 2 are true and statement 2 is the correct explanation of statement 1.

questions.explanation: Ignition temperature is the minimum temperature at which a substance starts burning.

questions.question:
Select the incorrect statements.

  1. All combustible substances do not produce flame.
  2. A blue flame indicates incomplete combustion.
  3. A yellow flame deposits black soot on cooking utensils.
  4. Kerosene is a better fuel than petrol.
  5. Kerosene lamp gives off light due to incomplete combustion of kerosene.
questions.explanation: A blue flame indicates complete combustion. Petrol is a better fuel than kerosene.

questions.question: Which of the following represents the characteristics of a good fuel?

options.option: Low calorific value and low ignition temperature
High calorific value and high ignition temperature
High calorific value and low ignition temperature
Low calorific value and high ignition temperature

questions.explanation: High calorific value and low ignition temperature

questions.question:
Which of the following substances will produce flame on burning?

  1. Candle
  2. Magnesium ribbon
  3. Charcoal
  4. Petrol
  5. Camphor
  6. Paper
questions.explanation: Candle, magnesium ribbon, petrol, camphor and paper produce flame on burning.

questions.question: Flames are formed when the substance:
questions.explanation: All of these

questions.question:
Calorific values of four fuels P, Q, R, S and T is in the order, T > R > Q > S > P.
P, Q, R, S and T could be respectively

options.option: Petrol, hydrogen, LPG, cow dung cakes and wood
Petrol, LPG, hydrogen, wood and cow dung cakes
Cow dung cakes, kerosene, LPG, wood and hydrogen
LPG, petrol, wood, cow dung cakes and hydrogen

questions.explanation:
The order of calorific values is:
Hydrogen > LPG > Kerosene > Wood > Cow dung cakes

questions.question: The heat of combustion of a fuel:

options.option: Is equal to the heat of formation

questions.explanation: Is always negative

questions.question:
Observe the given experimental set-ups carefully. In figure (I), a thin long copper wire is introduced into the non-luminous zone of a candle flame; in figure (II), a clear glass slide is introduced into the luminous zone while in figure (III), one end of a glass tube is introduced into the dark zone of a candle flame.

Which of the following is incorrect regarding these experiments?

options.option: Blackish ring will be formed on glass plate in figure II because of unburnt carbon particles in the middle zone of candle flame.
A flame is formed at the end of glass tube in figure III due to unburnt wax vapours in the innermost zone of candle flame.
Copper wire in figure I will become red hot because outermost zone is the hottest part of flame.

questions.explanation: Outermost zone is the hottest part of flame, unburnt carbon particles are present in the middle zone of candle flame and unburnt wax vapours are present in the innermost zone of candle flame.

questions.question: Main constituents of benzol are
questions.explanation: Benzene, toluene and xylene

questions.question: Higher efficiency in the combustion of solid fuel cannot be achieved by

options.option: Adopting efficient - fuel firing technique and equipment.
Keeping the the gas exhaust temperature very high.

questions.explanation: Keeping the the gas exhaust temperature very high.

questions.question:
Select the correct statements.

  1. Dry powder of chemicals like sodium bicarbonate or potassium bicarbonate when released near the fire, gives off CO2.
  2. Water should be used to extinguish fire caused by cooking oil.
  3. When CO2 stored in cylinders is sprayed on fire, it cuts off the supply of oxygen as well as brings down the temperature of the fuel.
  4. Sand can be used to extinguish fire caused by electrical equipments.
questions.explanation: Water should not be used to extinguish fire caused by cooking oil.

questions.question: During combustion of gaseous fuels, deficiency of air:
questions.explanation: Lengthens the flame.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements is incorrect?

  1. A luminous zone is formed due to complete combustion of fuel.
  2. A substance that does not vaporise during burning gives flame.
  3. A good fuel should have high ignition temperature.
  4. All of these
questions.explanation:
A luminous zone is formed due to incomplete combustion of fuel.
A substance which vaporises during burning gives flame. A good fuel should have low ignition temperature.

questions.question: Presence of ________ in a dry gaseous fuel does not contribute to its calorific value.
questions.explanation: Oxygen

questions.question: Low temperature carbonization:

options.option: Produces higher quantity of gas than high temperature carbonisation.
Is mainly for producing smokeless domestic coke.
Is meant for the production of 'metallurgical coke.

questions.explanation: Is mainly for producing smokeless domestic coke.

questions.question: Which of the following is a false statement?

options.option: Complete combustion of a fuel gives carbon monoxide gas.
Ideal fuel is cheap and easily available.
Inflammable substances have very low ignition temperature.

questions.explanation: Combustion produces heat and light.

questions.question: Which is not the example of combustion?

options.option: Spontaneous reaction of coal dust in coal mines.

questions.explanation: Spontaneous reaction of coal dust in coal mines.

options.option: Ratio of fixed carbon to volatile matter increases.
High rank coals have higher amount of volatile matter.

questions.explanation: Ratio of fixed carbon to volatile matter increases.

questions.explanation: Friability and oxygen content

questions.question: Combustion of pulverised coal as compared to that of lump coal:

options.option: Provides a lower rate of heat release.
Can be done with less excess air.

questions.explanation: Can be done with less excess air.

questions.question: The head of a safety match contains
questions.explanation: Antimony trisulphide and potassium chlorate

Q. Exposure of sodium to open air (ii) Slow combustion

questions.explanation:
Rusting of iron - Slow combustion
Exposure of sodium to open air - Spontaneous combustion
Burning of paper - Rapid combustion
Burning of crackers - Explosion

questions.question: Liquid and gaseous fuels have more advantages over solid fuels. Which of the following is not an advantage?

options.option: Liquids and gaseous fuels have higher ignition temperature than the solid fuels.
Liquids and gaseous fuels are easier tostore since solid fuels occupy lot of space
Liquids and gaseous fuels burn completely, leaving no residue.
Calorific value of liquid and gaseous fuels is higher than the solid fuels.

questions.explanation: Liquids and gaseous fuels have higher ignition temperature than the solid fuels.

questions.question: Riya's mother was cooking potato curry on a Chulha. She observed that the copper vessel was getting blackened from outside. It may be due to ________.
questions.explanation: Improper combustion of fuel

questions.question:
Aditi, accidently puts an inverted glass over a lighted candle.
She observed that the candle blows off in few seconds. Why did this happen?

options.option: The candle keeps burning as long as air is present in the glass and then it goes off.
The ignition temperature of the candle becomes low due to glass cover hence it goes off.
The candle keeps burning as long as the complete wax melts away and then goes off.
O2 is produced when candle burns which helps the candle to burn initially.

questions.explanation: The candle keeps burning as long as air is present in the glass and then it goes off.

questions.question: Global warming leads to a rise in sea level which causes floods in the coastal areas. One of the main reasons of global warming is ________.

options.option: Combustion of fuels releases unburned carbon particles which causes decrease in temperature.
Combustion of fuels releases CO which decreases the temperature.
Combustion of fuels releases SO2 which increases the temperature.
Combustion of fuels releases CO2 which causes increase in temperature.

questions.explanation: Combustion of fuels releases CO2 which causes increase in temperature.

questions.question: Fuel gases containing hydrocarbons (i.e. coke oven gas) are not preheated before burning, mainly because

options.option: The hydrocarbons crack thereby choking and fouling the heat transfer surface by carbon soot
There are chances of explosion during preheating

questions.explanation: The hydrocarbons crack thereby choking and fouling the heat transfer surface by carbon soot

questions.question: Rapid combustion is

options.option: when gas burns, it produces heat and light.
Neither when gas burns, it produces heat and light and when material suddenly bursts into flames.

questions.explanation: when gas burns, it produces heat and light.

questions.question:

CNG 50000
LPG 55000
Coal 25000-33000


Arrange these fuels in correct order of their efficiency.
questions.explanation: LPG > CNG > Diesel > Coal

questions.question: When a frying pan containing cooking oil is kept for long on a burning stove then cooking oil catches fire, because:

options.option: Its temperature is lower than the critical temperature.
Its temperature has reached to its ignition temperature.
Its temperature is higher than the critical temperature.
Its temperature is lower than its ignition temperature

questions.explanation: Its temperature has reached to its ignition temperature.

questions.explanation: A is non-luminous (hottest) zone, B is luminous zone (moderately hot) and C is dark zone (least hot).

questions.question:
Select the correct statements.

  1. Both soda acid type and foam type extinguisher cannot be used in fighting electrical fires.
  2. Liquid carbon dioxide can be used for both electrical and oil fires.
  3. Gaseous fuels are better than solid or liquid fuels.
  4. Burning of charcoal produces flame with four distinct zones.
questions.explanation: Both soda acid type and foam type extinguisher can be used in fighting electrical fires. Charcoal does not produce flame on burning.

questions.question:

In which case the candle will go off first?
questions.explanation: The candle will go off first in apparatus (c) as the supply of air is totally cut off.

questions.question: Burning of coal dust in a coal mine is an example of
questions.explanation: Burning of coal dust in a coal mine is an example of explosion.

questions.explanation: All of these

questions.question:
While demonstrating an activity, the science teacher set up the experiment as shown in the figure.

Which of the following represent the correct observations?

  1. Paper cup burns in Case 1.
  2. Temperature of water rises in Case 2.
  3. Paper cup absorbs heat from water in Case 2.
  4. Paper cup loses heat to water in Case 2.
  5. Ignition temperature of paper cup is not reached in Case 2.
questions.explanation: I, II, IV and V only

questions.question: The formation of soot particles during burning of a candle is due to

options.option: Unburnt carbon particles left behind because of insufficient oxygen for burning

questions.explanation: Unburnt carbon particles left behind because of insufficient oxygen for burning.

questions.question: If 10 kg of a fuel X produces 1,60,000 J of heat on complete combustion then, the calorific value of X is
questions.explanation:
10 kg of X produces heat = 1,60,000 J
1 kg of X will produce heat = {tex}\frac {1,60,000}{10}\text J{/tex} = 1,6000 J = 16 kJ
Hence, calorific value of fuel X = 16 kJ/kg

questions.question: Deforestation may lead to:
questions.explanation: Deforestation means clearing of forest and using that land for other purposes. Forest act as a natural absorber of rainwater, deforestation leads to a decrease in the water holding capacity of the soil or in other words we can say that infiltration of water is reduced so, there are floods.

questions.question: Large scale cutting of trees is named:
questions.explanation: Large scale cutting of trees is called deforestation. Deforestation leads to global warming, drought, and soil erosion.

questions.question: The existence of a variety of animals, plants, and microbes in a particular area is:
questions.explanation: Biodiversity is the presence of a variety of animals, plants, and microbes in a particular area. Biodiversity depends upon the climatic condition and availability of water.

questions.question: Biotic components of an ecosystem does not include:
questions.explanation: The living components of an ecosystem are called biotic components of ecosystem. Ecosystem includes microbes, plants, and animals. Minerals are nonliving component of an ecosystem so, minerals are not included in biotic components.

questions.question: A sustainable functional unit of biosphere is called:
questions.explanation: Ecosystem is the basic functional unit of biosphere. It is self contained ecological system  which consists of distinct biotic and abiotic component

questions.question: The largest among various protected areas for conservation of plants and animals:
questions.explanation: Wildlife sanctuaries, national parks, biosphere reserves, etc. are protected areas for conservation of plants and animals present in the area. Biosphere reserve is the largest among various protected areas for plants and animals. Biosphere reserves covers large areas of protected land for conservation of wildlife animal resource and traditional life of the tribals living in the area.

questions.question: A place in environment where an organism lives is
questions.explanation: Habitat

questions.question: Full form of IUCN is:

options.option: International union of conservation of national parks and nature
International union of conservation of national parks and natural resources
International unity of conservation of nature and natural resources
International union of conservation of nature and natural resources

questions.explanation: IUCN stands for International union of conservation of nature and natural resources. It is an international organization working in the field of nature conservation and sustainable use of natural resource.

questions.question: The regions of the surface and atmosphere of earth occupied by living organism is called:
questions.explanation: Biosphere have all the necessary things which are required to living organisms. It includes the lithosphere, hydrosphere, and atmosphere.

questions.question: Threatened species does not include:
questions.explanation: Those species that are less in number and going to extinct are called threatened species. Extinct species are those which are already lost from the earth for example dinosaurs.

questions.question: Those species which existed on earth once but are  not found anywhere now:
questions.explanation: Species that no longer exist anywhere on earth are called extinct species for example dinosaurs and dodo bird.

questions.question: Species confined to a specific geographical area of land is called:
questions.explanation: Those species of plants and animals which are found exclusively in a particular area of land are called endemic species. Endemic species are found in only a particular area due to climatic factors.

questions.question: Panchmarhi biosphere reserve has a national park named:
questions.explanation: Satpura national park is situated in Panchmarhi biosphere reserves in Madhya Pradesh. It is built to protect tigers and other animals.

questions.question: Animals showing seasonal movements are  known as:
questions.explanation: Some animals show seasonal movements due to unfavourable climatic changes. These animals are called migratory animals.

questions.question: To prevent loss of biodiversity we:
questions.explanation: To prevent loss of biodiversity we must regulate climate changes. Changes in temperature, rainfall, and other climatic factors influence the survival of organisms.

questions.question: Endemic flora of Pachmarhi biosphere reserve includes:
questions.explanation: Endemic flora of Pachmarhi biosphere reserve includes both sal and wild mango. Endemic species are present in particular locality only.

questions.question: Even protected forests are unsafe due to our:
questions.explanation: Even protected forests are unsafe due to our greed. Human being exploits the natural resources for getting economic value without considering about future generation.

questions.question: One major cause of extinction of organisms can be:
questions.explanation: Disturbances in habitat of organisms for urbanisation and industrialisation causes extinction of organisms. Construction of dam is also a major cause of habitat loss.

questions.question: Extinction of plants and animals will cause?
questions.explanation: Every organism is a part of one or more food chain. Extinction of plants and animals will cause damage to food chain .

questions.question: We need to protect forests to conserve:
questions.explanation:
We need to conserve forest because it-

  1. Releases Oxygen,
  2. Checks air pollution,
  3. Control moisture and prevents water pollution,
  4. Prevents soil erosion and increases soil fertility,
  5. Provides shelter to birds and nest.

questions.question: If we cut a few trees we must:
questions.explanation: Cutting of trees creates an ecological imbalance in nature and harm air, water, and land. so we should plant more trees if we cut a few trees

questions.explanation: Teak is one of the best quality of wood used for furniture due to durability and strength. Finest teak of India is obtained from forests of Satpura.

questions.question: We need to protect environment for:
questions.explanation:
We must protect environment because:
Global warming is a serious environmental issue. The continuous emission of carbon dioxide by thermal power generating units is the leading cause for the surge in greenhouse gases.
High-level of environmental pollution is detrimental for human and animal health.
Forests are fast depleting. It may cause soil erosion and drought situation.
Excessive use of pesticides and contamination of soil have negatively affected soil output. These fruits and vegetables are not very healthy for human health.

questions.question: Species is a group of population capable of:
questions.explanation: Species is a group of living organisms consisting of similar individuals capable of exchanging genes or interbreeding.

questions.question: We conserve biodiversity by:
questions.explanation: We conserve biodiversity by biosphere reserve, botanical garden, and sanctuaries. These are protected areas for the conservation of plants and animals present in that area.

questions.question: Some popular activities that are dependent upon biodiversity are:
questions.explanation: Gardening, aquarium, and herbarium are some popular activities that are dependent upon biodiversity of plants and animals.

questions.question: One of the most important causes of biodiversity is:
questions.explanation: Different climatic zone are found in different parts of world. Different species live in different climatic zone. This variation in climatic zone causes biodiversity.

questions.question: Most of the biodiversity hotspots are:
questions.explanation: Forest is considered as a biodiversity hotspot because of enormous number of species living in it. There are many varieties of birds, animals, insects, and plants inhabiting the forest.

questions.question: Which of these is a well known migratory species?
questions.explanation: Migratory species are species that move from one habitat to another during different times of the year, as they cannot live in the same environment all year round due to seasonal limitations in factors such as food, sunlight, and temperature. Albatross is a well known migratory species.

questions.question: A habitat change may:

options.option: increase the number of a given species
decrease the number of a given species

questions.explanation: A habitat is an ecological or environmental area that is inhabited by a particular species. changes in habitats brought on by alterations in farming practices, tourism, pollution, ....etc decreases the number of a given species.

questions.question: Removal of fertile top soil will cause:
questions.explanation: Removal of fertile top soil is called soil erosion. In absence of fertile soil, no plants grows and area slowly changes into desert and the process is called desertification.

questions.question: Maintaining the organism in nature, refers to:
questions.explanation: Preservation of organisms is the process of maintaining the organism in nature. Preservation may be in situ or ex situ depending upon species to be conserved.

questions.question: The existence of endemic species is under threat due to:
questions.explanation: The existence of endemic species is under threat due to increasing human population, habitat destruction, and introduction of exotic species into a foreign ecosystem.

questions.question: Project tiger was launched to:

options.option: protect and preserve tigers of the world
protect tigers of India and neighbouring countries

questions.explanation: Project tiger was launched in 1973 in India. The main objective of Project Tiger is to ensure a viable population of tiger in India for scientific, economic, aesthetic, cultural, and ecological values.

questions.question: When we kill a frog or lizard ruthlessly we:

options.option: threaten the survival of neighbouring species only
create an imbalance in the abiotic environment

questions.explanation: Frog eat grasshopper and lizard eat insects they play important role in food chain. killing frog and lizards ruthlessly will increase the number of grasshopper and insects. Grashoppers and insects may cause damage to crop in farms so killing lizards or insects ruthlessly means we are harming ourselves.

questions.question: If you have saved at least one sheet of paper per day you can save:
questions.explanation: Paper is made from plants. If we save paper regularly, many tree can be saved. Trees are lifeline for survival of all animals directly or indirectly.

questions.question: The permanent solution to the problem of deforestation can be:
questions.explanation: Reforestation is the permanent solution to the problem of deforestation. Planting tree on deforested land is called reforestation. A permanent solution to deforestation would, therefore, be proper planning on part of humans to understand the importance of trees. Simply planting trees is not a solution to this problem. We need to find that where major deforestation has taken place and practice more afforestation in that region. Also, vigorous cutting of trees for the purpose of development should be avoided. Thus the best solution to deforestation is to not allow them to be cut.

questions.question: Which of the following is multipurpose protected area that may also contain other protected areas in it:
questions.explanation: Biosphere reserve is multipurpose protected area that may contain protected areas in it. Biosphere reserves help ensure the environmental, economic, and social sustainability of the region, by encouraging wise use of natural and human resources. They provide practical ways to resolve land use conflicts and to protect biological diversity.

questions.question: Preservationist applies the principle of:
questions.explanation: Preservation is considered better than conservation. Preservationist applies the principle of non interference in natural system (without interference by human beings).

questions.question: One of the cause of species extinction is:
questions.explanation: Species extinction occurs due to more carbon dioxide gas in the atmosphere. More carbon dioxide leads to the greenhouse effect and global warming. Many species of plants and animals cannot tolerate the rising temperature due to globle warming and become extinct.

questions.explanation: Endemic species are those species of plants and animals which are only found exclusively in a specific habitat. For example-Kangaroos are only found in Australia and Asiatic lions also known as the Indian lions are only found in Gir National Park of Gujarat in India.

questions.question: The leaves are green due to ________.
questions.explanation: The leaves are green due to the presence of chlorophyll. It is a green colour pigment found in the thylakoid membranes of the chloroplast in the leaves. It absorbs deep blue and red light and reflects green colour. It absorbs the light energy from the sun, which is important for plants to make their food through photosynthesis.

questions.explanation: Wildlife sanctuaries are meant for the conservation of biodiversity in their natural habitat. In these places, animals and plants are protected in their natural; habitat and activities like hunting, poaching, felling trees are strictly prohibited.

D. Oliver Ridley Turtle iv. Gahirmatha Sanctuary in Orissa

options.option: a - ii, b - iv, c - i, d - iii
a - iv, b - iii, c - ii, d - i
a - iv, b - iii, c - i, d - ii
a - i, b- ii, c - iii, d - iv

questions.explanation: a - i, b- ii, c - iii, d - iv

questions.question:

  2. Wild Ass
  3. Dinosaur

questions.question:
Which one of the following groups consists exclusively of endangered animals?

  1. Lions, Wild Buffalo, Barasingha, Wild Ass
  2. Barasingha, Goat, Camel, Langoor
  3. Lion, Wild Ass, Rhinoceros, Tiger
  4. Jackal, Wild buffalo, Squirrel, Sparrow

questions.question: The species which has become extinct in the past is ________ .

questions.explanation: all of these

questions.question: Forests
questions.explanation: all of these

questions.question: Removal of top fertile layer of soil by wind or water is called
questions.explanation: erosion

questions.question: More soil erosion occurs where there is
questions.explanation: high rainfall

questions.question: Soil conservation is

options.option: conversion of sterile soil into fertile soil
protection of soil against loss of its fertility

questions.explanation: protection of soil against loss of its fertility

questions.question: Deforestation is the major cause of

questions.question: Frequent floods in plains of Northern India is due to

questions.question: What are the causes of extinction of wildlife?
questions.explanation: All of these

questions.question: Plants and animals are best protected in
questions.explanation: National parks

questions.question: The main object of Man and Biosphere programme is to

questions.explanation: all of these

questions.question: Which of the following is killed for oil and blubber?

questions.question: Which of the following is a wildlife sanctuary in Rajasthan?

questions.question: Which of the following is a bird sanctuary?

questions.question: Which of the following categories are include in Red Data List?

questions.question:
Which of the following statement is incorrect?

  2. Biosphere reserve are multipurpose protected areas.
  3. Sanctuaries are special protected areas for protection of wild animals.
  4. None of these.
questions.explanation: Statement (d) is incorrect.

questions.question: Which of the following is the first site to come under Project Tiger?
questions.explanation: Jim Corbett National Park, Uttaranchal

questions.question: 5th June is
questions.explanation: World environment day

questions.question: Chipko movement is connected with

options.option: Protection of environment including habitat and wildlife.

questions.explanation: Protection of environment including habitat and wildlife.

questions.question: Which of the following is/are the steps to be taken to conserve biodiversity?

options.option: Hunting of animals which is banned should be strictly enforced.
Protected areas like National Park, Wildlife sanctuaries and biosphere reserve should be established.

questions.explanation: All of these

questions.question: Which term is used for the species that are found only in a particular area?
questions.explanation: Endemic

questions.question: Why should we recycle paper?

options.option: Production of paper requires lot of wood pulp.

questions.explanation: Production of paper requires lot of wood pulp.

questions.question: Which of the following is a timber-producing tree?
questions.explanation: Rosewood

questions.question: Which of the following animals are declining in number due to habitat loss?
questions.explanation: Indian rhinoceros

questions.question: On which day does the United Nation celebrates International Day of Forest every year?
questions.explanation: 21st March

questions.question: How many nations have signed the Convention on Biological Diversity (CBD)?
Where is The Lalbagh Botanical Garden situated?
questions.explanation: Bengaluru

questions.question: Which of the following live in the mountaineous region?
questions.explanation: The giant panda

questions.question: Which part of India re termed as natural miracles?
questions.explanation: Western Ghats

questions.question: How can a forest be protected?
questions.explanation: Conservation Programmes

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a part of conservation programmes?
questions.explanation: Culturing of silkworms and honeybees

questions.question: Biodiversity of a place constitutes
questions.explanation: all living organisms

questions.question: Deforestation affects the
questions.explanation: biotic and abiotic factors

questions.question: Reforestation means
questions.explanation: planting trees and other plants

questions.question: Rhinos are poached for their
questions.explanation: horns

questions.question: Project Tiger is a conservation project to protect
questions.explanation: Royal Bengal tigers

questions.question: Plant diversity can be preserved through the use of
questions.explanation: seed banks

questions.question: A plant cell lacks:
questions.explanation: Haemoglobin is present in RBC’s to carry oxygen. Mitochondria, plasma membrane is present in both plant and animal cells.

questions.question: Number of cells found in unicellular organism is:
questions.explanation: Those organisms that are made up of only one cell are called unicellular organism. Amoeba, paramecium, bacteria, etc are unicellular organisms.

questions.question: All living organisms are made up of:
questions.explanation: Cell is the structural and functional unit of all living organisms. Unicellular made up of only one cell and multicellular are made up of more than one cells.

questions.question: Cells are capable of performing:
questions.explanation: Cells are capable of performing respiration, synthesis, and digestion of food to obtain energy. Cells is the functional and structural unit of living organisms.

questions.question: Robert Hooke observed under microscope:
questions.explanation: Robert Hooke observed dead cells of thin slice of cork. He observed small compartments and called it cell.

questions.question: Hen's egg is a:
questions.explanation: Hen’s egg is a single cell. Most of the cells are not visible with naked eyes. Microscope is needed to observe cells but Ostrich eggs and Hen’s eggs are also single cell.

questions.question: In unicellular organism all the functions are performed by the:
questions.explanation: In unicellular organism all the functions are performed by the single cell. Amoeba, paramecium, euglena etc are unicellular organism.

questions.question: Cell responsible to receive and transfer message in human body parts is:
questions.explanation: Nerve cells are responsible to receive and transfer message in human body parts. Nerve cells together form the nervous system of body.

questions.question: Cell membrane is also called:
questions.explanation: Cell membrane is also called as plasmalemma or plasma membrane. It forms the outer covering of the cell made up of protein and fat. It is semi-permeable in nature.

questions.question: Unit of inheritance in living organisms is:
questions.explanation: The transfer of traits from one generation to next generation is called inheritance. Inheritance is carried out by gene located in nucleus of the cell.

questions.question: Living substance of a cell is collectively known as:
questions.explanation: The living substance of a cell is collectively known as protoplasm. Protoplasm is fluid present between nucleus and plasma membrane of the cell. It contain all the cell organelles.

questions.question: The thread-like structures present in the nucleus are _______
questions.explanation: Chromosomes are thin thread like structure present inside nucleus of the cell. Chromosome consists of DNA and protein. Genes are located on chromosome.

questions.question: The fluid present between cell membrane and nucleus is:
questions.explanation: The fluid present between cell membrane and nucleus is called cytoplasm. Cytoplasm is thick fluid medium in which different cell organelles floats.

questions.question: In multicellular organisms, the work is:
questions.explanation: In multicellular organisms, the work is divided. Division of labour is essential for proper functioning of multicellular organism.

questions.question: Hydrolytic enzymes help in breaking down various materials so they are present in:
questions.explanation: Lysosomes contain hydrolytic enzymes that help in breaking down various materials. Lysosome is called the suicide bag of the cell.

questions.question: The cell organelle found to be present in both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells is:
questions.explanation: Ribosome is called protein factory of the cell. Ribosomes are present in both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells.

questions.question: Cell grows in size by:
questions.explanation: Cell grows in size by digesting food materials and using them to form different cell organelles including cell membrane.

questions.question: Which of the following group of cells need regular replacement in our body?
questions.explanation: Blood cells need regular replacement in our body. Blood cells undergoes replacement in our body after fixed interval of time.

questions.question: The structure of a living organism is determined by:

options.option: property of membrane that bounds cells in its body

questions.explanation: The structure of a living organism is determined by organisation of cells in its body. Cell organise in particular way to form tissue and organ of the living organism.

questions.question: Energy production in the cell is the responsibility of:
questions.explanation: Mitochondria is called the powerhouse of the cell as energy production in the cell is the responsibility of mitochondria. In mitochondria, energy is produced in form of ATP.

questions.question: Cell wall of bacteria contains:
questions.explanation: Cell of bacteria consists of peptidoglycan. This kind of glucose forms rigid structure around the bacterial body.

questions.question: Name an Organelle which serves as a primary packaging area for molecules that will be distributed throughout the cell?
questions.explanation: Plastids

questions.question: The structure that is/are porous?
questions.explanation: Both nuclear membrane and plasma membrane are porous and allow the entry and exit of substances in and out of the cell.

questions.question: In a human being life starts with:
questions.explanation: In human being life starts with fertilised egg. Mitotic division in zygote leads to formation of many cells that organise in particular way to form embryo.

questions.question: Scientists use dyes to study:
questions.explanation: To study the details of cells scientist use dyes. Dyes impart colours to the cells to visualise the different organelles properly.

questions.question: Amit observed various types of cells. He reported largest vacuoles in a:
questions.explanation: In plant cell single large vacuole is present in the cytoplasm. In animal cell many small vacuoles are present. So, largest vacuole is present in plant cell. Virus is acellular and lack vacuole.

questions.question: Shreya tried to observe sections of cells under microscope but transparent sections were not visible so she viewed after:
questions.explanation: Size of the cell is related to size of the animal body. Multicellular organism contains many cells arranged to form organs. Different cells are specialised to perform particular function in the body of organisms but not the size.

questions.question: In a large animal like elephant life begins with :
