questions.explanation: The entire living organisms either small or large as elephant begin their life with single celled zygote. Zygote formed by fusion of male and female gametes.

questions.question: Extra chromosomal DNA is found in prokaryotic cells is called:
questions.explanation: Extra chromosomal DNA is found in prokaryotic cells is called as plasmid. It contains circular DNA. It forms an important tool in biotechnology.

questions.question: Studying minute details of cell became possible due to:
questions.explanation: Electron microscope magnify the size of the cell more than thousands times. It becomes possible to study the minute details cells due to electron microscope.

questions.question: Cells that cannot reproduce is/are:
questions.explanation: Red blood corpuscles and nerve cells are not able to reproduce. RBC’s are replaced in blood periodically and nerve cell increase in length but not in number.

questions.question: Amisha observed a slide and saw a pair of guard cells in it. She is observing slide of:
questions.explanation: Guard cells are part of stomata through which exchange of gases and transpiration takes place in leaves of plant. The slide was of course plant leaf.

questions.question: Ribosomes are not found in:
questions.explanation: Ribosomes are present in all type of cell that helps in protein synthesis. It is present in amoeba, blue green algae as well as white blood cells. Tobacco mosaic virus (TMV) is acellular structure that lack ribosome.

questions.question: The smallest possible cell is:
questions.explanation: Pleuro pneumonia like organism (PPLO) is considered as smallest possible cell having fluid like body. It causes disease in plants.

questions.question: Cell organelle better known as protein factory of the cell are:
questions.explanation: Ribosomes are known as protein factory of the cell because ribosome provides site for protein synthesis using mRNA and t-RNA.

questions.question: Plants lack a skeletal framework in their body but this function is compensated by:
questions.explanation: Skeletal framework is absent in plant body but rigidness in cell body is due to presence of cell wall which is made up of cellulose.

questions.question: The peel of a potato contains:

options.option: cell wall and cell membranes of those cells

questions.explanation: The peel of potato contains cells. Potato tuber is underground stem. The outer layer of plant cell is cell wall made up of cellulose.

questions.question: If paramecium:: cilia, then euglena::?
questions.explanation: Paramecium contains small finger like projections called cilia all over the body that help them in movement. Euglena contains long hair like projection on body called flagella.

questions.question: If study of microbes is microbiology then study of cell is:
questions.explanation: The study of microbes is called microbiology and branch of biology in which we study about cell is called as cytology.

questions.question: If cell membrane:: skin then Nucleus::?
questions.explanation: Cell membrane controls the entry and exit of substances in and out of the cell as skin protects the body. Nucleus is called brain of the cell as it controls the functioning cell.

questions.question: Shubham was surprised to see the plastids providing colour to leaves. He discovered its name to be :
questions.explanation: Plastids provide colours to green colour to leaves. Plastids that provide green colour to leaf is called chloroplast that contains chlorophyll for photosynthesis. Chloromoplast impart all other colours except green.

questions.question: Majority of cells cannot be seen directly with our naked eyes because:

options.option: Cells are present only inside the body

questions.explanation: A cell is the smallest and basic unit of life which has definite structure and performs a specific function. Most of them cannot be seen with naked eyes because they are microscopic (i.e. very-very small).

questions.question: Which one of the following term is not a part of the nucleus?
questions.explanation: Ribosome is not a part of the nucleus. They are found floating in the cytsosol or attached to the endoplasmic reticulum. The nucleus is the largest organelle in cell that contain thread-like structures called chromosomes on which genes are present. The nucleolus is the round irregular structures present inside the nucleus.

questions.question: A suitable term for the various components of cells is:
questions.explanation: Various components of cells are called cell organelles. The tissue is a group of similar cells which work together to perform a particular function.

questions.question: Read the following terms and select the pair that is related to inheritance of characters.
questions.explanation: Chromosome and genes are related to the inheritance of characters. Chromosomes are present in the nucleus. They contain genes, which are the unit of inheritance.

questions.question: The unit of measurement used for expressing dimension (size) of cells is:
questions.explanation: Micrometer is the unit of measurement used for expressing dimension of cell.

questions.question: The most important function of cell membrane is that it:

options.option: controls only the entry of materials into cells.
controls the entry and exit of materials from cells.
allows entry and exit of materials without any control.
controls only the exit of materials from cells.

questions.explanation: The cell membrane controls the entry (movement into the cell) and exit (movement out of the cell) of materials. It also protects and gives shape to the cell.

questions.question: Paheli accidentally placed her hand over a flame and immediately pulled it back. She felt the sensation of heat and reacted due to the action of
questions.explanation: Nerve cells have a special shape long and branched (with projections) to receive and transmit messages between the brain and other body parts. Thus they help to feel sensations and in responding to them.

questions.question: Of the following parts of a cell listed below, name the part that is common to plant cell, animal cell and a bacterial cell.
questions.explanation: The cell membrane is a common part of every cell. It encloses the protoplasm, protects the cell, gives the cell its shape, and controls the entry and exit of substances.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a cell?
questions.explanation: The Virus is not considered a cell. It is an exception to the cell theory and cannot survive on its own.

questions.question: Which of the following feature will help you in distinguishing a plant cell from an animal cell?
questions.explanation: A cell wall is a distinguishing feature between a plant cell and an animal cell. It is a thick wall made up of cellulose around the cell membrane that exclusively present only in the plant cell.

questions.question: Under a microscope Paheli observes a cell that has a cell wall but no distinct nucleus. The cell that she observes is
questions.explanation: The cell observed by Paheli is a bacterial cell. It is a prokaryotic cell that has a cell wall (like plants) but no distinct nucleus. A plant cell has a cell wall and a well-defined nucleus, whereas, in an animal cell, the former is not present. A nerve cell is an animal cell that is long and branched to function as a transmitter between the brain and other parts.

questions.question: Chromosomes are present in
questions.explanation: Chromosomes are present in nucleus. These are condensed form of DNA packed with histone proteins. There are specific number of chromosome for every species of organisms. For example, there are 23 pairs of chromosomes in every cell of human body.

questions.question: The energy house of a cell is called-
questions.explanation: Mitochondria are tiny organelles inside cells that are involved in releasing energy from food. This process is known as cellular respiration. It is for this reason that mitochondria are often referred to as the powerhouses of the cell.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a plastid?
questions.explanation: Ribosome

questions.question: Which organelle is present in plant cell but not in animal cell?
questions.explanation: Plastids are present in plant cells which synthesise and store important chemical compounds used by the cell. There are many types of plastids as the chloroplast, chromoplast and leucoplasts. These are absent in animal cells.

questions.question:
Select correct statement from the following

  1. Viviparous animals lay eggs.
  2. External fertilisation takes place sparrow.
  3. Amoeba reproduces by binary fission.
  4. Zygote is a single cell.
questions.explanation:
1. Amoeba is a unicellular organism and it reproduces through binary fission.
  2. Zygote is a single cells that is formed as a result of the union of the male and female gametes.
  3. Viviparous animals are born as live young individuals. Therefore, they do not lay. eggs.
  4. Birds reproduce by internal fertilisation, during which the egg is fertilised inside the female.

questions.question: Which is called the Suicidal Bag?
questions.explanation: Lysosomes are known as suicide bags of the cell because they contain lytic enzymes capable of digesting cells and unwanted materials.

questions.question: Which of the following cell organ is called the siucidal bag?
questions.explanation: Lysosomes are known as suicidal bags of the cell because they have lytic enzymes which are capable of digesting cells and unwanted materials.

questions.question: Which component of the cell is called the powerhouse of the cell?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria called the power house of the cell. These organelles are involved in aerobic cellular respiration, i.e. oxidation of cellular nutrients and energy generation of the cells as ATPs.

questions.question: Which one of the following is found in plant cells but not found in the animal cells?
questions.explanation: Cell wall

questions.question: Which one of the following is found in cells of plants, animals as well as bacteria?
questions.explanation: Cell membrane or plasma membrane is found in cells of plants, animals as well as bacteria. It is outermost living, and delicate covering of cell. It separates the contents of the cell from its external environment. It helps to maintain the shape of the cell and acts as a mechanical barrier.

questions.question: Select the correct sequence of organelles missing in Amoeba, Spirogyra and Streptococcus respectively.
questions.explanation: Amoeba is an animal cell so it lacks cell wall and plastids. Spirogyra is a plant cell so it lacks centrosome and Streptococcus is a bacterial cell so it lacks true nucleus and membrane bound cell organelles.

questions.question: Refer to the given Venn diagram and select the correct option regarding it.

options.option: Organelle X is a membrane less organelle.
Organelle X is the main site of protein synthesis.
Organelle X is made up of RNA and protein.

questions.explanation: According to the given Venn diagram, organelle X is ribosome.

questions.question:
Match column I with column II and select the correct option from the given codes.

Column I Column II
P. Ribosomes (i) Suicidal bag
Q. Mitochondrion (ii) Protein factories
R. Chloroplast (iii) Powerhouse of cell
S. Lysosome (iv) Kitchen of cell
questions.explanation: P - (ii), Q - (iii), R - (iv), S - (i)

questions.question: Refer to the slide of tissue X showing some cells and select the correct option regarding it.

options.option: It is an animal tissue as the cells lack cell membrane
It is an animal tissue as the cells lack cell wall.
It is plant tissue as the cells lack vacuoles and nucleus
It is a plant tissue as the cells possess cell wall.

questions.explanation: The given figure shows slide of onion peel. It is a plant tissue where cells show prominent cell wall and large vacuole.

questions.question:
Arrange the cell organelles useful for intracellular digestion, intracellular respiration, lipid synthesis and cell secretion in a sequence.
P. Golgi complex
Q. Lysosomes
R. Mitochondria
S. SER
questions.explanation: Q - R - S - P

questions.question: Refer to the given Venn diagram and select the incorrect option.

options.option: Organelle R is an organelle that stores reserve food dissolved in water and also takes part in osmoregulation.
Organelle P could be an organelle that takes part in synthesis of ATP.
Organelle P could be an organelle that controls growth and reproduction of cell.
Organelle Q could be an organelle that contains green pigment chlorophyll.

questions.explanation: According to the given Venn diagram, P is a double membrane bound organelle that is present in both plant and animal cell. It could be either nucleus or mitochondrion. Q is a double membrane bound organelle that is present only in plant cell. So, it could be chloroplast (plastid). Organelle R is present in animal cell, so it could be centriole.

questions.question: Refer to the given flow chart and select the correct option.

options.option: Organism P manufactures its proteins with the help of ribosomes occurring freely in cytoplasm.
Lysosome is present in all organisms P, Q, R and S.
R could be Chlamydomonas whereas S could be Spirogyra.
Organism Q and R can manufacture their carbohydrates with the help of chloroplasts.

questions.explanation:
P is a prokaryote, it lacks nucleus and membrane bound organelles but it possesses ribosomes which are found freely in cytoplasm and take part in protein synthesis.
Q is an animal, R and S are plants where R is multicellular and S is unicellular.

questions.question: Identify P, Q, R and S in the given Venn diagram and select the incorrect option regarding them.

options.option: Q could be an organelle that is involved in formation of spindle during cell division.
R could be an organelle that contains genetic material organised as DNA along with proteins to form chromosomes.
S could be an organelle that produces energy currency of the cell, ATP through cellular respiration.
P could be a colourless organelle that helps to store reserve food in the form of lipids, carbohydrates and proteins.

questions.explanation: According to the given Venn diagram, cell organelles P, Q, R and S could be leucoplast, centrosome, ribosome and mitochondria respectively. Nucleus is a specialised double membrane bound protoplasmic body which contains genetic material organised as DNA along with proteins to form chromosomes.

questions.question:
In the given figure, showing the ultrastructure of a plant cell, identify the parts marked as P and Q and select the correct option regarding them.

  1. Part P helps in osmoregulation.
  2. Cellular respiration occurs in part Q.
  3. Synthesis of sugars occurs in part Q.
  4. Part P provides colour to flowers and fruits.
questions.explanation: In the given figure, P and Q represent vacuole and chloroplast respectively. Cellular respiration occurs in mitochondria and chromoplast provides colour to flowers and fruits.

questions.question: Robert Hooke observed the cells for the first time in
questions.explanation: Slice of cork.

questions.question:
Rectify the given statements by replacing the underlined words (wherever necessary) and select the incorrect option.

  1. Protoplasm occupies the space between cell membrane and the nucleus.
  2. Centriole synthesises, stores and transport material within the cell.
  3. Energy is synthesised and stored in the form of ATP by mitochondria.
  4. By-products of metabolic activity get stored in lysosome.

options.option: ATP must not be replaced as it is correctly mentioned.
Centriole must not be replaced as it is correctly mentioned.

questions.explanation: Centriole must be replaced with endoplasmic reticulum.

questions.question: Paramecium is
questions.explanation: Paramecium is a unicellular eukaryote.

questions.question: Which of the following organisms possess pseudopodia or false feet?
questions.explanation: Pseudopodia are locomotory structures formed by cellular processes present in Amoeba.

questions.question: The longest cell in human body is
questions.explanation: Neuron.

questions.question:
Select the odd one out.

  1. Euglena
  2. Amoeba
  3. Chlamydomonas
  4. Hydra
questions.explanation: Hydra is a multicellular organism whereas Euglena, Amoeba and Chlamydomonas are unicellular organisms.

questions.question: Golgi apparatus was discovered by
questions.explanation: Camillo Golgi.

questions.question: Which of the following is also known as suicidal bag of cell?
questions.explanation: Lysosomes contain hydrolytic enzymes. When its contents are released within cell then they digest entire cell hence also known as suicidal bags of cells.

questions.question: Organelle involved in modification, secretion and storage of substance is _________.
questions.explanation: Golgi apparatus.

questions.question: Which of the following is absent in animal cell?
questions.explanation: Cell wall is the outer rigid, non-living layer present exclusively in plant cells.

questions.question: The protein factories of the cell are
questions.explanation: Ribosomes are the sites of protein synthesis hence also known as protein factories of cells.

questions.question: Refer to the given Venn diagram and select the incorrect option regarding structures V, W, X, Y and Z.

options.option: Both W and X could be double membrane structures but inner membrane of W is smooth whereas that of X is thrown into folds.
W takes part in photosynthesis whereas Z connects genetic material with plasma membrane.
Y could be a membrane-less organelle involved in protein synthesis whereas Z could be an ingrowth of cell membrane that contains respiratory enzymes.
V could be an outermost protective covering which is composed of lipopolysaccharides in bacteria and cellulose in plants.

questions.explanation: W is chloroplast and X could be centrosome or lysosome. Centrosome is not bounded by any membrane and lysosome is a single membranous structure.

questions.question: ________ is the site of development of ribosomal RNAs.
questions.explanation: Nucleolus.

questions.question: ________ is the site of lipid synthesis.
questions.explanation: SER.

questions.question: If chloroplasts of plant cells are completely lost over time in the process of evolution, then which of the following processes will be directly affected?
questions.explanation: In cells, chloroplasts are involved in the process of photosynthesis. Hence, if they are removed completely, then this process will be affected.

questions.question:
Refer to the given Venn diagram and select the option that correctly identifies cell organelle P.

  1. Centrosome
  2. Cell wall
  3. Mitochondria
  4. Golgi complex
  5. Chloroplast
questions.explanation: In the given Venn diagram, P represents the cell organelles which are present both in plant cell and animal cell. Mitochondria and Golgi complex are present both in plant and animal cell. Centrosome is present only in animal cell whereas cell wall and chloroplast are present only in plant cell.

questions.question: ATP synthesis takes place in
questions.explanation: Carbohydrates and fats are oxidised with the help of enzymes present in mitochondria. The energy released during the process is used in the synthesis of ATP. Hence, mitochondria are known as powerhouse of cell.

questions.question:
Geetanjali is a student of class VIII. One day her elder sister wrote the given statements regarding a cell organelle.

  1. Bounded by single membrane
  2. Filled with watery fluid
  3. Helps in osmoregulation
  4. Stores metabolic by-products

She is referring to
questions.explanation: Vacuole

subtopics.name: Organisms show Variety in Cell Number, Shape and Size

questions.question: Refer to the given figure and select the correct option regarding it.

options.option: P synthesises proteins whereas Q synthesises carbohydrates.
P and Q are present in all cell types.
P is present only in plant cells whereas Q is present in both plant and animal cells.
P synthesises lipids whereas Q synthesises carbohydrates.

questions.explanation: In the given figure P is chloroplast and Q is rough endoplasmic reticulum.

questions.question: Refer to the given flow chart and select the correct option for organisms P, Q and R.

options.option: Organism Q is an autotroph whereas organism R is a heterotroph.
Double membrane bound cell organelles are present only in organism Q.
Organelle known as suicidal bag is characteristics of organism P and Q whereas organelle known as kitchen of cell is characteristic of organism R.
Organisms P and Q lack membrane bound organelles.

questions.explanation: According to the given flow chart, P is a prokaryote, Q is a eukaryote specifically plant and R is a eukaryote specifically animal.

questions.question:
Refer to the given characteristic of an organelle and select the option that correctly identifies it.

  1. It is abundantly found in enzymes secreting cells.
  2. It has ribosomes attached to its surface.
questions.explanation: The organelle described here is rough endoplasmic reticulum.

questions.question: Anil wants to see microscopic view of the organelle which takes part in the transmission of hereditary characters from one generation to the next. Which labelled part will his teacher focus to make a microscopic view of the organelle?
questions.explanation: In the given diagram, P, Q, R and S are mitochondria, nucleus, vacuole and ER respectively. The transmission of hereditary characters occurs through genes that are present on chromosomes in the nucleus. So, the teacher will focus on nucleus.

questions.question:
Refer to the given ultrastructure of an animal cell. Identify the labelled parts (P-S), match them with their functions and select the correct option.

  1. Synthesising proteins from amino acids
  2. Synthesising lipids
  3. Packaging of materials synthesised in cell
  4. Secreting digestive enzymes
questions.explanation: P-Ribosomes, Q-Golgi body, R-Lysosome, S-Smooth endoplasmic reticulum.

questions.question: The cell organelle not present in animal cell is ________ .
questions.explanation: cell wall

questions.question:
Organelle X is abundantly present in those cells of our body that fight against pathogens by engulfing and destroying them. They are more in number in animal cells than plant cells.
Identify organelle X and select the correct option regarding it.

options.option: Organelle X is often referred to as powerhouse of cell.
Organelle X is bound by double membrane.

questions.explanation: Organelle X described here is lysosome which is single membrane bound organelle. It contains hydrolytic enzymes and help in disposal of cell debris and dead cells themselves. It can bring about entire cell death if its contents are released within cell. Hence, it is also called suicidal bags of cells.

questions.question:
In a survey, it was found that organelle P is deteriorating in a population of unicellular organisms present in an area. Major reason for this was found out to be a chemical mutagen present in water and soil of that locality. The organelle is membrane less and occurs freely in cytoplasm and also found attached to ER.
Which of the following holds true regarding this?

options.option: The organisms will die immediately due to stoppage of cellular respiration.
The organisms will survive for sometime but will die eventually as it cannot manufacture its proteins, some of which are essential for maintaining life processes.
The organisms will survive for sometime but will die eventually as they cannot perform photosynthesis.
The organisms will die immediately as it cannot manufacture its carbohydrates and lipids.

questions.explanation: The organelle described here is ribosome. Ribosomes are the main sites of protein synthesis. In their absence the cell will not be able to manufacture some of its crucial proteins. If the organelle is deteriorating slowly in an organism then it will eventually die.

questions.question: Prokaryotic cells are found in ________ .
questions.explanation: bacteria

questions.question:
Refer to the given characteristics of organelle X.

  1. Involved in the formation of those organelles that contain hydrolytic enzymes
  2. Involved in secretion, modification and storage of substances

Identify organelle X and select the correct statement regarding it.

options.option: Organelle X is often referred to as protein factories of cell.
Organelle X is absent in all metabolically active cells.
Organelle X is known as dictyosome in plant cells.
Organelle X is absent in Chlorella and Ulothrix.

questions.explanation: The organelle described here is Golgi apparatus. In plant cells it occurs as discrete units known as dictyosomes.

questions.question:
The given figure shows an unicellular organism known as amoeba. Why there is no need for an amoeba to have division of labour?

  1. It is in contact with the surroundings directly
  2. It obtains nutrients and removes waste products between the cell and the surrounding
questions.explanation: Only II

questions.question:
Lysosomes are called suicide bag of the cell because

  1. It causes destruction of a cell through the action of its own enzymes
  2. Lysosomes engulfs viruses and bacterias
questions.explanation: Only I

questions.question:
The unicellular organism is ________ .
A.  B.  C.  D.

Which one of the following statements is not CORRECT about cell wall of plant cells?

  1. It is made up of cellulose.
  2. It protects the cells.
  3. It is made up of fats.
  4. It gives shape to the cell.
questions.explanation: It is made up of fats.

questions.question: The functional unit of life is called ________ .
questions.explanation: cell

questions.question: The jell-like fluid substance present in cell is called ________.
questions.explanation: cytoplasm

questions.question:
Identify the CORRECT statement.

  1. Tissue is a group of dissimilar cells.
  2. An organ consists of similar cells.
  3. Vacuoles are not found in plant cells.
  4. Prokaryotes do not have nucleus.
questions.explanation: Vacuoles are not found in plant cells.

questions.question: Who is known as the Father of Microbiology?
questions.explanation: Antonie van Leeuwenhoek

questions.question: Plasmodesmata are located in narrow areas of ________ .
questions.explanation: cell wall

questions.question: Which one of the following is called the energy currency of the cell?
questions.explanation: ATP

questions.question: Which of the following cell organelles does not contain DNA?
questions.explanation: Chloroplast

questions.question:
Which of these cell organelle and its function are matched CORRECTLY?

  1. Chloroplast - Controls all activities.
  2. Nucleus - Gives shape to the body.
  3. Cell wall - Synthesizes food.
  4. Mitochondria - Provides energy.
questions.explanation: Mitochondria - Provides energy.

questions.question: Rearrange the cell division given below and select the CORRECT option.
questions.explanation: i, iii, ii, iv

questions.question: Jiya observed a cell under a microscope. She saw a transparent jelly- like substances in the cell. What was that substance?
questions.explanation: Cytoplasm

questions.question: Which cell organelle is responsible for cell respiration?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria

questions.question: Which one of the following has a prokaryotic structure?
questions.explanation: Bacteria

questions.question: The cell shown in the diagram is________ .
questions.explanation: RBC

questions.question: Identify the organelle which is responsible for making proteins.
questions.explanation: Ribosome

questions.question: Look at the diagram shown here. Which of the following best describes the area marked as P?

options.option: It controls the substances entering and leaving the cell.
Most of the cell processes take place here.

questions.explanation: Most of the cell processes take place here.

questions.question: X is the part of a cell that carries information that controls the characteristics that are passed from one generation to the next generation. Identify X.
questions.explanation: Nucleus

questions.question:
Identify the correct match.

  1. Leucoplasts - The colorless plastids
  2. Chloroplasts-The green coloured plastids
  3. Chromoplasts-The coloured plastids
  4. Protoplasts - These are not plastids
questions.explanation: All of these

questions.question:
Which of the following statement(s) is /are correct?

  1. Cilia are present in Paramecium
  2. Flagella are present in Euglena
  3. Cilia help in growth of the cell
  4. Both cilia and flagella help in movement
questions.explanation: I, II and IV

questions.question: X and Y are two cells. X is a dead cell while Y is a living cell. Which of the following explains the correct difference between X and Y?

options.option: There is change in the surrounding atmosphere of both X and Y.
There is change in the specific organization of both X and Y.
The vital processes of X are destroyed while the vital processes of Y are not destroyed.
There is no change in the surrounding atmosphere of both X and Y.

questions.explanation: There is change in the surrounding atmosphere of both X and Y.

questions.question: The given diagram shows a small glass object placed on the material which is to be mounted. This is called______and it is placed very cautiously to ______.

options.option: Glass slide, avoid folding of the material

questions.explanation: Cover slip, avoid air bubbles

questions.question: What is the benefit of having a division of labour in a multicellular living organism?

options.option: It enables the multicellular living organism to defend the bacteria better.
It enables efficient functioning of the processes in a multicellular living organism.
It reduces the energy requirement in a multicellular living organism.
It reduces the waste products produced in a multicellular living organism.

questions.explanation: It enables efficient functioning of the processes in a multicellular living organism.

questions.question: Jiya had a fall and had some bruises. Which of the following statements describes what will happen to the cells in her body?

options.option: The cells in the body will not be affected by the fall.
The number of cells in the body will remain the same.
New cells will be produced to replace the damaged cells.
The cells which were knocked by the fall will double itself.

questions.explanation: New cells will be produced to replace the damaged cells.

questions.question: Nerve cells help our body to connect and communicate messages to our brain. Which of the following correctly explain the function of nerve cells?

options.option: It transport oxygen to all the parts of the body.
It brings about movement in the body parts.
It carry message to and from the brain

questions.explanation: It carry message to and from the brain

questions.question: Riya has prepared cheek cell mount and Tina has prepared onion cell mount. To distinguish microscopically between both the cell mounts, one should look for the presence or absence of:
questions.explanation: Plastid

questions.question: The barrier between the protoplasm and outer environment in a plant cell is
questions.explanation: cell membrane

questions.question: Animal cell is limited by
questions.explanation: plasma membrane

questions.question: Which of the following would not be considered part of a cell's cytoplasm?
questions.explanation: Nucleus

questions.question: The cell organelle associated with cell secretion is
questions.explanation: Golgi apparatus

questions.question: If the nucleus is a cell's control centre and chloroplasts its solar collectors. Which of the following might be called the cells combination food processor and garbage disposer?
questions.explanation: Lysosome

questions.question: Lysosomes are the reservoirs of
questions.explanation: hydrolytic enzymes

subtopics.name: Comparison of Plant and Animal Cells

questions.question: Ribosomes are the centre for
questions.explanation: protein synthesis

questions.question: Which of the following is called the brain of the cell?
questions.explanation: Nucleus

questions.question: Which one is not a part of nucleus?
questions.explanation: Endoplasmic reticulum

questions.question: Nucleus is separated from surrounding cytoplasm by a nuclear envelope which is
questions.explanation: double and porous

questions.question: Nucleoplasm is continuous with cytoplasm through
questions.explanation: Nuclear pores

questions.question: The common feature amongst nucleus, chloroplast and mitochondrion is
questions.explanation: DNA

questions.question: The function of the nucleolus in the cell is
questions.explanation: synthesis of RNA and ribosomes

questions.question: Type of reproduction in which fusion of male and female gametes occur is called
questions.explanation: The fusion of male and female gametes takes place during sexual reproduction. Fusion results to the formation of zygote that is first cell of any living organism.

questions.question: In asexual reproduction
questions.explanation: In asexual reproduction only one parent is involved. The new organism is identical to parent. No gametes formation takes place in asexual reproduction.

questions.question: Human male germ cell is called
questions.explanation: Human male germ cell is called sperm. Sperm is produced inside the testes of male. Sperm contain head and long tail that help them to swim.

questions.question: Internal fertilization do not occurs in
questions.explanation: The mode of fertilization in which fusion of male and female gamete takes place inside the body of female is called internal fertilization. In fish fertilization occurs outside the body of female fish.

questions.question: The transformation of larva into adults through drastic change is called
questions.explanation: The process of transfer of larva into adults through drastic change in their body is called metamorphosis. Larva changes into totally different shape and size through metamorphosis.

questions.question: Fusion of male and female gamete is called
questions.explanation: The process of fusion of male and female gamete is called fertilization. Fertilization may be internal or external depending upon organism.

questions.question: Budding is found in
questions.explanation: Budding is a mode of asexual reproduction in which a small outgrowth develop on body surface that enlarge and get separated from parent body. Budding takes place in hydra and yeast.

questions.question: Puberty ends when an adolescent reaches
questions.explanation: The age at which boys and girls start producing male and female gametes is called puberty. Puberty end when an adolescent reaches reproductive maturity.

questions.question: Process of formation of spermatozoa is called
questions.explanation: The formation of spermatozoa is called spermatogenesis. Spermatogenesis occurs in testes of male at puberty to form male gametes.

questions.question: The testes produce male gametes called
questions.explanation: Testes is primary sex organ of male in which sperms are produced at maturity. Sperms are male gametes produced in large number for fertilization.

questions.question: Fusion of male and female gametes produces
questions.explanation: The process in which fusion of male and female gamete takes place is called fertilization. Fertilization leads to formation of single cell called zygote.

questions.question: Internal fertilisation occurs
questions.explanation: Internal fertilization occurs inside the female body. Male gamete is transferred inside the female body during copulation where gametes fuse to produce zygote.

questions.question: A tadpole develops into an adult frog by the process of
questions.explanation: A tadpole develops into an adult frog by the process of metamorphosis. A tadpole resemble to fish having tail that undergoes morphological change to change into frog.

questions.question: The number of nuclei present in a zygote is
questions.explanation: Zygote is formed by fusion of male and female gametes. Each zygote contains one nuclei having 2n number of chromosome. Zygote is the first cell of life.

questions.question: Menstrual cycle is repeated at an average interval of
questions.explanation: Menstrual cycle is repeated at an average interval of 29 days. In the middle of menstrual cycle egg is released.

questions.question: Cessation of menstrual cycle in female is known as
questions.explanation: Cessation of menstrual cycle in female is known as menopause. Menopause occurs at the age of about 50 years.

questions.question: The process of delivery of the foetus is called
questions.explanation: The process of delivery of the foetus is called parturition. Parturition occurs at the end of pregnancy period of 40 weeks.

questions.question: Conversion of spematid into a spermatozoan is called
questions.explanation: Conversion of spematids present inside the testis into spermatozoa is called spermiogenesis.

questions.question: The release of egg from ovum is described as
questions.explanation: The release of egg from ovum is described as ovulation. Ovulation occurs in the middle of menstrual cycle form each ovary each month.

questions.question: In the male human, sperm contain 22 pairs of autosomes and
questions.explanation: A human cell containing 22 autosomes and a chromosome is a sperm. A human sperm contains either an X or a Y chromosome, thereby determining the sex of the offspring after fertilization. XX = female. XY = male.

questions.question: The period between fertilization and birth is called as
questions.explanation: The period between fertilization and birth of fully mature child is called gestation. Gestation period varies from animal to animal. In human beings, gestation period is 40 weeks.

questions.question: Which hormone controls metamorphosis in frogs?
questions.explanation: Metamorphosis is the morphological change in tadpole into adult frog. Metamorphosis requires hormone thyroxin to undergo metamorphosis.

questions.question: Which one is the most likely reason of not occurring regular menstrual cycle

options.option: Maintenance of high concentration of sex hormones

questions.explanation: Irregular menstrual cycle may be due to a number of hormonal or physiological reasons but most likely reason of not occurring regular menstrual cycle is fertilization of ovum.

questions.question: The yellowish coloured milk secreted from the breast shortly after birth of the baby is called
questions.explanation: The yellow coloured milk secreted from the breast shortly after birth of the baby is called colostrum. This milk provide immunity to child to protect against a number of diseases.

questions.question: What is the number of sperms released in vagina in single insemination?
questions.explanation: During copulation insemination occurs in which male release about 400 millions of sperms present in semen.

questions.question: External genitalia of human female is called as
questions.explanation: External genitalia of human female are called as Vulva. Vulva are muscular fatty structure having fold of skin from outer side that leads to vagina.

questions.question: Copper-T prevents from
questions.explanation: Copper –T is an intrauterine contraceptive device that helps in contraception. It is implanted in uterus by trained nurse or doctor. It prevents the fertilization of male and female gametes.

questions.question: Two hormones that regulate the menstrual cycle are
questions.explanation: Menstrual cycle in female is regulated by hormone estrogens and progesterone. Menstrual cycle is a 28-29 days cycle during which ovulation occurs in middle of this cycle.

questions.question: Reproduction is essential life processes which help in
questions.explanation: Reproduction is essential life processes which help in continuity of race as the life span of each individual if fixed after which organism dies.

questions.question: Which of the following organism do shows binary fission
questions.explanation: Binary fission takes place in amoeba during which amoeba divides into two daughter cells by simple meiotic division.

questions.question: Fertilisation is external in
questions.explanation: The mode of fertilization in which fusion of male and female gametes takes place outside the female body. External fertilization occurs in fish and amphibians.

questions.question: Hydra reproduce asexually by formation of
questions.explanation: Hydra reproduces asexually by formation of small outgrowth on the body that enlarge and separate out as new species called budding.

questions.question: Sexual reproduction is better mode of reproduction because offspring
questions.explanation: Sexual reproduction is better mode of reproduction because offspring have better chances of survival. Sexual reproduction leads to creation of variation that help in survival of species.

questions.question: Every sexually reproducing organism, including human being begin life as a single cell called
questions.explanation: Zygote is formed by fusion of male and female gametes. Every sexually reproducing organism including human beings begin life as single cell called zygote.

questions.question: The features that help to distinguish the male from female are called
questions.explanation: Male and female can be distinguished from each other externally by secondary sexual characters such as breast, mustache, beard, etc.

questions.question: The sex of child depends on the
questions.explanation: Male produce two kinds of sperms, half containing X chromosome and half contain Y chromosome. The sperm that fertise the egg of female decides the sex of child. X cromosome is responsible for the development of female and Y cromosome is responsible for male .

questions.question: Female hired to develop the in-vitro fertilized egg of another female to maturity is called
questions.explanation: Surrogacy is a method or agreement whereby a woman agrees to carry a pregnancy for another person or persons, who will become the newborn child's parent(s) after birth. The female hired to develop the in-vitro fertilized egg of another female to maturity is called surrogate mother.

questions.question: In human female, menstruation can be deferred by the administration of
questions.explanation: In human female, menstruation can be deferred by the administration of combination of estrogen and progesterone hormone as pills to avoid pregnancy.

questions.question: It is misconception that

options.option: Sex is determined by vigor of male
Women are responsible for sex of child
Male are responsible of sex of child

questions.explanation: Misconception is wrong believing spread in society. It is a misconception that women are responsible for sex of child, although, father is responsible for sex of child.  There are two types of sex chromosomes X and Y chromosomes. Female cell contains the XX pair and the male contains XY pair. A human sperm contains either an X or a Y chromosome, thereby determining the sex of the offspring after fertilization. XX = female. XY = male.

questions.question: The growing foetus draws its nourishment through the
questions.explanation: The growing foetus gets its nourishment through placenta. Placenta is a tube like structure that connects foetus with mother uterus.

questions.question: The common duct formed by union of vas deference and duct from seminal vesicle is called
questions.explanation: The common duct formed by union of vas deference and duct from seminal vesicle is called urethra. Urethra is common duct for release of urine and semen during copulation.

questions.question: The delivery of child or child birth is called
questions.explanation: The delivery of child or child birth is called parturition. Parturition occurs at the completion of gestation period through vagina.

questions.question: Placenta is

options.option: Channel for providing essential requirement for growth of embryo

questions.explanation: Placenta is channel for providing essential requirement for growth of embryo. Through placenta nutrients and gases are transferred to foetus from mother.

questions.question: Seminal vessels is present at the junction of
questions.explanation: A seminal vessel is present at the junction of prostate and urethra. This vessel release fluid medium and nutrient for sperm movement.

questions.question: In humans, the development of fertilised egg takes place in the
questions.explanation: The development of the fertilised egg takes place in the uterus (womb). The embryo gets embedded in the wall of the uterus for development by implantation.

questions.question: Animals exhibiting external fertilisation produce a large number of gametes. Pick the appropriate reason from the following.

options.option: Food is available in plenty in water.
The animals are small in size and want to produce more offsprings.
Water promotes production of large number of gametes.

questions.explanation: A large number of gametes are released by such animals to ensure a better chance of fertilization. Since the gametes are laid in water, many of them are carried away by moving water, wind, or rain and many are eaten by other animals. Therefore, the production of a large number of gametes is necessary.

questions.question: Reproduction by budding takes place in
questions.explanation: In Hydra, reproduction takes place by budding. A small part of the body of an organism grows as 'bud' which then detaches and becomes a new organism. The other options Amoeba, Paramecium, and bacteria reproduce asexually by binary fission.

questions.question: In human beings, after fertilisation, the structure which gets embedded in the wall of uterus is
questions.explanation: After fertilization, the zygote divides repeatedly to form a ball of hundreds of cells called an embryo. The embryo moves down from oviduct into the uterus, where it gets embedded. This is called implantation.

questions.question: Aquatic animals in which fertilisation occurs in water are said to be:
questions.explanation: The aquatic animals in which fertilization occurs in water are oviparous with external fertilization. They lay eggs and release sperms outside the body in the water for fertilization to take place. The young one top is formed outside the body in water from the zygote formed inside eggs.

questions.question: After fertilization, the resulting cell which gives rise to a new individual is the:
questions.explanation: After fertilization (i.e. combining of sperm with the egg), the zygote is formed. It is the beginning of the formation of a new individual. All the multicellular animals start their life from a single cell called a zygote.

questions.question: Which of the following test determines or finalizes fatherhood?
questions.explanation: Deoxyribonucleic acid, or DNA, is the genetic material you inherit from your mother and father. Paternity refers to fatherhood. A DNA paternity test uses DNA, usually taken from a cheek swab, to determine whether a man is the child’s biological father.

questions.question:
Which one of the following is oviparous?

  1. Human being
  2. Dog
  3. Cow
  4. Hen
questions.explanation: Hen is oviparous animal. It lays eggs. Cows, dogs and human beings are viviparous animals which give birth to young ones.

questions.question: Two cells that fuse to give zygote are called
questions.explanation: Two cells that fuse to give zygote are called gametes. These are formed in sexually reproducing organisms in male and female individuals. Males produce sperms and females produce ova or egg cell in their respective reproductive systems.

questions.question: The gender of an infant is determined by-
questions.explanation: A child’s gender (male or female) is determined by the chromosome that the male parent contributes. Females have XX sex chromosomes. Males have XY sex chromosomes. A male infant result if the male contributes his Y chromosome while a female infant results if he contributes his X chromosome.

questions.question: External fertilisation occurs in
questions.explanation: External fertilisation usually occurs in aquatic environments where both eggs and sperm are released into the water e g. Fishes, amphibians, etc.

questions.question: Who is known as the father of Genetics?
questions.explanation: Gregor Mendel is known as the Father of Genetics. He experimented on pea plants and discovered the basic inheritance rules. He deduced that genes come in pairs and are inherited as distinct units, one from each parent.

questions.question: Dolly, the sheep, was born on 5th July, 1996 and was the first mammal to be
questions.explanation: Dolly, the sheep, was born on 5th July, 1996 was first mammal to be produced through cloning (differentiated animal cell). It is the production of organism, which are genetically identical to their parents. These idential structures are called clones.

questions.question: External fertilisation is very common in
questions.explanation: External fertilisation is a mode of reproduction in which a male organism's sperm fertilises a female organism's egg outside of the female's body. For example, In amphibian (toads, frogs), mostly algae, bryophytes, salmon, starfish, etc. external fertilisation takes place in water.

questions.question: Which of these is not a mammal?
questions.explanation: Sharks are not mammals as they do not own any of the characteristic features that define a mammal. They are not warm-blooded. Sharks are considered as a species of fish, but unlike most fishes, a sharks’ skeleton is made of cartilage. They are carnivores and they feed on other smaller fish.

questions.question: By which physical process, thrombin is related?
questions.explanation: Blood Clotting and Fibrinogen. Thrombin is proteolytically cleaved from prothrombin in the clotting cascade. Thrombin converts fibrinogen into fibrin. It binds thrombin and inhibits thus the further coagulation process.

questions.question:
Hen ________

  1. undergoes external fertilisation
  2. is oviparous
  3. is viviparous
  4. undergoes internal fertilisation
questions.explanation:
Hen is an oviparous bird which lays eggs. In all birds, internal fertilisation takes place. Thus, the development of embryo occurs outside the body.
Hence, option (ii) and (iv) both are correct.

questions.question: In Amoeba, reproduction takes place by
questions.explanation: Amoeba is an unicellular protozoan organism, in which the reproduction mainly takes place by binary fission in favourable conditions. It is a mode of asexual reproduction. In this process the parent cell divides into two daughter cells of equal size. These are genetically identical to each other.

questions.question: Insufficient production of ________ in the tadpoles leads to their incomplete development.
questions.explanation: Insufficient production of thyroxine leads to incomplete development of tadpole larva into adult (metamorphism). Iodine is required for the production of thyroxine. If the water body in which larva is present lacks sufficient iodine, then it will also affect the metamorphism of tadpole.

questions.question: At the onset of puberty ________ is released by testes.
questions.explanation: At the onset of puberty in males, (sex hormone) testosterone is released by testes. It helps in the production of sperms in testes and development of secondary sexual characters like deeper voices growth of facial hair, growth In height, etc.

questions.question: Reproduction is
questions.explanation: biological process of producing young ones

questions.question: Asexual reproduction is common in
questions.explanation: All of these

questions.question: Which type of reproduction amongst the following involves single parent?
questions.explanation: Asexual reproduction

questions.question: The production of an exact copy of an animal by asexual reproduction is known as
questions.explanation: cloning

questions.question: Male gametes are also called
questions.explanation: Both antherozoid and sperm

questions.question: Female gametes are also called
questions.explanation: both egg and ovum

questions.question: Which of the following is not a part of the human male reproductive system?
questions.explanation: Oviducts

questions.question: The egg laying mammals are
questions.explanation: Both Platypus and Echidna

questions.question: The faulty functioning of an endocrine gland can make a person very short or very tall. This gland is
questions.explanation: pituitary

questions.question: The beginning of menstruation at puberty is called
questions.explanation: menarche

questions.question: The male hormone is
questions.explanation: testosterone

questions.question: The most conspicuous visible change that occurs in boys during puberty is
questions.explanation: development in voice box

questions.question:
Given below are events that lead to pregnancy and development of embryo.

  1. Fertilisation of egg
  2. Maturation of egg
  3. Release of egg
  4. Embedding of embryo in uterus

Which of the following options gives the correct order of sequence in which they occur?

The dramatic changes in body features associated with puberty are mainly because of the secretions of

  1. Thyroxine
  2. Oestrogen
  3. Adrenaline
  4. Testosterone
questions.explanation: (ii) and (iv)

questions.question:
Pimples and acne are formed due to the increased activity of

  1. Adrenal glands
  2. Sebaceous giands
  3. Thyroid gland
  4. Sweat glands
questions.explanation: (ii) and (iv)

questions.question: Adolescents should be careful about what they eat, because

options.option: proper diet is needed for the rapid growth taking place in their body

questions.explanation: proper diet is needed for the rapid growth taking place in their body

questions.question: Male reproductive part of the flower is
questions.explanation: stamen

questions.question: The process of release of ovum by female organism is called ________.
questions.explanation: ovulation

questions.question: Sperms move by
questions.explanation: tail

questions.question: How many sperms take part in fusion with female gamete?
questions.explanation: Only one

questions.question: Fertilization of ovum takes place in
questions.explanation: fallopian tube

questions.question: During in-vitro fertilization, the process of fertilization, occurs in
questions.explanation: test tube in laboratory (outside the body)

questions.question: Egg laying animals are known as
questions.explanation: oviparous

questions.question: During metamorphosis

options.option: pupa of silk worm change into adult
both tadpole change into adult & pupa of silk worm change into adult

questions.explanation: both tadpole change into adult & pupa of silk worm change into adult

questions.question: Which one of the following is concerned with asexual reproduction?
questions.explanation: Spores

questions.question: Binary fission occurs in
questions.explanation: All of these

questions.question: Which type of reproduction is most common in Hydra?
questions.explanation: Budding

questions.question: Binary fission is a form of
questions.explanation: asexual reproduction

questions.question: Fragmentation occurs in
questions.explanation: Spirogyra

questions.question: What is the duration of pregnancy in a human female?
questions.explanation: 36-40 weeks

questions.question: What is the term used for the structure formed after the fertilisation of male and and female gamete?
questions.explanation: Zygote

questions.question: Which of the following give birth to young ones?
questions.explanation: Tiger

questions.question: In which of the following process, both the parents are required to produce their offspring?
questions.explanation: Sexual reproduction

questions.question: Which of the following organisms show fragmentation?
questions.explanation: Planaria

questions.question: Which of the following type of reproduction does an amoeba show?
questions.explanation: Binary fission

questions.question: How many types of fertilisations happen in animals?
Which of the following animals is a hermaphrodite?
questions.explanation: Earthworm

questions.question: Which of the following animalslay eggs?
questions.explanation: Birds

questions.question: Which of the following animals show metamorphosis?
questions.explanation: Butterfly

questions.question: The age at which boys and girls start producing gametes is called__________.
questions.explanation: The age at which boys and girls start producing gametes is called puberty. A number of changes take place in the body at puberty. On average, girls begin puberty around ages 10 –11 and end puberty around 15 -17; boys begin around ages 11–12 and end around 16-17.

questions.question: The right meal for adolescents consists of
questions.explanation: The right meals for adolescents include chapatti, dal, Vegetable etc. The meal should provide carbohydrates, proteins and fats along with vitamins for proper growth and development. One should take balanced diet during puberty.

questions.question: Which among the boys and girls puberty starts earlier?
questions.explanation: Puberty starts early in girls in comparison to boys of the same age. In girls, it starts at the age of around 12-13 years.

questions.question: Deficiency of which hormone cause diabetes?
questions.explanation: Deficiency of insulin hormone causes diabetes. Insulin converts the excess sugar in the blood into glycogen to lower the sugar level in blood.

questions.question: Which one is another terms for teenage?
questions.explanation: Adolescence is another term for teenage. The age between 12-19 years is called as teenage. During this period many physiological changes take place in the body.

questions.question: Glands without ducts?
questions.explanation: Endocrine glands are ductless glands which secrete hormones. Hormones are chemical messengers secreted by endocrine glands directly into the blood.

questions.question: Which gland release insulin hormone?
questions.explanation: Insulin hormone is released by the pancreas. Insulin controls the sugar level in the blood to avoid high blood pressure and heart disease.

questions.question: Thyroxin produced by
questions.explanation: Thyroid gland

questions.question: Reproductive phase in women continues for how many decades?
questions.explanation: Four

questions.question: Pancreas secretes which of the following hormones?
questions.explanation: Insulin

questions.question: Hormone reaches here through the bloodstream?
questions.explanation: Hormones are released by endocrine glands. The hormone reaches to target site through the bloodstream.

questions.question: Which gland is called dual gland?
questions.explanation: The testis is called dual gland as testis produces sperms as well as male hormone testosterone.

questions.question: The most conspicuous visible change that occurs in boys during puberty is:
questions.explanation: The most clearly visible change during puberty is the sudden increase in height of boys and girls. Appearance of moustache and beard in boys and enlargement of the mammary gland in girls are other changes.

questions.question: Which mineral is essential for formation of thyroxin?
questions.explanation: Iodine is essential for formation of thyroxin hormone. Thyroxin hormone is essential for metabolism of nutrients and mental development.

questions.question: The process of changing tadpole to frog is called__________.
questions.explanation: The process of changing tadpole to frog is called metamorphosis. During metamorphosis fish likes tadpole changes into adult frog.

questions.question: Reproduction is essential life processes which help in
questions.explanation: Reproduction is essential life process which helps in the continuity of race or species on earth as each individual has a fix life span.

questions.question: The period of pregnancy is known as
questions.explanation: The period of pregnancy is known as gestation period. It ranges from fertilization of ovum to parturition. It is of about 40 weeks.

questions.question: The release of egg from ovum is described as
questions.explanation: The release of ovum or egg from ovary is known as ovulation. Ovulation occurs at the middle of menstrual cycle.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a secondary sexual feature?
questions.explanation: Pimples, moustache and body hairs are a secondary sexual character in male and female. Ovulation is the release of ovum from ovary which is a primary sexual feature.

questions.question: Menstrual cycle, in human female is completed in
questions.explanation: Menstrual cycle in human female starts at puberty. Menstrual cycle is completed in generally 28 days. In the middle of the menstrual cycle, ovulation takes place.

questions.question: All organism reproduce sexually after certain growth and maturity. That period of growth is called
questions.explanation: All organism reproduce sexually after certain growth and maturity. That period of growth is called the growth phase which completes at puberty.

questions.question: Every sexually reproducing organism, including a human being, begins life as a single cell called________.
questions.explanation: Fusion of male and female gametes results into formation of zygote. Zygote is the first single cell from which all the sexually reproducing organism begin their life.

questions.question: Which of the following is a primary sex organ?
questions.explanation: Ovary is the primary sex organ of female. Ovary produces female gamete called ovum and release female hormone progesterone and estrogen.

questions.question: Hormone responsible for milk ejection after the birth of baby is:-
questions.explanation: Milk ejection start from mammary gland of female just after parturition of baby. Oxytocin hormone is responsible for milk ejection after the birth of baby.

questions.question: Secondary sexual changes occurs
questions.explanation: Secondary sexual changes occur at puberty in which a number of physical and morphological changes occur that includes moustache and beard in male and enlargement of mammary gland.

questions.question: In the male human, sperm contains 22 pairs of autosomes and
questions.explanation: A human cell contains 22 pairs of autosomes and 2 pairs of sex chromosomes. The sex chromosomes are the X and the Y chromosomes and human females have two X chromosomes while the human males have one X and one Y chromosomes.

questions.question: Sex hormones are released by:
questions.explanation: Sex hormones are released by testes in males called testosterone and in females by ovaries called progesterone and estrogen.

questions.question: Which of the following is a exocrine gland?
questions.explanation: Those glands that have ducts to pass secretion to target organ is called exocrine gland. Salivary gland is an example of exocrine gland. Pancreas, pituitary, and adrenal glands are endocrine glands.

questions.question: The gland whose development is affected by iodine is________.
questions.explanation: Development of the thyroid gland is affected by iodine. Thyroid gland is located in the neck that produces thyroxin hormone.

questions.question: Which hormone controls metamorphosis in frogs?
questions.explanation: Metamorphosis in frogs is controlled by thyroxin hormone. Metamorphosis is a drastic change in the shape of organisms as a tadpole to frog.

questions.question: Increase in size of the breast and oily skin are:
questions.explanation: At puberty, a number of changes take place in boys and girls. Increase in the size of the breast and oily skin are secondary sexual characters of girls.

questions.question: A woman is not able to produce a child after menopause as:
questions.explanation: A woman is not able to produce a child after menopause because the menstrual cycle of such women get stopped and gamete is not released.

questions.question: Chips and packed snacks should never replace regular meals as they do not have
questions.explanation: Chips and packed snacks should never replace regular meals comprising of bread and rice along with vegetables and pulses as these things do not have adequate nutritional value.

questions.question: Which of the following disease is sexually transmitted?
questions.explanation: The disease which is transmitted from one partner to another partner during sexual intercourse is called sexually transmitted disease. AIDS is a sexually transmitted disease.

questions.question: Which of the following contain iron rich food?
questions.explanation: Green leafy vegetable like spinach contains iron rich food that is essential for haemoglobin formation in the blood.

questions.question: Which of the following minerals is an essential constituent of hemoglobin of blood?
questions.explanation: Iron is an essential constituent of hemoglobin of blood. Hemoglobin is essential for carrying oxygen in the blood.

questions.question: Walking and playing in fresh air keeps the body
questions.explanation: Walking and playing in fresh air keeps the body fit and healthy. Walking and playing increase the heartbeat and controls the blood pressure.

questions.question: AIDS is caused by dangerous virus called__________.
questions.explanation: Acquired immune deficiency syndrome (AIDS) is caused by a dangerous virus called human immune virus (HIV).

questions.question: Which of the following activity do not cause HIV infection?
questions.explanation: Shaking hands with HIV positive person do not cause HIV infection. It is transmitted by sexual contact with HIV infected person, sharing syringe with HIV infected person and from infected mother to their child.

questions.question: We should not use drugs as they are_________.
questions.explanation: We should not use drugs as drugs are addictive in nature as a person start using it, it becomes difficult to give up the same that ruin the family and health.

questions.question: The period of life, where the body undergoes changes leading to reproductive maturity is called__________.
questions.explanation: The period of life, where the body undergoes changes leading to reproductive maturity is called adolescence.

subtopics.name: Role of Hormones in Initiating Reproductive Function

questions.question: Puberty ends when an adolescent reaches:
questions.explanation: Puberty ends when an adolescent reaches reproductive maturity and becomes able to reproduce physically and mentally as well.

questions.question: Initially girls grow faster than boys. At what age both reaches to their maximum height?
questions.explanation: Initially girls grow faster than boys. At the age of 18 years both boys and girls reaches to their maximum height.

subtopics.name: Reproductive Phase of Life in Humans

questions.question: Acne and pimples on face is due to secretion:
questions.explanation: Acne and pimples on face are due to secretion of oily glands during puberty. Acne and pimples occur in boys and girls at puberty.

questions.question: First menstrual flow is called_________.
questions.explanation: The first menstrual flow is called menarche that starts at puberty in girls. After menarche menstrual cycle takes place regularly till menopause.

questions.question: The change from larva to adult is called__________.
questions.explanation: The change from larva to adult is called metamorphosis. During metamorphosis, organism changes suddenly into new form of organism.

questions.question: The disease diabetes mellitus is the result of
questions.explanation: The disease diabetes mellitus is the result of under secretion of insulin hormone from the pancreas. Insulin controls the sugar level in blood.

questions.question: The gland which is exocrine and endocrine both is
questions.explanation: The pancreas gland is exocrine as well as an endocrine gland because the pancreas releases hormones as well as pancreatic enzymes for digestion of protein.

questions.question: The development of a new individual from a single gamete without fertilization is called
questions.explanation: The development of a new individual from a single gamete without fertilization is called parthenogenesis. In honey bee, production takes place by pathogenesis.

questions.question: What will happen if the thyroid is removed from a tadpole?

options.option: The larva will produce a giant frog
The tadpole will grow into a dwarf frog

questions.explanation: If the thyroid is removed from a tadpole, it will continue in the larval stage as thyroxin hormone is necessary for the metamorphosis of a tadpole to frog.

questions.question: It is misconception that:

options.option: Sex is determined by vigor of male
Male is responsible for the sex of the child
Women are responsible for sex of child

questions.explanation: It is a misconception that women are responsible for the sex of the child. Male sperm is actually responsible for the sex of the child as it may contain X or Y sex chromosome.

questions.question: Ovary and testis release hormone as well as gametes, so they are called___________.
questions.explanation: Ovary produces ovum as well as produce hormone progesterone and testis release sperm as well as testosterone hormone. So, ovary and testis are called dual gland.

subtopics.name: How is the Sex of the Baby Determined?

questions.question: The belief that the mother is completely responsible for the sex of the child is wrong because the child:

options.option: develops in the body of the mother.
gets sex chromosome only from the mother.
gets sex chromosome only from the father.
gets one sex chromosome from the mother and the other from the father.

questions.explanation: The child gets one sex chromosome from the mother and one from the father. The mother has X X combination and always contributes an X-chromosome. It is the sperm of father that determines the sex of the child. This is because half of the sperms have X-chromosome and the other half have Y-chromosome.

questions.question: AIDS can spread from an infected person to another person through
questions.explanation:
AIDS-causing virus can be easily passed from an infected person to another person through:

  1. Sharing of syringes for blood transfusion, taking drugs, etc.
  2. Sexual contact.
  3. From infected mother to foetus.

It is not spread through an insect vector (such as insect bite) or through casual contact (such as sharing food, comb, etc).

questions.question: For the metamorphosis of tadpoles which of the following elements must be available in the water?
questions.explanation: For metamorphosis of tadpoles, iodine should be present in water. This is because metamorphosis is brought about by thyroxine hormone that requires the presence of iodine for its production by the thyroid gland.

questions.question: Structures present in a cell which is responsible for determination of the sex of a baby is:
questions.explanation:
The sex of a baby is determined by the type of sex chromosomes present in the fertilized egg from which the baby develops. There are two types of sex chromosomes -X and Y.
A female has only two X-chromosomes, i.e. XX, whereas a male has one X and one Y-chromosome, i.e. XY combination.

questions.question: Adolescents should be careful about what they eat, because

options.option: taste buds are well developed in teenagers.
proper diet is needed for the rapid growth taking place in their body.

questions.explanation: Adolescents should be careful about what they eat, because proper diet is needed for the rapid growth taking place in their body.

questions.question: Reproductive age in women starts when their:
questions.explanation: Reproductive age in women starts when their menstruation starts.

questions.question: The biggest part of human brain is-
questions.explanation: The biggest part of the brain is the cerebrum. The cerebrum is the thinking part of the brain and it controls your voluntary muscles the ones that move when yon want them to.

questions.question: Deficiency of thyroxin in the body causes
questions.explanation: Deficiency of thyroxin in human body causes a disease called goitre. In this, the neck area {where thyroid gland is present) swells because of malfunctioning of thyroid gland due to iodine deficiency.

questions.question: The hormone produced by pancreas is
questions.explanation: The pancreas secretes insulin hormone which allows the body to regulate levels of sugar in the blood.

questions.question:
Which one of the following is correctly matched?

  1. Adrenaline - Growth hormone
  2. Insulin - Female hormone
  3. Oestrogen - Stress reliever hormone
  4. Testosterone - Male hormone
questions.explanation: Testosterone is a male sex hormone secreted by testis. It promotes secondary sexual characteristics such as increased muscle and body mass and the growth of body hair. Adrenaline hormone is a stress hormone or emergency hormone. Insulin is secreted by {tex}\beta{/tex} cells of islet of Langerhans of pancreas, and oestrogen is a female hormone secreted by graffian follicles on the ovaries.

questions.question: Testosterone in boys is produced from
questions.explanation: testes

questions.question: In a frog the process of metamorphosis is regulated by
questions.explanation: thyroxine

questions.question: In amphibians, metamorphosis is done by
questions.explanation: thyroid gland

questions.question: Sperms in male are produced in
questions.explanation: testes

questions.question: A hormone secreted in a female body only
questions.explanation: progesterone

questions.question: Which of the following is not a part of female reproductive system?
questions.explanation: embryo

questions.question: After fertilization the human embryo grows inside the
questions.explanation: uterus

questions.question: The fusion of male and female gametes usually takes place inside the
questions.explanation: fallopian tubes

subtopics.name: Role of Hormones in Completing the Life History of Insects and Frogs

questions.question: Once a girl starts menstruating, her body needs more
questions.explanation: iron

questions.question: One ovum is released in females once in every
questions.explanation: 28 days

questions.question: If fertilization doesn't occur, the ovum is released out from
questions.explanation: menstrual cycle

questions.question: The cell formed after fusion of egg and sperm is
questions.explanation: both fertilized egg & zygote

questions.question: If the sperm contribute Y to the ovum, the gender of the baby will be

options.option: It depends on the sex chromosome present in ova

questions.explanation: male

questions.question:
Figure given below shows the stages of development after fertilisation has taken place in the oviduct.

Which of the following represents P, Q and R?
questions.explanation: P - Zygote, Q - Embryo, R - Foetus

questions.question: The stage when the reproductive organs reach sexual maturity is called
questions.explanation: puberty

questions.question: Sexual maturity in males is obtained at the age of
Adam's apple is observed in
questions.explanation: boys

questions.question: Secretions of endocrine glands are called
questions.explanation: hormones

questions.question: When we are under stress or angry, the hormone which helps us to adjust, is
questions.explanation: adrenaline

questions.question: Pituitary gland secretes which hormone from ovaries in girls
questions.explanation: estrogen

questions.question: Under which condition does metamorphosis take place?

options.option: Females are twice the size of males.
Females are bright-coloured while males are dull.
Young ones eat the same food as adults.

questions.explanation: Young ones look different from adults.

questions.question: The process of release of ovum by the respective ovary is called as
questions.explanation: ovulation

questions.question: Which of the following is a male sex hormone?
questions.explanation: Testosterone

questions.question: Which of the following is the age group of an adolescent?
questions.explanation: 12 and 19 years

questions.question: By which age do the girls reach full physical development?
questions.explanation: 15-17 years

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an endocrine gland?
questions.explanation: Liver

questions.question: Which of the following is a female sex hormone?
questions.explanation: Both Progesterone and Oestrogen

questions.question: Which of the following is a hormone related disorders?
questions.explanation: Diabetes

questions.question: Which of the following disorder is caused by iodine deficiency?
questions.explanation: Goitre

questions.question: Which of the following endocrine glands is present near the kidneys?
questions.explanation: Adrenal

questions.question: Which microorganism causes AIDS?
questions.explanation: Virus

questions.question: Which of the following glands is known as the master gland?
questions.explanation: Pituitary

questions.question: A push or pull on an object is called
questions.explanation: A push or pull on an object is called force. Force can make stationary body in motion and moving body in rest.

questions.question: The motion imparted to objects is due to the
questions.explanation: The motion imparted to object is due to the action of force. If force acts on the object along the direction of motion, its speed increases. If force is applied on a direction opposite to the direction of motion, its speed decreases.

questions.question: S.I. unit of force is
questions.explanation: S.I. unit of force is Newton. When a force pushes a body of mass one kg to a distance of one meter per second, the force is equal to one Newton.

questions.question: In game of tug-of war, two teams
questions.explanation: In a game of tug-of war, two teams pull the rope in opposite direction. As the two teams are acting in opposite directions, they will balance each other and there is no net force acting on the rope. As soon as one team applies stronger force and pulls the rope toward itself, the team becomes the winner.

questions.question: Perpendicular force acting per unit area is called:
questions.explanation: The perpendicular force acting per unit area is called Thrust. S.I. unit of thrust is Newton.

questions.question: If the direction and magnitude of the applied force changes, its effect
questions.explanation: If the direction and magnitude of the applied force changes, its effect also changes. If two forces are acting in the same direction, they add up. If two forces are acting in opposite directions, the net force is the difference between the two.

questions.question: S.I. unit of pressure is
questions.explanation: S.I. unit of pressure is Pascal. One Pascal is equal to one Newton per meter square. Larger the force, larger will be the pressure exerted.

questions.question: A force applied on an object may change its
questions.explanation: A force applied on an object may change its speed. When force is applied in direction of motion speed will increase and if the force is applied in a direction, opposite to its motion, the speed decreases.

questions.question: Force applied on an object in same direction
questions.explanation: The force applied on an object in same direction as the object is moving add the speed of the object but when applied in opposite direction, its reduces.

questions.question: The strength of a force is usually expressed by its
questions.explanation: Force is a vector quantity as it contains both direction and magnitude but strength of a force is usually expressed by its magnitude. It is a numerical value with an appropriate unit. S.I unit of force is newton (N).

questions.question: The state of rest is considered to be the
