questions.explanation: The state of rest is considered to be a state of zero speed. When the position of a body with respect to its surroundings does not change with time, it is said to be at rest. There is no displacement at the time.

questions.question: Which change always occurs when force is applied
questions.explanation: Change in state of motion always occurs when force is applied on the body. Moving body may come to rest or resting body may come to motion.

questions.question: Which force enables us to perform all activities involving movement or bending of our body?
questions.explanation: Muscular force enables us to perform all activities involving movement or bending of our body. Muscular force is produced by the muscles in our body, due to the presence of contractile protein. It is contact force as our body has to directly or indirectly in touch with the object.

questions.question: A force which opposes the motion is called
questions.explanation: A force which opposes the motion is called friction. Friction always acts in the direction opposite to the direction of motion. This is the reason why moving objects slow down and stop.  Friction is an example of contact force.

questions.question: The frictional force exerted by fluids is also called
questions.explanation: The frictional force exerted by fluids is also called drag. Water and other liquids exert force of friction when objects move through them. The friction force exerted by fluids depends on its speed with respect to fluid and also on the shape of the object and the nature of the fluid.

questions.question: The threaded tyres of cars, trucks, and bus
questions.explanation: The threaded tyres of truck, cars and buses provide better grips with ground because threads in tyres creates more irregularity to the surface of the road and causes greater frictional force to act and form better grips.

questions.question: A body will remain in state of motion on rolling on ground if
questions.explanation: Frictional force always acts in opposite direction of motion to slow down or stopped the body from moving. A body will remain in state of motion on rolling on ground if friction is absent or the body will not move at all as friction is due air also.

questions.question: Brakes of vehicle is stopped by using brake that works on property of
questions.explanation: Wheels of vehicle is stopped by using brake that works on property of friction. We deliberately increase friction by using brake pads in the brake system and arrest the motion of the rim and finally the wheel, which helps in slowing and stopping the vehicle.

questions.question: The force required to overcome friction at the instant an object start moving from rest is measure of
questions.explanation: Static friction is the friction that exists between a stationary object and the surface on which it is resting. Static force should be greater than frictional force in magnitude.

questions.question: The force by which earth attracts the other body is called
questions.explanation: The force by which earth attracts the other body is called gravitational force. The earth pulls all objects towards itself with a force. Gravitational force depends upon mass of the body and distance from the center of earth. It is a non contact force as the force exerted by the earth can act from a distance.

questions.question: In atmosphere, wind blows due to
questions.explanation: In atmosphere, winds blows due to unequal heating of air at two places. The sun heats the earth's surface unevenly. As heat is transferred to the air, we get regions of warm and cool air which can turn into regions of low and high pressure. This difference in pressure makes a force that causes the wind to flow.

questions.question: One Newton is equal to
questions.explanation: One Newton is equal to 1 kg m/s2. One newton is equal to 1 kilogram meter per second squared.

questions.question: Kabaddi players rub their hands with soil for a
questions.explanation: Kabaddi players rub their hands with soil for a better grip of their opponents. Soil increase the roughness and creates more friction while catching the player firmly such that not to slip.

questions.question: Sliding is replaced in most machines by
questions.explanation: Ball bearings reduces friction of moving parts in a machine. So, sliding friction is replace by rolling friction. Ball bearings are spheres that are held in a track. They are used between a wheel and a fixed axle or between a wheel- axle and a vehicle shaft. They are used to provide better linear motion or rotation around a fixed axis.

questions.question: School bags have broader strips to
questions.explanation: School bags have broader strips to decrease pressure that is applied on the shoulder. The load remains the same but the area increases. Since pressure is inversely proportional to area, the greater the area, the lesser the pressure, and the pressure on the shoulder reduces in broad straps.

questions.question: Friction between any two objects is due to
questions.explanation: Friction between any objects is due to surface irregularities on them. Friction is the resistance to motion of one object moving relative to another. Friction arises due to strong atomic or molecular forces of attraction between the two surfaces at the points of contact. In rough surfaces, the actual area of contact is very small, so the pressure at the points of contact becomes very large. So rough surfaces have more friction than smooth or polished surfaces.

questions.question: One Pascal is equal to
questions.explanation: The S.I. unit of pressure is Pascal.One pascal is the pressure that results from the action of 1 N of force acting on a unit area.

questions.question: A motorcycle with worn out treads on tyres is more likely to skid due to
questions.explanation: A motorcycle with worn out threads on tyres is more likely to skid due to less friction on slippery road. Tyres have grooves to increase the friction, if these grooves are worn out the grip loses and the friction between the tyre and the surface of the road decreases. There will be less friction between the motorcycle and the road which is most likely to skid.

questions.question: A force of 5 N is applied on a body to create an acceleration of 2m/s. The mass of the body is equal to
questions.explanation: Here, force = 5 N and acceleration =2m/s . Force = Mass {tex}\times{/tex} Acceleration, Mass = Force/Acceleration. So, Mass = 5 N/2m/s = 2.5 Kg

questions.question: The weight of air in a column of the height of the atmosphere and area 10cm {tex}\times{/tex} 10 cm is equal to
questions.explanation: The weight of air in a column of the height of the atmosphere and area 10cm {tex}\times{/tex} 10cm is equal to 1000 kg. Atmospheric pressure is the weight exerted by the overhead atmosphere on a unit area of surface.

questions.question: The pressure inside our body is
questions.explanation: The pressure inside our body is equal to atmospheric pressure due to which we do not get crushed under the pressure of the atmosphere. The internal pressure developed by the fluids inside the body cells and the atmospheric pressure are equal. At high altitudes where the atmospheric pressure is less then the pressure inside of our body, we feel popping in the air.

questions.question: Pressure exerted by water at the bottom of the container depends on the
questions.explanation: The pressure exerted by water at the bottom of the container depends on the height of the column of water. Pressure of water increases with depth.

questions.question: A force of 200 N is applied to an object of area 2 sq. m. the pressure will be
questions.explanation:
Here, Force = 200N, Area = 2 sq m.
Pressure = Force/Area.
Pressure = 200N / 2  sq m =100 Pascal (Pa).

questions.question: Pressure is
questions.explanation: Pressure is inversely proportional to area of contact and directly proportional to force acting on per unit area. Increased area of contact results in applying bigger force which results in lower pressure and less area of contact results in less force which in turn results in high pressure.

questions.question: Two boys exert force of 40 N and 60 N in opposite direction on an object. The resultant force is
questions.explanation:
Since the two forces are acting in opposite directions, the net force is the difference between the two forces. The net force will act in the direction of the larger force.
Force exerted by the first boy = 40N
Force exerted by the second boy = 60N
The resultant force exerted by two boys on same object will be : Force exerted by the second boy (60N) - Force exerted by the first boy(40N) = 20N

questions.question: It is easy to walk on sand with flat shoes because
questions.explanation: It is easy to walk on the sand with flat shoes because flat shoes have a larger surface areas. As pressure is inversely proportional to area. So, a larger surface area will produce less pressure on sand and it will become easy to walk.

questions.question: Same force produces more pressure on
questions.explanation: Same force produces more pressure on smaller area as pressure is inversely proportional to area of contact. So, high pressure in less area of contact and less pressure in more area of contact. Example: Cutting instruments like knife and scissors have sharp edges or less area of contact.

questions.question: 1N/ sq m is equal to
questions.explanation:
The S.I. unit of pressure is Pascal. One Pascal is equal to the pressure that results from the action of one Newton of force on one meter square area.
So,1Pascal = 1 N / 1 sq m.

questions.question: The lateral pressure exerted by liquid at same height is
questions.explanation: The lateral pressure exerted by liquid at same height is equal in all direction and pressure increases with depth.

questions.question: A box weighing 2 kg exerts a force of 20 N on the ground. The box covers an area of 2 sq. m on the ground. The pressure exerted by the box on the ground is
questions.explanation:
Here, Force exerted by the box on ground = 20N
Area of the ground the box covers = 2 sq m
Since, Pressure = Force/ Area.
Pressure exerted by the box on the ground = 20N / 2 sq m = 10Pa.

questions.question: Liquid pressure increases with
questions.explanation: Pressure exerted by liquid increases with an increase in depth. As the depth of liquid increases, the weight of liquid column pushing down from above increases, and hence the pressure also increases. To protect this pressure, sea divers uses a special suit.

questions.question: At high altitudes atmospheric pressure
questions.explanation: At high altitudes the atmospheric pressure decreases, as density of air decrease as we move upward. Due to the difference in the atmospheric pressure and the internal pressure developed by the fluids inside the body cells  sometimes popping of ears occurs at high altitudes.

questions.question: Which of the following effect is not produced by force?
questions.explanation: Force can change the state of motion, the shape, and size of the object, and change the state of motion but can never change the mass of the body. Force is the product of mass and acceleration.

questions.question: The force depends on
questions.explanation: Force depends upon mass and acceleration of the body. Actually force acting on a body is directly proportional to product of mass and weight. An object with a larger mass needs a stronger force to be moved along at the same acceleration as an object with a small mass.

questions.question: Rate of change in velocity is called
questions.explanation: The rate of change in velocity is called acceleration. Acceleration is a vector quantity and has both magnitude and direction. An object's acceleration is the net result of all forces acting on the object.

questions.question: The force required to move a stationary body is called
questions.explanation: Forces that cause a change in the motion of an object are called unbalanced forces. Unbalanced forces are not equal and opposite. Example: In a tug of war the team that pulls harder than the other team wins. This unbalanced force is required to overcome the gravitational force acting on the body.

questions.question: If the force applied on a moving body in direction of motion than
questions.explanation: If the force applied on a moving body is in the same direction of motion than the speed of the body increases. As both the forces are acting in the same direction, they add up to make a bigger force. This large force makes the speed increases. And if force acts in the opposite direction, speed decreases.

questions.question: When batsman hits the ball, it changes its
questions.explanation: When a batsman hits the ball, it changes the direction of ball as well as speed of the ball. Due to the force applied by the bat along the direction of motion the speed of the ball increases.

questions.question: How much force is applied on a body of mass 10 kg to increase its acceleration by 2m/s sq?
questions.explanation:
Here, Mass of the body = 10kg
Acceleration = 2m/s sq
Force= Mass {tex}\times{/tex} Acceleration =10kg {tex}\times{/tex} 2m/s sq = 20N.

questions.question: A body moving on leveled grounds need to apply constant force to
questions.explanation: Frictional force always works in opposite direction of motion. A body moving on leveled grounds need to apply constant force to overcome frictional force provided by ground.

questions.question: The direction in which swimmer applies the force to move forward is
questions.explanation: The swimmer applies backward direction to move forward. For every action there is an equal and opposite reaction, so when a person pushes the water backwards the water pushes him forward.

questions.question: When action and reactions forces are equal and opposite, They don't cancel

options.option: Because they are in state of rest
Because they are acting on two different bodies

questions.explanation: Action and reaction forces are equal and opposite to each other but don’t cancel each other because they are acting by two different bodies. Newton's Third Law of motion states that for every action force, there is an equal and opposite reaction force.

questions.question: A force produces an acceleration of 5m/s when applied on a body of mass 4kg. The magnitude of force applied is
questions.explanation:
Here, Acceleration = 5m/s sq 
Mass of the body = 4kg.
Force = Mass {tex}\times{/tex} Acceleration = 4kg {tex}\times{/tex} 5m/s sq = 20N.

questions.question: Which force is responsible for the stability of our universe?
questions.explanation: The force of gravitation is responsible for the stability of our universe. Gravitational force acts on each and every object which attracts the bodies towards itself. The planets revolve around the sun on their own axis is because of the gravitational force of the sun. Gravitational force depends on the mass and the distance of the object.

questions.question: Truck or motor-bus has much wider tyres to
questions.explanation: Truck or motor-bus has much wider tyres to decrease the pressure on road because pressure is inversely proportional to area of contact,  and this increases the area of contact and reduces pressure.

questions.question: A perpendicular force of 50 N is acting on a circular plate of radius 7cm. The pressure acting on plate will be
questions.explanation:
Here, Force = 50 N
Radius of circular plate = 7cm
Area of a circle = {tex}\pi{/tex} r sq= 3.14 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 = 154 cm sq = 154/10000 m sq = 0.0154 m sq.
Pressure = Force/Area = 50N/0.0154 m sq = 3246.75 Pa

questions.question: At higher altitude, nose bleeding is a common problem because

options.option: Atmospheric pressure is more at higher altitude
Atmospheric pressure is less at higher altitude

questions.explanation: Nose bleeding occurs at high altitudes is due to the difference in the atmospheric pressure and internal pressure developed by the fluids inside the body cells. At higher altitudes, atmospheric pressure is less, and pressure inside our body remains the same. The blood pressure in our body equal is to atmospheric pressure on earth.

questions.question: Muscular force: contact force :: magnetic force : ?
questions.explanation: Muscular force is example of contact force because the interacting bodies are in contact to each other. Magnetic force is an example of non-contact force as the interacting bodies need not be in contact with each other.

questions.question: A boy having area of 70 cm sq. exerts a pressure of 7N/cm sq. on the ground. The Force acting on the ground will be
questions.explanation:
Here, Pressure exerted by the boy on ground = 7N/cm sq
Area on the boy occupied = 70 cm sq
As, Pressure = Force/ Area
The force acting on the ground = Pressure {tex}\times{/tex} Area
= 7 N/ cm sq {tex}\times{/tex} 70 cm sq
= 490 N

questions.question: During dry weather, while combing hair, sometimes we experience hair flying apart. The force responsible for this is
questions.explanation: During dry weather, while combing hair, sometimes we experience hair flying apart. The force responsible for this is electrostatic force. The force exerted by a charged body on another charged or uncharged body is known as electrostatic force.

questions.question: Two objects repel each other. This repulsion could be due to

options.option: either a magnetic or an electrostatic force

questions.explanation: When two objects repel each other, this repulsion could be due to either magnetic or electrostatic force. In electrostatic force, similar charges may repel each other. In a magnetic force, similar poles may repel each other.

questions.question: Which one of the following forces is a contact force?
questions.explanation:
When the applied force is in contact with an object, then this is called contact force. Among the given options, the force of friction is a contact force because the force of friction arises due to contact between surfaces.
Magnetic force, the force of gravity, and electrostatic force are non-contact forces.

questions.explanation: Dyne

questions.explanation: contact force

questions.explanation: 1 kg

questions.explanation: mass

questions.explanation: muscular

questions.explanation: the head on the suitcase

water increases the friction between the road and the tyres

questions.explanation: same weight

questions.explanation: 10 N/m2

the water exerts low pressure on bottom wall

questions.explanation: 1.01 {tex}\times{/tex} 105 N/m2

questions.question: While walking on the ground we apply force on
questions.explanation: According to Newton's third law; every action has an equal and opposite reaction. This is relevant to walking because when you put your foot on the ground, you are applying a force to it. In doing this, the ground also actually applies an equal force onto your foot, in the opposite direction, pushing you forward.

questions.question: In preparing a chapatti from dough ________ force is applied.
questions.explanation: Muscular force is applied to change the shape of a ball of dough to a chapatti. This force leads to the change in shape and size of chapatti.

questions.question:
Pressure is-

  1. Scalar Quantity
  2. Vector Quantity
  3. Both Scalar and Vector Quantity
  4. Tensor Quantity
questions.explanation: Pressure is a scalar quantity, not a vector quantity. It has a magnitude but no direction associated with it. Pressure acts in all directions at a point inside a gas. At the surface of a gas, the pressure force acts perpendicular to the surface.

questions.explanation: 1 calorie = 4.18 joule

subtopics.name: Force – A Push or a Pull

questions.question: On which principle rocket works?
questions.explanation: The rocket works because of the law of conservation of linear momentum. The law of conservation of linear momentum is very important in physics. Momentum is defined as the mass of an object times its velocity.

questions.question: Two forces each of 20 N act simultaneously on a body, the maximum net force and minimum net force are
questions.explanation:
When two forces act in the same direction and parallel to each other; then the net force is
Fnet = 20N + 20 N = 40 N

When two forces act in the opposite direction as shown in the figure.

Here, F1 = 20 N
F2 = - 20N (opposite direction)
= 20 - 20 = 0

questions.question: A ball is moving on a floor (surface) along a straight path with a uniform speed State the conclusion you can draw from this statement

options.option: The force acting on the bail is zero.
A non-contact force is acting on the hall against frictional force.
No gravitational force is acting on the bail.
No frictional force acts on the rolling objects.

questions.explanation: A non-contact force is acting on the ball against frictional force due to which ball is moving on a floor along a straight path with a uniform speed.

questions.question: 1 horsepower is equal to-
questions.explanation: The electrical equivalent of one horsepower is 746 watts in the International System of Units (SI), and the heat equivalent is 2,545 BTU (British Thermal Units) per hour. Another unit of power is the metric horsepower, which equals 4,500 kilogram-metres per minute (32,549 foot-pounds per minute) or 0.9863 horsepower.

questions.question: Which one is not the unit of pressure?
questions.explanation:
The SI unit of pressure is pascal. The pressure is given by
{tex}p=\frac{\text { Force }}{\text { Area }}=\frac{\mathrm{N}}{\mathrm{m}^2}{/tex}
In C.G.S system the unit of pressure is dyne/cm2.
Thus, N-m-1 is not a unit of pressure.

subtopics.name: Forces are due to an Interaction

questions.question: The drops of rain are circular due to-
questions.explanation: Raindrops take up the spherical shape due to the surface tension of water which is caused due to the tendency of water molecules to stick together. The spherical shape is having the least possible surface area due to which it can resist any of the external force in the atmosphere.

questions.question: The food can be cooked easily in pressure cooker, because-
questions.explanation: Food is cooked more quickly in a pressure cooker because at the higher pressure (1 bar/15 psi), the boiling point of water rises from 100 °C (212 °F) to 121 °C (250 °F). The hotter steam is able to transmit its thermal energy o the food at around 4 times the rate of conventional boiling.

questions.question: A man is standing on a weighing machine in a lift. The reading of machine will be minimum when machine will move
questions.explanation: A man is standing on a weighing machine placed in a lift. The reading of machine will be minimum when machine will move when lift falls freely downward.

questions.question: In which of the positions given below, any person creates maximum pressure on earth?
questions.explanation: Any person standing on one leg creates maximum pressure on earth.

questions.question: A rectangular block on the top of a table is kept in such a manner that its various sides touch the table. In different situations, this block imposes-
questions.explanation: A rectangular block has 6 sides with 4 larger sides and 2 smaller sides as their length is longer than the breadth.

questions.question: The value of g at the centre of earth becomes
questions.explanation: The value ‘g’ at the centre of earth becomes zero.

questions.question: A gas filled balloon moves up because ________

options.option: gravity force does not act on the ballon
upward force on the balloon is more than frictional force
there is no frictional force on the balloon
upward force on the balloon is more than gravitational force

questions.explanation: A gas filled balloon moves up because upward force on the balloon is more than gravitational force, only then the balloon will be able to go up against this force.

questions.question: Why the food is cooked quickly in pressure cooker?
questions.explanation: The pressure cooker traps that hot air and moisture with the food, which expedites the cooking process. In other words, the moisture surrounding the food itself reaches higher temperatures than it would without the pressure, which speeds up the chemical processes involved in cooking.

questions.question: An ice cube is floating in a glass filled up to its upper point. When ice melts, then-

options.option: The level of water will remain same

questions.explanation: According to the Archimedes principle, the floating substance displaces some liquid, so when the ice melts, there will be no change in the water level as the melted ice will occupy the same volume as it was occupying earlier.

questions.question: When a rubber ball bounces back from the surface, the effect of forces is to

options.option: Change the direction of motion and change the shape of body
None of thesehe surface, the effect of forces is to

questions.explanation: When a rubber ball falls on a hard surface, the curved surface of ball in contact with surface becomes flat momentarily and direction of its motion is reversed simultaneously.

questions.question: Upward push which is acting on a body which is partially or totally immersed in a liquid or gas is called:

questions.explanation: None of these

It will stop moving immediately when force is applied
It will decelerate, stop and then move in the opposite direction with a constant speed

questions.explanation: It will decelerate, stop and then move in the opposite direction with a constant acceleration

In the backward direction on the front wheel and in the forward direction on the rear wheel
In the backward direction on the rear wheel and in the forward direction on the front wheel
In the forward direction on both the wheels

questions.explanation: Same as v

  2. Is zero if the car is moving with a uniform velocity

Start reading when the speed reaches 15 km/hr

Friction is acting on the box in the direction opposite to the box's movement

options.option: Gravitational force is increased in upward going lift
The mass of our body is increased.

An unbalanced force is created in upward direction.

questions.question: If the force on the surface is doubled and area of contact is reduced to half, pressure will
questions.explanation:
Pressure, P {tex}=\frac{F}{A}{/tex}
When force is doubled and area is reduced to half, we get
{tex}P=\frac{2 F}{\frac{A }{2}}=4\left(\frac{F}{A}\right){/tex}
Pressure will become four times.

questions.explanation: pressure

questions.explanation: Since the body is at rest; balanced force acts on it. The balanced forces are (i) its weight (ii) the normal reaction of the ground.

The pressure of the gas is equal to the pressure on the same level in the other limb.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Total pressure of the gas, P = atmospheric pressure + pressure due to mercury column
= (760 + 150 mm) of Hg = 910 mm of Hg

questions.question: An object of weight W is pulled along a rough horizontal surface by a force F. The force of friction is Fr. Which diagram correctly shows the direction of these forces on the object?
questions.explanation: The weight W acts vertically downward and force Fr opposite to applied force F.

questions.explanation: atmosphere

questions.question: A manometer is connected to a gas supply. Pressure can be measured in cm of water. The pressure of the gas is

options.option: 12 cm of water less than the atmospheric pressure.
8 cm of water less than the atmospheric pressure
12 cm of water more than the atmospheric pressure

questions.explanation: Due to increased pressure of gas supply, it presses the water level in the other limb of manometer to go higher. The excess pressure is equal to difference of water level in the two limb of U-tube.

questions.explanation: Frictional force on the ball opposing its motion.

  3. Atmospheric pressure increases as height above sea level increases.
  4. Upthrust on a floating block in a liquid is equal to the weight of liquid displaced by the block.
questions.explanation: : Atmospheric pressure decreases at higher altitudes.

questions.question: An object is accelerating under the influence of a force F1 on a frictionless surface. A while later, an opposing force F2 of the same magnitude acts on it as shown in the figure. What will happen to the object?

options.option: The object will come to rest immediately after the opposing force acts on it.
The object will move at constant velocity.
The object will move in the opposite direction.

questions.explanation: Two forces F1 and F2 are equal and opposite so net force becomes zero. Hence object will keep on moving with constant velocity and its acceleration becomes zero.

questions.question: Two boys A and B are applying force on a block. If the block moves towards the right, which one of the following statements is CORRECT?

options.option: Net force on the block is towards A
Magnitude of force applied by A is lesser than that of B
Magnitude of force applied by A is greater than that of B
Magnitude of force applied by A is equal to that of B.

questions.explanation: Magnitude of force applied by A is greater than that of B

an increase in the effect of the force exerted on them.
a decrease in the effect of the force exerted on them.
an Increase in the force exerted on them.

questions.explanation: a decrease in the effect of the force exerted on them.

Function Effect of force

(ii) A batsman hitting a boundary (b) Changing shape of object
(iii) A stone being rotated on a circular path uniformly (c)Changing direction of motion only
questions.explanation:
Squeezing a piece of lemon to extract juicechanging shape.
A batsman hitting a boundary-changing direction and magnitude of motion.
A stone being rotated on a circular path uniformly (with constant speed) - changing direction of motion only as the magnitude (speed) remains constant.

questions.question: In a circular path, direction of motion is changed by force which acts

options.option: Towards the tangent on the circular path passing through object.
Towards the centre on the circular path passing through object.
Away from the centre of circular path along the line joining centre and the object.

questions.explanation: In a circular path, direction of motion is changed by force which acts towards centre.

questions.question: Which instrument is used for measuring air pressure?
questions.explanation: Barometer

questions.question: For a force to change the direction of motion of the object only and not to change the magnitude of its velocity, its line of action must be
questions.explanation: When a force acts perpendicular to direction of motion of an object, only the direction of motion is changed. The magnitude of velocity remains unaffected, as in case of circular motion.

questions.question:
Identify the correct statements.

  1. The speed of an object is zero in the state of rest.
  2. A change in the state of motion of an object is described by its speed only.
  3. A change in the state of motion of an object is described either by its speed, or by the direction of motion, or both.
questions.explanation: Both direction and magnitude of velocity of an object change the state of body.

questions.question: Satellites revolve around the earth with the help of
questions.explanation: It is the gravitational force between the satellite and Earth which holds the satellite in its orbit around the Earth.

questions.question: To stretch the bow, an archer applies ________.
questions.explanation: The force is exerted by the muscles of arm to stretch the bow. An archer applies contact force i.e.,. muscular force to stretch bow.

questions.question: Magnetic force is ___________ .
questions.explanation: non-contact force

questions.question: A stone is thrown up in air. The two forces which come into effect immediately after the throw is
questions.explanation: When a stone is thrown up in air, gravitational pull of the earth reduces its speed. The frictional force by air also comes into effect as the stone goes up.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements is/are correct?

  1. Gravitational and frictional are non-contact forces.
  2. Friction is contact force and magnetic force is non-contact force.
  3. Muscular is contact force and gravitational force is non-contact force.
  4. Nuclear and gravitational are non-contact forces.

options.option: Statement II, III and IV are correct.
Statement I, II and III are correct
Statement I, III and IV are correct.
Statement I, II and IV are correct.

questions.explanation: Frictional force and muscular force are the contact force while magnetic force and nuclear force, gravitational force are non-contact forces.

questions.question: Magnetic needle always aligns itself in north south direction due to ________.
questions.explanation: Magnetic force of earth’s magnetic field, forces a magnet to remain aligned in North-South direction.

questions.question: The unit of force is ________
questions.explanation: all of these

questions.question:
I, II and III are three containers containing water upto different heights.

Arrange the pressure at the bottom of them in decreasing order.
questions.explanation: Pressure in liquid is directly proportional to height of liquid column.

questions.question: A body is said to be under balanced force when the resultant force applied on the body is ___________ .
questions.explanation: Zero

questions.question:
Which of the following represent correct values for the normal atmospheric pressure?

  1. 101.3 kilopascals
  2. 76 mm of mercury
  3. 101.3 pascals
  4. 76 cm of mercury
questions.explanation:
1 atmospheric pressure = 76 cm of mercury
= 101.3 kPa

questions.question: Through a pipe, water is being sucked through mouth. Which of the following statements is correct?

options.option: Pressure at P is more than that at Q.
Pressure at Q is more than atmospheric pressure.
Pressure at P and Q is equal.
Pressure at Q is more than that at P.

questions.explanation: When air is sucked through mouth, region around Q will have partial vacuum (less pressure) and at P, pressure will be equal to atmospheric pressure.

questions.question: In the figure, find the pressure of gas in the vessel V.

options.option: Gas pressure is more than atmospheric pressure.
There is no relation between gas pressure and atmospheric pressure.
Gas pressure is equal to atmospheric pressure.
Gas pressure is less than atmospheric pressure.

questions.explanation: Due to greater pressure gas water level at P is less than at Q

questions.question:
Match column I with column II and select the correct option from given codes.

Column I Column II
(i) Applying brakes on a moving car. (a) Change in direction of motion
(ii) Stretching a spring. (b) Change in magnitude of motion (speed)
(iii) Firing a spring gun. (c) Change in shape of object
(iv) Hitting a cricket shot.
When your friend pushes your bike from behind, what will happen to the speed of the bike?
questions.explanation: Speed will increase

questions.question:
Match column I with column II and select the correct option from given codes.

Column I Column II
(i) Small grain pieces fall down while sieving grains (a) Magnetic force
(ii) A gas filled balloon moving up in air. (b) Electrostatic force
(iii) During dry weather we see hair flying apart while combing it. (c) Gravitational force
(iv) The doorbell chimes when we press the button. (d)Buoyant force
(v) Meteor burns in air after it enters atmosphere. (e)Frictional force
(vi) During dry weather, synthetic clothes often stick to our skin.

Match column I with column II and select the correct option from given codes.

Column I Column II
(i) Non contact Force (a) Opposes motion between two surfaces
(ii) Frictional force (b) Magnetic force
(iii) Pressure (c) A man falling down with parachute
(iv) Floatation (d) Pascal
(v) Unbalanced force (e) Buoyant force
questions.explanation: A man with parachute falling down experiences unbalanced force in downward direction.

questions.question:
Choose the correct option.
I. A blunt knife cuts with difficulty because of increased pressure it creates.
II. On increasing the level of height, atmospheric pressure decreases.
questions.explanation: A blunt knife creates relatively less pressure due to increased cutting edge area. As we go up in the atmosphere from the surface of earth, the atmosphere pressure goes on decreasing.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements are correct?

  1. Pressure in liquids and gases always act downwards.
  2. We start bleeding on reaching higher altitudes due to decreased atmospheric pressure there.
  3. Camels have broad feet to decrease pressure on sand.
  4. Force is equal to pressure multiplied by area.
questions.explanation: II, III and IV only

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, find the points where pressure is maximum and minimum respectively.
questions.explanation: Pressure exerted by liquids increases with the depth of the liquid.

questions.question:
Select the incorrect statements.

  1. When we stand up, we exert less pressure on the ground than the pressure we exert in case we squate.
  2. The length of mercury column that the atmospheric pressure can balance is 76 cm.
  3. Manometer measures the atmospheric pressure.
questions.explanation: When we stand, greater pressure is created on ground due to less surface occupied by our feet. Manometer measures pressure of a gas in a vessel.

questions.question: Pressure at a point inside a liquid does not depend on

options.option: The acceleration due to gravity at that point
The depth of the point below the surface of the liquid

questions.explanation: Pressure at a point inside a liquid does not depend on the shape of the containing vessel.

questions.question: Which of the following does not work on the existence of atmospheric pressure?

options.option: Rise of mercury in glass tube of thermometer
Sticking of suction hook on the wall of a room
Rise of iodine solution in the glass tube of dropper
Rise of cold drink in a long plastic straw

questions.explanation: Rise in tem perature of mercury causes its expansion in the thermometer.

questions.question: Two pieces of metal when immersed in a liquid have equal upthrust on them; then

options.option: Both are floating to the same depth

questions.explanation:
The upthurst acting on an object is equal to the weight of the liquid displaced i.e. product of the volume of the object, acceleration due to gravity and the density of the liquid.
Therefore, for the same upthrust, the volume of the two should be same.

questions.question: Which force/s helps/help us walk?
questions.explanation: Friction prevents slippage on the ground and thus helps us in walking.

subtopics.name: Pressure Exerted by Liquids and Gases

questions.question: Which of these is an example of a contact force?

options.option: A magnet attracting a hanging iron nail.
A plastic comb attracting small pieces of hair.
A boat moving on water by the action of wind on its sail.

questions.explanation: A boat moving on water by the action of wind on its sail.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a pushing force?
questions.explanation: Lifting

questions.question: Four forces are acting on a body. If the body doesn't change its position or shape, the forces

options.option: must add up to zero when taken as vectors.

questions.explanation: must add up to zero when taken as vectors.

questions.question: In which of the following case the property of force is applied that 'force can change the direction of an object?

options.option: A girl whirling a stone in a vertical circle using a string

questions.explanation: All of these

questions.question: What force keeps the spider web together?
questions.explanation: Tension force

questions.question: Gravitational force acts

options.option: only between the Sun and the planets moving around it.
only between the Sun and the Earth.
only between the Earth and the bodies on it.

questions.explanation: between all bodies in the universe.

questions.question: What will be the gravitational force on an object having mass 20 kg?
Which of these is not a contact force?
questions.explanation: Magnetic force

questions.question: Which of the following class of forces is different from others?
questions.explanation: Stretching of a spring

questions.question: A block has dimensions 5 cm {tex}\times{/tex} 20 cm {tex}\times{/tex} 10 cm. It has a mass of 2 kg. The ratio of minimum to maximum pressure it exerts on the change in orientation is
A rectangular iron block is kept over a table with different faces touching the table. In different cases, the block exerts
questions.explanation: same thrust and different pressure

questions.question: An iron block of sides 5 cm {tex}\times{/tex} 8 cm {tex}\times{/tex} 15 cm has to be pushed along the floor. The force required will be minimum when the surface in contact with ground is
questions.explanation: Force is same for all surfaces

questions.question: The size of an air bubble rising up in water
questions.explanation: increases

questions.question: Pressure in liquids depend on

options.option: height of liquid column above the point where you measure the pressure

questions.explanation: height of liquid column above the point where you measure the pressure

questions.question: Pressure is also measured in
questions.explanation: mm of Hg

questions.question: As we go deeper beneath the surface of liquid, the pressure
questions.explanation: increases

questions.question: Atmospheric pressure is measured by
questions.explanation: Barometer

questions.question: A manometer is used to measure
questions.explanation: pressure

questions.question: How does pressure vary as we come down from mountain top to sea level?
questions.explanation: Increases

questions.question: Which of the following is an expression for the pressure?
questions.explanation: Force per unit area

questions.question: What is the unit of measurement of blood pressure?
questions.explanation: mmHg

questions.question: What is the upward force applied by a fluid?
questions.explanation: Buoyancy

questions.question: Which of the following forces can act only on materials such as iron and nickel?
questions.explanation: Magnetic

questions.question: How does the blood reaches to the extremities of the body?
questions.explanation: High pressure in arteries

questions.question: What is the SI unit of force?
questions.explanation: Newton

questions.question: What is the SI unit of pressure?
questions.explanation: Pascal

questions.question: Which of the following properties does a force have?
questions.explanation: Magnitude, direction, and affect the speed

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of contact forces?
questions.explanation: Both muscular and frictional

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a non-contact force?
questions.explanation: Muscular

questions.question: What kind of substances are known as lubricants
questions.explanation: Decrease friction

questions.question: Friction produces
questions.explanation: Friction produces heat. When two surfaces in contact rub each other produces friction resulting in the production of heat. All machines get warm due to this.

questions.question: Sprinkling of powder on the carom board
questions.explanation: Sliding friction is less than static friction. Powder fill the rough surface of carrom board due to which friction between the carrom coins and the board reduces and coins slides easily.

questions.question: Fluid friction can be minimized by
questions.explanation: Liquid and gases are called fluid. Friction in fluid can be minimized by giving suitable shape called streamline shape. As streamlined shape is narrow at the ends and broader in the middle due to which it reduces the fluid friction and helps an object to move forward easily.

questions.question: The heat in metals is conducted by the process of
questions.explanation: The process of heat transfer in which particles transfer heat one after the other in contact is called conduction. The heat in metals is conducted by the process of conduction because molecules in solid are tightly packed. they just transfer their energy to adjacent molecule without leaving their place.

questions.question: In machine, friction between moving part is reduced by
questions.explanation: In machine, friction between moving part is reduced by using lubricants. These lubricants fill the irregularities of the surface and make it smoother.

questions.question: A force which opposes the motion is called
questions.explanation: A force that opposes the motion is called friction. It generates when two surfaces are in contact with each other. Friction depends upon the type of surface whether smooth or rough. Rough surface offers more friction.

questions.question: Rolling friction is smaller than?
questions.explanation: All of these

questions.question: The frictional force exerted by fluids is also called
questions.explanation: The frictional force exerted by fluids is also called as drag. This kinds of frictional force can be overcome by changing the shape of the moving body in fluids. Such shape is called streamlined shape

questions.question: Striking a matchstick produces fire by
questions.explanation: When a matchstick is rubbed on match box fire is produced due to friction. Matchstick contain phosphorous that catch fire easily at very low temperature.

questions.question: The substance which reduce friction are called
questions.explanation: The substances which reduce friction are called lubricants. Lubricants include oils, paints, and grease. Lubricant stick with the surface and fill the irregularities of it to reduce friction.

questions.question: When we attempt to move any surface, we apply a force to overcome
questions.explanation: When we attempt to move any surface, we apply a force to overcome interlocking between the surfaces called static friction present due to irregularities on the surface.

questions.question: The sole of shoes becomes flat after using few months because of
questions.explanation: The sole of shoes becomes flat after using few months because of wearing due to friction. There is friction between road and sole of shoe constantly which leads to flatness of the shoe sole with time.

questions.question: Which shape will have minimum friction in a fluid?
questions.explanation: The objects with pointed tips will have minimum friction in a fluid. The pointed tips offer less resistance due to less area of contact.

questions.question: Frictional force is necessary in
questions.explanation: Frictional force is necessary in using matchstick. When matchstick is strike with matchbox friction is caused due to which heat is generated. The phosphorous in matchstick catch fire.

questions.question: Fluids are
questions.explanation: The liquid and gases together called fluids. They have nature to flow due to less intermolecular force of attraction bteween their molecules.

questions.question: What does air resistance do to falling objects?
questions.explanation: Air resistance slows down the falling object as it increase with the increase in surface area of the object and acts against the gravitational force due to which it slows down the speed of object to fall down

questions.question: Which of the following objects have low friction?
questions.explanation: Car tyres, ice skates, and wicketkeeper gloves have more friction as compared to goalkeeper gloves as more friction is not required in this case.

questions.question: Friction between any two objects is due to
questions.explanation: The friction between any two objects is due to surface irregularities on them. These irregularities creates interlocking of the surfaces in contact to create friction.

questions.question: Carpenters often grease screws before fixing them to
questions.explanation: Carpenters while fixing screws, grease the tips of screw to decrease the friction. Grease present on screw smooth the irregularities hence reduce the friction offered by wood articles.

questions.question: A body is rolling over the ground or sliding over the same ground. In which case, the friction will be more?

options.option: There is no friction in both case

questions.explanation:
Sliding friction is always more than rolling friction because during sliding of a body more surface area is in contact not in case of rolling 
so, a body rolling over the ground will offer less friction than body sliding over the same ground.

questions.question: Streamlining is
questions.explanation: Streamlining is making the tips thin. Streamlining shape reduce the friction in fluids like air and water as it has narrow tips which reduces the area of contact

questions.question: Ball bearing is used in cycles because

options.option: Rolling friction is more than sliding friction
Rolling friction is less than sliding friction

questions.explanation: Ball bearing is used in cycles because rolling friction is less than sliding friction. Hence, most of the moving parts of machine have ball bearing to reduce friction.

questions.question: The SI unit of frictional force is
questions.explanation: The S.I. unit of force is Newton. All kinds of force are measured in terms of Newton which is equal to force that push a body of mass 1kg to a distance of 1meter.

questions.question: A car with worn out treads on tyres is more likely to skid due to
questions.explanation: A car with worn out treads on tyres is more likely to skid due to less friction. The treads in tyres produce more friction that prevents the vehicle from skidding by providing more grip on road.

questions.question: It is difficult to move a cycle with brakes on because
questions.explanation: It is difficult to move a cycle with brakes on because friction between wheels and road surface increases as both the surfaces interlock each other.

questions.question: Oils and lubricants help in reducing friction because they
questions.explanation: Oils and lubricants help in reducing friction because the lubricants fill the irregularities in the surface of moving bodies and hence reduce friction.

questions.question: Striker moves longer distance on carom when
questions.explanation: The striker of carom moves longer distance with same force when powder is sprinkled on board. The minute particles of powder fills the irregularities on surface that reduce the friction and striker easily cover more distance.

questions.question: Which of the following method is not used to reduce friction?
questions.explanation: Using ball bearing, lubricating and greasing reduce the friction between two surfaces while treading the tyres of vehicles increase the friction that is necessary for motion as it increases the contact area which increases the friction.

questions.question: The force of friction which comes into play when one body moves over the surface of other body is called
questions.explanation: Kinetic friction is the force of friction which comes into play when one body moves over the surface of other body.

questions.question: Rolling friction and sliding friction are two types of
questions.explanation: The friction develops when one body moves over the other body is called kinetic friction. Rolling friction and sliding friction are two types of kinetic friction.

questions.question: When the friction is more

options.option: One body is rough and other is smooth

questions.explanation: The friction is more when two bodies in contact are rough. Rough surface have more irregularities that interlock the surfaces and leads to more friction.

questions.question: The frictional force that counter balance the applied force during stationary state is called
questions.explanation: The movement in a stationary body is possible only when the applied force is greater than the frictional force. The frictional force that counter balances the applied force is called static friction. In static friction both the surfaces interlock each other strongly.

questions.question: In which of the substance friction is less
questions.explanation: Gases offers less friction than liquid and solid because in air there in more intercellular space. Hence, air will offer less friction in comparison to kerosene, water, and ice.

questions.question: Friction offered by water is good for the
questions.explanation: The friction offered by water is good for the movement of ships and boat in water. In ships and boat applied force is directed in backward direction and due to opposite nature of frictional force ships or boat moves in forward direction.

questions.question: It is difficult to apply brakes to stop the moving ship because

options.option: Friction exerted by water is very small
There is no friction between solid and liquid
Friction exerted by water is very high

questions.explanation: It is difficult to apply brakes to stop the moving ship because friction exerted by water is very small to stop the moving ship having very high momentum.

questions.question: Friction of air can be observed if a falling object is
questions.explanation: Friction of air can be observed if a falling object is light. When a tiny paper or feather falls from height, it takes long duration due to friction of air on it.

questions.question: Which of the following living organisms have streamlined body?
questions.explanation: Fish and birds have streamlined body to reduce the friction offered by water and air respectively. They have pointed tips and broader middle portion that help in reducing friction.

questions.question: Which of the following method is applied in moving parts of machine to reduce friction?
questions.explanation: The moving parts of machine are fitted with ball bearing to convert sliding friction into rolling friction. As rolling friction is always less than sliding friction due to less area in contact..

questions.question: To walk on slippery ground is difficult because the

options.option: Frictional force is very high to walk
Frictional force is not enough to prevent slip
Ground offer very high resistance to walk
Habit of walking on slippery ground is not enough to us

questions.explanation: Slippery ground have very less friction to prevent slipping so, it is difficult to walk on such grounds. As it does not allow two surfaces to be in contact with each other properly.

questions.question: Which of the following method is applied to increase friction of vehicles?
questions.explanation: Friction is necessary for movement of vehicle on road. To increase friction treading of tyres is done. Regular oiling of engine, cleaning of vehicle, and less inflating of the tyres reduce friction.

questions.question: The shoes of football player have spikes in their sole to
questions.explanation: The shoes of football players have spikes in their sole to increase friction with ground while running to prevent slipping.

questions.question: Sandpaper is used to clean the metal surface to remove rust as because

options.option: Sand paper has rough surface to decrease friction
Sand paper has smooth surface to increase friction
Sandpaper has rough surface to increase friction

questions.explanation: Sand papers are used to clean the metal surface to remove rust because sandpapers has a rough surface to increase the friction to removes the rust.

questions.question: In moving machine, friction
questions.explanation: In moving machine, friction decreases the efficiency of machine. Friction in machine causes its heating due to which it smooth the surfaces causing damage to machine.

questions.question: Friction involves
questions.explanation: Friction involves unnecessary expenses of energy because due to friction heat is generated which leads to more consumption of energy or fuel.

questions.question: Sonic boom is due to
questions.explanation: When a body moves with speed greater than speed of sound craking sound is produced in air called sonic boom. Sonic boom is due to friction between air and supersonic objects.

questions.question: Moving ball slowly decrease its speed in a grassy field due to
questions.explanation: Moving ball slows down its speed in a grassy field due to friction between ball and grass. Rough surface offers more friction than smooth surface which slows down the speed of a moving ball.

questions.question: Friction is a
questions.explanation: Friction is a contact force acting in opposite direction of moving body. Friction is created when one body moves over the surface of another body in contact to each other.

questions.question: The force responsible for changing the state of motion of an object is
questions.explanation: The force responsible for changing the state of motion of an object is friction. Moving body slows down due to friction and finally come to rest.

questions.question: Force of friction always acts on all
questions.explanation: The force of friction always acts on all moving objects due to contact between the two surfaces. Object moving in air, water, or on surface experiences friction.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of contact force?
questions.explanation: Magnetic force, gravitational force and electrostatic force are example of non-contact force while frictional force is an example of contact force because it arise only when two surfaces are in contact to each other.

questions.question: Friction depends upon

options.option: Smoothness of surface and nature of surface

questions.explanation: Friction depends upon the smoothness of surface and nature of surface. Rough surface offers more friction than smooth surface

questions.question: The force required to keep the object moving with same speed is a measure of
questions.explanation: When an object moves on a surface, resistance is offered and moving object slows down. To keep object moving with same speed a constant force is required which is the measure of sliding friction.

questions.question: It is difficult to walk on an oily floor because
questions.explanation: It is difficult to walk on an oily floor because force of friction is very less on oily surface. The irregularities present on surface are filled with oils that prevent the interlocking of the two surfaces.

questions.question: Sound can travel through
questions.explanation: Sound needs a medium through which it can travel. Sound cannot travel through a vacuum. Sound can travel through solid, liquid, and gases.

questions.question: Whenever the surfaces in contact tend to move or move with respect to each other, the force of friction comes into play

options.option: irrespective of whether the objects are solid, liquid, or gaseous.
only if one of the two objects is gaseous.
only if one of the two objects is liquid

questions.explanation: The force of friction comes into play irrespective of the states of matter. The force of friction does not depend upon the state of matter of any particular object. Force of friction acts in solids, liquids, and gases.

questions.question: To sharpen the blade of a knife by rubbing it against a surface, which of the following will be most suitable?
questions.explanation: Stone will exert more friction and reaction which helps to sharpen the blade of the knife.

questions.question: A toy car released with the same initial speed will travel farthest on
questions.explanation: The car will travel farthest on the surface which will exert minimum friction. Therefore, a marbled surface will be much suitable for the 's movement.

questions.question: If we apply oil on door hinges, the friction will
questions.explanation: The friction will reduce as oil works as a lubricant it will decrease the friction between the hinge and the door.

questions.question: A boy rolls a rubber ball on a wooden surface. The ball travels a short distance before coming to rest. To make the same ball travel longer distance before coming to rest, he may

options.option: sprinkle talcum powder on the wooden surface.
spread a carpet on the wooden surface.
cover the ball with a piece of cloth.

questions.explanation: The powder reduces the friction between the surface. So, the ball will go at a longer distance due to less friction.

questions.question: In a large commercial complex, there are four ways to reach the main road. One of the paths has loose soil, the second is laid with polished marble, the third is laid with bricks and the fourth has a gravel surface. It is raining heavily and Paheli wishes to reach the main road. The path on which she is least likely to slip is
questions.explanation: Gravel because the contact area is rough thus the friction is more.

questions.question: Mobil oil is applied on iron shutters to ________ friction.
questions.explanation: Mobil oil is applied on iron shutters to decrease the friction. After the applying of mobil oil, the shutter become very smooth and we could easily de-shutter it by applying less effort.

questions.question: The surface of mortar and pestle (silbatta) used for grinding is etched again after long use to ________ friction.
questions.explanation: After prolonged use, the mortar and pestle (silbatta) loose the roughness due to which frictional force reduces and it does not work. So, we have to make it rough again for increase friction.

questions.question: Friction is increased by ________
questions.explanation: Friction is increased by treading of tyres. Tread does have an influence on traction in road tyres, if it is not so high that is lefts the tread ground off the road (<0.2 mm) helps sink tread rubber into rough road surfaces and penetrate lubricants or dirt. Peaks in the tread increase press into surface services and add contact points between the road and tyre and thus increase friction.

questions.question: The handle of a cricket bat is usually rough to ________.
questions.explanation: The handle of a cricket bat is usually rough to increase friction to have a better grip, therefore it doesn't slip out in the hand of the cricketer while batting.

questions.question: Boats and ships are given streamlined shapes to

options.option: Increase friction during their propagation through water.
Decrease friction during their propagation through water.

questions.explanation: Streamlined shapes decrease the friction.

questions.question: Vehicles with deflated tyres find it difficult to move because

options.option: It decreases friction by decreasing surface areas of contact
It increases friction by increasing surface area of contact
It converts rolling motion to sliding motion
Both It increases friction by increasing surface area of contact and It decreases friction by decreasing surface areas of contact.

questions.explanation: When the tyre is fully inflated, it becomes hard and gets compressed by the road to smaller extent. The rolling friction will reduce and the wheel partially slides. While for deflated tyre, rolling motion converts into sliding motion.

questions.question: The static friction between an object in contact with a surface when it just starting to slip over the surface is 5 N. Then, the value of its limiting friction and sliding friction is approximately
questions.explanation: Limiting friction is the maximum value of static friction and sliding friction is always less than static friction.

questions.question:
Statement I: Friction always opposes motion.
Statement II: Friction can be totally eliminated using lubricants.
questions.explanation: Friction doesn't opposes the motion of an object but opposes the relative motion between two objects. Frictional force can never be eliminated.

questions.question: A box is pushed alternately on a slippery and on a rough surface. On which surface is it more likely to turn on its edges?

options.option: On both the surfaces the chances are equal

questions.explanation: Turning effect is created by the frictional force by the ground. So on a rough surface, a box is more likely to turn on its edges.

questions.question:
A car is moving in forward direction with wheel rotating in clockwise direction. What is the direction of
force of friction due to rough road and due to air, acting on the car?

options.option: In forward direction by road and backward direction by air.
In backward direction by both road and air.
In backward direction by road and forward direction by air.
In forward direction by both road and air.

questions.explanation: Wheel is rotating clockwise, force of friction will act in forward direction (as shown in figure). Force of friction due to air will be in backward direction.

questions.question: The force which opposes the movement of an object when it travels through air or water?
questions.explanation: The frictional force executed by fluid is called drag.

questions.question: Radha tries to mover her cycle with the brakes on, but cannot do so. Why?

options.option: Rolling friction is more than sliding friction
Sliding friction is more than rolling friction

questions.explanation: Sliding friction opposes motion on road.

questions.question: Why a horse need to pull harder during the first few steps in pulling the cart?

options.option: No frictional force acts after the cart comes in motion.
Air friction is greater during first few steps
Limiting friction is greater than dynamic friction.
Sliding friction is greater than rolling friction.

questions.explanation: During the first few steps of motion, the horse has to pull the cart harder, because the horse has to walk against limiting friction.

questions.question: When we hold a tumbler in our hand to lift it, frictional force acts.
questions.explanation: The frictional force appears between our finger and the outer surface of tumbler because the tumbler tends to go down due to its weight. Friction appears in the vertically upward direction, to balance the weight.

questions.question: A monkey is climbing up a rope holding the rope by hand. Which of the following helps it to go up?
questions.explanation: When the monkey tends to slip down due to weight, frictional force appears between its fingers and the rope in upward direction which pushes it to go in upward direction.

questions.question: Vehicles with worn out tyres find it difficult to run on the road because
questions.explanation: When the surface of tyre becomes smoother due to wear and tear, the grip of tyre becomes loose. In other words, the frictional force between tyre and road becomes less.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about friction is wrong?

options.option: Friction can hold a nail fixed inside a wall.

questions.explanation: All the statements are correct.

questions.question: Suppose surface of the Moon and the Earth are equally rough. A box is pushed on the surface of the Moon, so that it slides there. Mark the correct statement.

options.option: It will be easier to push the box on the Moon than on the Earth.
It will be more difficult to push the box on the Moon than on the Earth.

questions.explanation: Larger the weight, more difficult it will be to push a luggage i.e., frictional force will be greater. As on Moon, gravity is less, therefore weight is less hence frictional force is less.

questions.question:
A fire can be made by rubbing two objects together such as rubbing two pieces of rock having rough surfaces. Which of the following statements explain why a fire can be made by rubbing two objects together?

  1. Friction slows down moving objects.
  2. Fire gives out heat and light energy.
  3. Friction produces heat.
  4. Fire has no mass.
questions.explanation: On rubbing two rough surfaces, fire can be produced due to friction.

questions.question: A force F is applied on the topmost point of a ball placed on a rough surface. Find the direction of frictional force that is created at the bottom most point B. (See figure)

options.option: Both Force in backward direction and Force perpendicular to surface.

questions.explanation: Due to force F, the ball will start rotating in clockwise direction (as shown in figure), hence frictional force appears in forward direction, to oppose rotation.

questions.question: A small body P is placed on another larger body Q whose upper surface is rough. Q is placed on a rough ground. A force F is applied on Q due to which both starts moving in forward direction. What is the direction of frictional force acting on P and Q?

options.option: Forward direction on both P and Q.
Backward direction on both P and Q.
Forward direction on Q and backward direction on P.
Forward direction on P and backward direction on Q.

questions.explanation: As the force F is acting on Q in forward direction so, the friction on Q will act in backward direction. The two blocks are moving together, so the equal force acting on P in forward direction.

questions.question: A body Q is placed on a frictionless surface. On the upper surface of Q which is rough, another body P is placed and it is pulled in forward direction with 100 N force. Both P and Q move together in forward direction, without P slipping over Q. Find the direction of frictional force on P and Q.

options.option: On P and Q in forward direction.
On P and Q in backward direction.
On P in forward direction and on Q in backward direction.
On P in backward direction and on Q in forward direction.

questions.explanation: Only force acting on body Q in horizontal direction is friction force. As the two are moving in forward direction. So, friction is acting on Q in forward direction and on P in backward direction.

questions.question: Which one of the following is a right arrangement?
questions.explanation: The order of right arrangement of given friction is static friction > sliding friction > rolling friction.

questions.question:
Consider the given statements and select the option which correctly identifies true (T) and false (F) ones.

  1. When a body is in steady motion on a surface, the force of friction is dynamic friction.
  2. The force of friction between two bodies always acts parallel to the surfaces in contact with each other.
  3. It is easier to push a lawn roller than to pull.
  4. Limiting friction is greater than sliding friction
questions.explanation: When we push an object, there is one component of force that adds to the weight of the body hence there is more friction. When we pull an object, vertical component of force is against the weight of the body and hence there is reduction in friction. So it is easy to pull a lawn roller than to push.

questions.question: A bicycle is being pedalled on a horizontal road. The forces of friction exerted by the road on the front and rear wheels are F1 and F2 respectively. Then

options.option: Both F1 and F2 act in the backward direction.
F1 acts in the forward direction, F2 acts in the backward direction.
F1 acts in the backward direction, F2 acts in the forward direction.
Both F1 and F2 act in the forward direction.

questions.explanation: Force of friction acts on the rear wheels in forward direction to give a push to the bicycle in forward direction. It is sliding friction. On the front wheel, rolling friction acts against the motion of the bicycle so it acts in backward direction.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements are incorrect?

  1. It is easier to roll than to slide some heavy object over another fixed object.
  2. If there is less friction between the car tyres and the road, then the car can move forward very smoothly without skidding.
  3. Friction helps a person to climb a tree or a pole.
  4. Friction helps us to light a matchstick.
  5. Ball bearing are designed to increase friction between moving parts of machines.
questions.explanation: Friction of road helps the cars to move forward, so statement (ii) is wrong. Ball bearings are designed to reduce friction between two surfaces. So, statement (v) is also wrong.

questions.question:
Read the given statements carefully.

  1. Gymnasts apply some coarse substance on their hands.
  2. Tying shoe laces.
  3. Applying the brakes on a car.
  4. Writing on the blackboard with a chalk.

In which of the following cases is the friction helpful?
questions.explanation: Gymnasts apply some coarse material on their palm to increase friction so that it can give them firm grip over their opponents. In lace, knot remains tied due to friction. In absence of friction, knot will be untied easily. Brakes stop a car by applying frictional force on the rim of the wheels. Writing on blackboard involves erosion of the chalk from its ends and sticking of particles to the blackboard. Erosion is caused by frictional force. So, friction helps in all the given cases.

questions.question: Human beings can produce sound due to the vibrations of the ________.
questions.explanation: The sound is produced in human beings by the vibrations of vocal cords.

questions.question: In human ear, the part of outer ear that vibrates when hit by sound waves.
questions.explanation: When the sound waves fall on the ear-drum, it starts vibrating back and forth rapidly.

Rubbing of sand paper on sand paper

questions.explanation: Friction opposes the relative motion between two surfaces. Thus higher the friction lower is the distance travelled by the object.

questions.explanation: The friction which arising when as object rolls over the surface of another body is known as rolling friction.

questions.explanation: Maximum value of static friction is called limiting friction.

options.option: Rolling friction is less than static friction.
Static friction is greater than rolling friction.
Static friction is greater than sliding friction
Sliding friction is less than rolling friction.

questions.explanation: Rolling friction is always less than sliding friction.

questions.explanation: Sliding friction is always less than static friction.

questions.question:

questions.explanation: Friction causes the surface in contact to wear out.

questions.question: Friction reduces efficiency of a machine by
questions.explanation: Friction produces heat. The heat energy is produced by the conversion of mechanical energy into heat energy so there is wastage of energy and the efficiency of machine is reduced.

questions.question: Ball bearing makes movement between two surfaces easier, because it
questions.explanation: Ball bearing converts sliding friction into rolling friction due to small metallic sphere rolling between two layers.

questions.question: Carts with rubber tyres are easier to move than with iron wheels when

options.option: Friction between rubber tyres and road is more.
Friction between rubber tyres and road is less.
Friction between iron wheels and road is less.

questions.explanation: The friction between rubber tyres and road is much smaller than that between iron wheels and the road.

options.option: Friction will be equal in both the cases.

questions.explanation: Friction does not depend on the area of contact.

The force of friction between two bodies is

options.option: Surfaces of both the objects exert force in a direction opposite to each other.
Surface of upper object exerts force of friction on the lower object.
Surface of lower object exerts force of friction on the upper object.
Surfaces of both the objects exert force on each other in the same direction.

questions.explanation: Surfaces of both the objects exert force in a direction opposite to each other.

questions.question: Frictional force is exerted by
questions.explanation: all of these

less irregularities on the surface of table
less irregularites on the surface of road
more irregularities on the surface of table

questions.explanation: increase friction

questions.explanation: excessive friction

questions.explanation: viscous friction

shape of the object moving in the fluid
nature of fluids in which object is moving

questions.explanation: all of these
