questions.question: Time taken to complete one vibration is called
questions.explanation: The time taken by an oscillating pendulum to make one complete oscillation is called its time period. Time taken to complete on vibration is called as time-period of vibrating body.

questions.question: Sound cannot travel through
questions.explanation: Sound cannot travel through vacuum as sound require material medium for its propagation. Sound can travel through air, water, and solids.

questions.question: Sound helps us to
questions.explanation: Sound helps us to communicate with one another. We share our views and suggestions with other either by sound or through written medium.

questions.question: Sound is produced by
questions.explanation: Sound is produced by the vibration of an object. Vibration creates a disturbance in a medium which reaches to observer's ear through the air, water, or solid medium.

questions.question: Speed of sound increase with
questions.explanation: Speed of sound increase with increase in temperature, during propagation of sounds particles of medium travel faster at higher temperature.

questions.question: When a tightly stretched band is plucked it
questions.explanation: When a tightly stretched band is plucked it vibrate and produce sound due to disturbance in medium.

questions.question: Which of the following factor influence speed of sound most?
questions.explanation: Speed of sound depends upon nature of medium, temperature, humidity, and source of sound but nature of medium influence the speed of sound most. Sound travels faster in solid medium than air and water.

questions.question: Which of the following require material medium for propagation?
questions.explanation: Sound require material medium for its propagation which may be solid, liquid or gaseous in nature. Light can travel in vacuum as no medium is required.

questions.question: In most of the cases vibration of objects are not visible because
questions.explanation: The vibrations of object are not visible because in most of the cases amplitude of waves are very small. Amplitude is the maximum displacement from mean position.

questions.question: Loudness of sound depends upon its
questions.explanation: The maximum displacement from mean position is called its amplitude. The loudness of sound depends upon its amplitude.

questions.question: Sound of single frequency is called a
questions.explanation: The sound of single frequency is called tone. A number of tones together form a note of the sound.

questions.question: The shape of the outer part of human ear is like a
questions.explanation: Human ear consists of three parts- outer ear, middle ear, and inner ear. The shape of outer ear is like a funnel through which vibration reaches to eardrum.

questions.question: In which medium sound travels faster?
questions.explanation: The speed of sound depends upon the material medium in which sound is travelling. Sound travels faster in solid (metals) followed by water and air respectively.

questions.question: Which of the following travels fastest?
questions.explanation: The speed of light is 299,792,458 metres per second in air. Supersonic aircraft and rockets travel with a speed of about 350 m/s only.

questions.question: In human beings, sound is produced due to vibration of
questions.explanation: Vocal cords are situated in neck of human beings. Sound is produced due to vibration of vocal cords.

questions.question: Pitch of sound is determined by its
questions.explanation: The number of vibration per second is called frequency. The pitch of sound depends upon frequency of sound.

questions.question: Loudness of sound is measured in units of
questions.explanation: The loudness of sound is measured in Decibel (dB). Sound of loudness greater than 80 dB is harmful for our ears.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a bone present in human ear?
questions.explanation: Femur bone is not present in ear. Anvil, Hammer, and stirrups are present in the ears.

questions.question: Which nerve transmits sound impulse to brain form ear?
questions.explanation: Auditory nerve transmits sound impulses to brain from ear. Optic nerve, cardiac nerves are present in eyes and heart respectively.

questions.question: The part of ear which vibrates when outside sound fall on it is
questions.explanation: The tympanic membrane is the part of ear which vibrates when outside sound fall on it to create vibration.

questions.question: The function of three bones present in the middle of ear is to
questions.explanation: Three bones are present in the middle ear named as hammer, anvil, and stirrup. These bones are attached with eardrum one after the other to amplify the sound.

questions.question: Name the medium in which sound travels slower?
questions.explanation: The medium in which sound travels slower is gas.

questions.question: Sound can travel through
questions.explanation: Sound require material medium for its propagation. The medium may be solid, liquid or gases. Sound travel faster in solid medium than gaseous and liquid medium.

questions.question: The number of oscillations per second of a vibrating object is called its
questions.explanation: The number of oscillations per second of a vibrating object is called its frequency. Frequency is measured in Hertz (Hz).

questions.question: Noise pollution may cause
questions.explanation: Noise pollution may cause hearing impairment. Unpleasant sound having high altitude may harm the eardrum that leads to hearing problem.

questions.question: Time taken by an object to complete one oscillation is called
questions.explanation: The time taken by an object to complete one oscillation is called time-period. Time-period is reciprocal to frequency of sound.

questions.question: A pendulum oscillates 40 times in 4 seconds. The time period of vibration will be
questions.explanation:
Number of oscillation = 40, time taken = 4 seconds. 
Frequency = {tex}40÷4{/tex} = 10Hz (number of oscillations​ made per second)
Time period=1÷Frequency
Time period=1 {tex}\div{/tex} 10 = 0.1s

questions.question: Loud sound can be produced by
questions.explanation: Loudness of sound depends upon amplitude of the sound. Higher the amplitude, louder the sound will be.

questions.question: Plantation on the road side can
questions.explanation: Plantation on the road side can reduce noise pollution as plants are good absorber of sound and also reduce the carbon dioxide gas released by vehicles.

questions.question: We must never put a sharp, pointed things into our ear because it can
questions.explanation: We must never put sharp, pointed things into our ear because it can damage eardrum. Eardrum is thin membrane at the junction of outer ear and middle ear.

questions.question: Time-period of a wave is 0.01 seconds than its frequency is
questions.explanation:
Time-period of a wave is 0.01seconds then 
Frequency=1 {tex}\div{/tex} Time period
Frequency= 1 {tex}\div{/tex} 0.01
Frequency= 100 Hz

questions.question: The to and fro motion of an object is known as
questions.explanation: The to and fro motion of an object is known as vibration. Vibration is necessary for production of sound.

questions.question: An object oscillates 20 times in 1 second. The frequency of the wave produced will be
questions.explanation: The object oscillating 20 times in one second will have frequency of 20 Hz as number of oscillation or vibration per second is called frequency.

questions.question: The repetition of sound caused by the reflection of sound waves is called
questions.explanation: The repetition of sound caused by the reflection of sound waves is called Echo. Echo is produced in large halls or in hilly areas due to reflection of sound.

questions.question: Ultrasound equipment for investigating and tracking medical problems works at frequency
questions.explanation: Ultrasound equipment is used for investigating and tracking medical problems. The sound of frequency greater than 20kHz is called ultrasound.

questions.question: The sound which is pleasant to hear is called
questions.explanation: The sound which is pleasant to hear is called musical sound while sound which is unpleasant to hear is called noise.

questions.question: Which of the following health problem is not caused due to noise pollution?
questions.explanation: Noise pollution may cause a number of health problem like hypertension, anxiety, and lack of sleep. Diabetes is a hormonal problem not caused due to noise pollution.

questions.question: Which of the following sounds cannot be heard by human beings?
questions.explanation: Human ear can hear sounds of frequency between 20Hz to 20,000Hz. So, sound of frequency 10Hz cannot be heard by human beings.

questions.question: The police use high frequency whistles which can be heard by
questions.explanation: The police use high frequency whistles which can be heard by dogs but not by human beings.

questions.question: Motion of which of these object is known as periodic motion?
questions.explanation: The motion of object which repeats after equal interval of time is called periodic motion. The motion of Simple pendulum is periodic in nature.

questions.question: Lightening and thunder occurs together but lightening is seen earlier than sound as

options.option: Light do not require medium for propagation

questions.explanation: Lightening and thunder occurs together but lightening is seen earlier than sound because light travel much faster than sound.

questions.question: The time period of a vibration 500Hz is equal to
questions.explanation: Frequency of sound = 500Hz. Time-period = 1/frequency = 1/500 = 0.002 seconds.

questions.question: Which of the following generally do not cause noise pollution?
questions.explanation: Noise pollution is caused by vehicles, machines, and loudspeakers. Sitar is a musical instrument that does not produce noise.

questions.question: A child with hearing loss may have
questions.explanation: Generally, a child with hearing impairment from birth have defective speech.

questions.question: Which of the following have voice of higher frequency and shriller?
questions.explanation: The voice of woman have higher frequency and shriller than man. We can easily identify the voice of female from male.

questions.question: Vibration is also known as
questions.explanation: Vibration is also known as oscillatory motion as vibrating body oscillates to and fro. Oscillatory motion has fixed time-period.

questions.question: When the amplitude of sound is large, the sound produced is
questions.explanation: The loudness of sound depends upon amplitude of sound. So, when the amplitude of sound is large, the sound produced is large.

questions.question: Voice of male is heavy compared to woman because
questions.explanation: Voice of male is heavy compared to woman because male voice has low frequency as compared to female and male vocal cord is larger than female.

questions.question: The sound which is unpleasant to ear is called
questions.explanation: Noise is the sound which is unpleasant to ear. The pleasant sound produced by musical instruments is called music.

questions.question: Which of the following method is used to reduce the noise pollution along roads?
questions.explanation: To reduce noise pollution along roads, planting trees around roads is used. Plants are a good absorber of sound and reduce carbon dioxide concentration.

questions.question: Different types similar sounds can be differentiated due to
questions.explanation: Different kinds of similar sounds can be differentiated due to difference in amplitude and frequency. Pitch of different persons is also different

questions.question: When the amplitude is doubled than its loudness
questions.explanation: The loudness of sound depends upon amplitude of sound. When amplitude is doubled than loudness increase by four times.

questions.question: Which of the following vibrates with a low frequency?
questions.explanation: Drum has a thin membrane of skin that vibrates on beating. The number of vibration per second is less in the drum in comparison to whistle, flute, and sitar.

questions.question: Industrial noise can be reduced by

options.option: Installing industrial activity away from residential area

questions.explanation: Industrial noise can be reduced by installing industrial activity away from residential area and using safety for workers.

questions.question: The frequency of a sound wave is 200 Hz, how many times will it vibrates in one minute?
questions.explanation: Frequency of sound = 200Hz, time = 1minute = 60 seconds. Number of vibration = 60 {tex}\times{/tex} 200 = 12000times.

questions.question: Which of the following quantities are proportional to each other?
questions.explanation: Loudness of sound depends upon amplitude of sound waves. Loudness of sound is proportional to amplitude of sound.

questions.question: We are not able to hear the sound of pendulum although it is oscillating because
questions.explanation: Human ear can hear sounds of frequency 20Hz to 20,000Hz. So, we are not able to hear the sound of pendulum of frequency less than 10Hz.

questions.question: Sound travels faster in solid medium as compared to liquid and gases because
questions.explanation: Sound travels faster in the solid medium as compared to liquid and gases because particles of solid are very close to each other.

questions.question: Loudness of sound is proportional to
questions.explanation: Loudness of sound is proportional to the square of the amplitude. When the amplitude is doubled, loudness increases four times.

questions.question: A person is exposed to a loud sound continuously he may get
questions.explanation: When a person is exposed to a loud sound continuously he may get temporary impairment of hearing along with other health hazards.

questions.question: Total hearing impairment is usually

options.option: Due to viewing TV at high volume

questions.explanation: Total hearing impairment is usually from birth. Hearing impairment may also occurs due to accidents or some other reasons.

questions.question: In a classroom, all the students speak together the sound produced will be called
questions.explanation: Unpleasant sound is called noise. In classroom, all the students speak together the sound produced will be called noise.

questions.question: Sound cannot be heard directly on the moon because

options.option: There is no air on the moon

questions.explanation: Sound requires material medium for its propagation. On the moon, the atmosphere is absent so, sound cannot be heard directly on the moon in absence of air.

questions.question: The relation between the speed of sound, frequency, and wavelength is
questions.explanation: Speed of sound is equal to product of frequency and wavelength. Speed = frequency × wavelength.

questions.question: Speed of sound in sea water is slightly more than distilled water because
questions.explanation: Speed of sound increase with increase density. So, the speed of sound in seawater is slightly more than distilled water because density of seawater is more due to presence of salts.

questions.question: If the thunder is heard by a man 4 seconds after the lightening is seen. How far is the lightening from man? (Speed of sound in air is 330m/s )
questions.explanation: Speed of sound = 330 m/s, time = 4 seconds. Distance= speed {tex}\times{/tex} time = 330 m/s {tex}\times{/tex} 4 = 1320m. so, lightening occurs at a distance of 1320m.

questions.question: Voice of which of the following is likely to have minimum frequency?
questions.explanation: The voice of an adult man is of lower pitch in comparison to the voices of a baby boy, a baby girl and a woman. Since frequency of a sound is directly proportional to its pitch, man’s voice is of minimum frequency in comparison to a boy, a girl, or awoman’s voice.

questions.question:
A list of mediums is given below.

  1. wood
  2. water
  3. air
  4. vacuum

In which of these mediums can sound travel?
questions.explanation: Sound requires any medium to travel but in vacuum there is no medium, so sound cannot travel through them.

questions.question: An object is vibrating at 50 hertz. What is its time period?
questions.explanation:
We know that
Frequency {tex}=\frac{1}{\text { Time period }}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Time period {tex}=\frac{1}{\text { Frequency }}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Time period {tex}=\frac{1}{50}{/tex} = 0.02 s

questions.question: In order to reduce the loudness of a sound we have to

options.option: decrease its frequency of vibration of the sound.
increase its amplitude of vibration of the sound.
decrease its amplitude of vibration of the sound.
increase its frequency of vibration of the sound.

questions.explanation: Since loudness depends upon amplitude, so it can be increased by increasing amplitude and it can be decreased by decreasing amplitude.

questions.question: The loudness of sound is determined by the

options.option: product of amplitude and frequency of vibration
ratio of amplitude and frequency of vibration

questions.question:

(Take, speed of sound = 330 m/s)

Given, speed of sound = 330 m/s and time, t =10 s
We know that, speed = {tex}\frac{distance}{time}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} distance = speed {tex}\times{/tex} time
So, distance travelled by sound in 10s.
= 330 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 = 3300 m

questions.question: The frequency of ultrasound is:
questions.explanation: Ultrasound applies to all sound energy with a frequency above human hearing (20,000 hertz or 20 kilohertz). Typical diagnostic sonographic scanners operate in the frequency range of 2 to 18 megahertz, hundreds of times greater than the limit of human hearing.

questions.question: The to and fro or back and forth motion of an object about its mean position, which produces sound is called
questions.explanation: The 'To and fro' or 'back and forth' motion of an object about its mean position, which produced sound is called vibration.

questions.question: For which measurement the ‘decibel’ is used?
questions.explanation: Frequency, reported in Hertz (Hz), measures the number of sound vibrations in one second. Amplitude, reported on the decibel (dB) scale, measures its pressure or forcefulness.

questions.question: The part which vibrates in our vocal-box is-
questions.explanation: The larynx, or voice box, is located in the neck and performs several important functions in the body. The larynx is involved in swallowing, breathing, and voice production. Sound is produced when the air which passes through the vocal cords causes them to vibrate and create sound waves in the pharynx, nose and mouth.

questions.question: Which of the following characteristics of sound depends on amplitude of vibration?
questions.explanation: The intensity or loudness of a sound depends upon the extent to which the sounding body vibrate i.e., the amplitude of vibration. A sound is loudness as the amplitude of vibration is greates. Loudness is measured in units called decibels.

subtopics.name: Sound Needs a Medium for Propagation

questions.question: The speed of sound is maximum in-
questions.explanation: The speed of sound varies from substance to substance: typically, sound travels most slowly in gases, faster in liquids, and fastest in solids. For example, while sound travels at 343 m/s in air, it travels at 1,481 m/s in water (almost 4.3 times faster) and at 5,120 m/s in iron (almost 15 times faster).

questions.question: The energy circulating in telephone lines is-
questions.explanation: The telephone system converts acoustic energy (sound) into electric energy. The electric energy travels along the phone wires and is converted back into acoustic energy at the receiving end.

questions.question: If we increase the vibration in an object the time period will ________
questions.explanation:
If we increase the vibration in an object, the time period will decrease in same proportion. Since, a time period is the time taken for one complete cycle of vibration, i.e.
Time period = {tex}\frac{1}{ Frequency}{/tex}

questions.question: The wavelength of a certain sound wave is 110 m long. Given that the speed of sound in air is approximately 330 m s-1 and the audible range of a normal person is 20 Hz to 20000 Hz. An observer who is standing 220 m away from the source of this sound wave hears

options.option: Sound but its properties cannot be determined

questions.explanation:
{tex}\lambda{/tex} = 110 m, v = 330 m/s
v( frequency) = {tex}\frac{v}{\lambda}=\frac{330}{110}{/tex} = 3 Hz
Since frequency is less than 20 Hz, the sound will not be audible.

questions.question: A sound wave having frequency of 100 Hz will have wavelength of ________ at 0oC in air (Take velocity of sound as 330 ms-1).
questions.explanation:
As frequency u = 100 Hz,
v, speed of sound, v = 330 m/s at 0oC
Frequency {tex}\times{/tex} Wavelength = Velocity
Wavelength = {tex}\frac{\text { Velocity }}{\text { Frequency }}{/tex}
{tex}\lambda{/tex} = {tex}\frac{330}{100}{/tex} = 3.3 m

questions.question: When sound passes from air to water, its wavelength
questions.explanation:
When sound passes from air to water, its velocity increases but its frequency remains constant.
{tex}v=v \lambda \text { or } \lambda=\frac{v}{v}{/tex}
Velocity is directly proportional to wavelength.

questions.question:
Echolocation is used to locate shoals of fish in the sea. A pulse of sound wave is sent from the ship and is reflected by the shoal as shown in figure.

questions.question: In an experiment to measure the speed of sound in air, a boy stands 40 m away from a wall and bangs two pieces of wood together. questions.explanation:
Total distance covered during given activity
= 40 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex}50 m
Total time taken for 50 bangs =12 seconds
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Speed of sound in air = {tex}\frac{\text { Distance covered }}{\text { Time taken }}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{40 \times 2 \times 50}{12}{/tex} ms-1

questions.question: A device which prevents or allow the current to flow through it
questions.explanation: Switch is a device for breaking and making the connection. Switch is combined with positive terminal of circuit.

questions.question: In battery two or more cells are connected in
questions.explanation: Battery is a combination of two or more cells connected in series. It supplies electric power to the electrical or electronic devices.

questions.question: An electric cell produces electricity from the
questions.explanation: An electric cell produces electricity from the chemical stored in it. Here chemical energy is converted into electrical energy

questions.question: Inside the torch, two or more cells are placed with
questions.explanation: Inside the torch, two or more cells are placed with positive-negative-positive-negative terminal. This is done to arrange them in series connection

questions.question: Which of the following lightening device uses comparatively less energy?
questions.explanation: Compact fluorescent lamp (CFL) is less energy consuming device in comparison to tube light, bulb, and filament rod. CFLs use one-fifth to one-third the electric power and last eight to fifteen times longer.

questions.question: An electric current can produce:
questions.explanation:
When an electric current is passed through a conducting solution, it causes chemical reactions. The resulting effect is called the chemical effect of current.
When an electric current is passed through a bulb, its filament gets heated to a high temperature and bulb starts glowing. The resulting effect is the heating effect of current. Whenever an electric current te passed through a circuit, a magnetic field is produced around it. The resulting effect is the magnetic effect of current. Thus, an electric current can produce chemical, heating and magnetic effects.

questions.question: Which of the following group contain only conductor?
questions.explanation: Copper and silver is metal, so good conductor of electricity. Graphite is allotrope of carbon is also good conductor of electricity. They all have free electrons which take part in flowing of electric current

questions.question: ISI marks to ensure that the appliance is safe and wastage of energy is
questions.explanation: ISI marks on appliances ensure that the appliance is safe and the wastage of energy is minimum. ISI mark is a certification for industrial products in India. The mark certifies that a product conforms to the Indian Standard.

questions.question: A battery is used to
questions.explanation: In electric circuit, a battery is used to maintain potential difference between two terminals of appliances. It is necessary to the flow of current as current flow from high potential to low potential of voltage.

questions.question: Metals are good conductor of electricity because it can
questions.explanation: Metals are generally good conductor of electricity because it contains free electrons in their outermost orbit. The free electrons are responsible to flow of current  when potential difference is applied, giving rise to electric current

questions.question: Two similar charges are brought close to each other, then they will
questions.explanation: When two similar charges are brought close to each other, then they will repel each other on the other hand unlike charges attract each other.   This is the fundamental property of charge

questions.question: Chemical effect of electric current is used in ______.
questions.explanation: Chemical effect of electric current is used in electroplating in which a thin layer of other metal is deposited on other metal.This is done to avoid corrosion in metals or rusting in iron.

questions.question: Which types of solution do not conduct electricity?
questions.explanation: Solution of glucose do not produce ions when dissolved in water, so solution of glucose is poor conductor of electricity. For electricity to flow free ions should be produced after dissociation.

questions.question: Solution of acids are generally
questions.explanation: Solution of acid when dissociates in water produces free ions that help in flowing of electric current.  Hence, solution of acids is good conductor of electricity.

questions.question: LEDs are
questions.explanation: LEDs stands for light emitting diodes. It consists of two leads one longer and one smaller. Longer is positive and short is negative terminals.

questions.question: The two rods that are dipped in solution for electrolysis is called
questions.explanation: During electrolysis, two rods are dipped in salt solution. One rod is called cathode and other is called anode and both are together called electrode. Cathodes are negatively charged and anodes are positively charged electrodes.

questions.question: The passage of an electric current through a conducting solution causes
questions.explanation: Passage of electric current through a conducting solution causes chemical reaction in solution called chemical effect of electricity. Conducting solution contains free ions that conducts electricity.

questions.question: The electrode connected to negative terminal is called
questions.explanation: The electrode connected to negative terminal is called cathode. The positively charged particles deposited at cathode end. Whereas anode is connected to positive terminal.

questions.question: The substance through which current can flow easily are called
questions.explanation: The substance through which electric current can flow easily are called good conductor of electricity. Metals are good conductor of electricity. Copper metal is used widely as an electric wire.

questions.question: During electroplating, the object to be electroplated is attached with
questions.explanation: During electroplating, the object to be electroplated is attached with cathode, and object by which electroplating is done is attached to anode. Because metals are cations and they get deposited in cathode (negative terminal).

questions.question: Conductor having very high resistance is
questions.explanation: The obstruction in flow of electric current is called resistance. Conductor having very high resistance is called insulator. Air, cloth, and rubber are good electrical insulators.

questions.question: When common salt is dissolved in water it become more conducting because
questions.explanation: When common salt is dissolved in water it becomes more conducting because on passing electric current common salt (sodium chloride) dissociates into sodium and chlorine ions. These free ions are responsible for conduction.

questions.question: When LED is connected to a circuit the shorter wire is connected to
questions.explanation: LED stands for light emitting device. When LED is connected to circuit the shorter wire is connected to negative terminal and longer with positive terminal. Due to this convention two legs of LED are different in size.

questions.question: When electric current passes through the filaments of bulb it start emitting light due to
questions.explanation: The filaments of bulbs are made up of tungsten having very high resistance and high melting point. When current passes through the filament of bulb, it start emitting light due to heating effect of electric current. So in electric bulb, electrical energy is converting first to heat energy and then to light energy.

questions.question: In which material electricity does flow?
questions.explanation: Electricity does flows through Metallic materials as these are good conductor of electricity due to presence of free electrons.

questions.question: Which of the following liquid conduct electric current?
questions.explanation: Lemon juice contains citric acid that produces ions after dissociation in water. These ions conduct electricity.

questions.question: LED is better than bulb to use in tester as
questions.explanation: LED is used as tester in place of bulb because LED consumes less power and hence less electric current.

questions.question: Which effect of electric current is used for detecting the flow of current through solution when a torch bulb is used?
questions.explanation: When an electric current is passed through a conductor, the conductor becomes hot after some time and produce heat. This happens due to the conversion of some electric energy passing through the conductor into heat energy. This effect of electric current is called heating effect of current. The heating effect of current is used in various electrical heating appliances such as electric bulb, electric iron, room heaters, geysers, electric fuse, etc.

questions.question: Which of the following can not be used to detect the flow of current?
questions.explanation: As conductors are the materials which enable current to flow through them and we may get an electric shock that is why we cannot use conductors to detect the flow of current.

questions.question: Which of the following should be added to distilled water to make it conductor?
questions.explanation: Addition of acid, base, or salt or lime juice in distilled water makes it a good conductor of electricity as they provide ions on passing current.

questions.question: Electric supply is shut off before using water hoses in case of fire because
questions.explanation: As impure water is a good conductor of electricity.  if the main electrical supply is kept on, there are chances that the water may spill over it, and the chance to get an electric shock increases.

questions.question: When electric current is passed through acidified water then what is produced at positive carbon electrode
questions.explanation: When electric current is passed through acidified water, then oxygen gas is produced at positive terminal. Oxygen forms anions that are attracted towards positively charged electrode. while the hydrogen gas is produced at negative terminal.

questions.question: Chemical effect of electric current is used in
questions.explanation: Electroplating is a chemical process using which a metal is coated with a layer of another desired metal. Electroplating is done to make the metals shiny.

questions.question: The gas released on negative carbon electrode when current passes through acidified water?
questions.explanation:
When an electric current is passed through acidified water, it decomposes to give hydrogen and oxygen gas. The hydrogen gas is obtained at the cathode(negative electrode) and the oxygen gas is obtained at the anode(positive electrode).
The reaction for decomposition of water into oxygen and hydrogen is as follows:
2H2O(I) {tex}\to{/tex} 2H2(g) + O2(g)
Thus, the number of hydrogen molecules produced is twice the number of oxygen molecules. So, electrolysis of water produces 2 volumes of hydrogen gas and 1 volume of oxygen gas because the ratio of hydrogen and oxygen elements in water is 2:1 by volume. Therefore, the amount of gases collected in two test tubes is not equal.

questions.question: Which of the following is used for electroplating
questions.explanation: Chromium metal is used for electroplating because chromium is least reactive and provide shiny surface with scratch resistance.

questions.question: The chemical effect produced by an electric current depends on
questions.explanation: The chemical effect of electric current produced by an electric current depends on conducting solution and amount of current flowing through solution.

questions.question: Which metal is electroplated on iron for making cans?
questions.explanation: Tin is electroplated on iron for making cans for storing food and drinks as tin is less reactive than iron.

questions.question: In the process of refining copper metal a thin plate of pure copper is connected to
questions.explanation: Electrolytic refining is used to refine copper metal. A thin plate of pure copper is connected to cathode and thick impure copper plate to anode.

questions.question: During purification of copper, the electrolyte used is
questions.explanation: During purification of copper, the electrolyte is used is copper sulphate. A thin strip of pure copper is used as cathode and a thick impure copper as anode.

questions.question: When the free ends of tester are dipped into a solution, the magnetic needle shows deflection due to
questions.explanation: The magnetic needle responds to the flow of current between the free ends of a tester. When a tester is dipped into a "conducting" solution, the current will flow between the two terminals through the completed circuit and will lead to deflection in the magnetic needle. A conducting solution contains free ions, that can conduct electricity. For a "non-conducting" solution, the circuit will not be complete and hence there would be no current...

questions.question: While checking your tester, do not join its free ends for more than a few seconds otherwise
questions.explanation: While we joined the free ends of the tester, it consumes power and as a result, the cells of bettery may drain quickly.

questions.question: Lemon juice is good conductor of electricity due to
questions.explanation: Lemon juice contains citric acid that gives ions on passing electric current.

questions.question: We should not use electric supply from mains or generator to experiment because

options.option: Flow of current can not be measured

questions.explanation:
These are some precautions we should take care of during the experiment.

  • Never touch a lighted electric bulb connected to the mains. It may be very hot and your hand may get burnt badly.
  • Do not experiment with the electric supply from the mains or a generator or an inverter. You may get an electric shock, which may be dangerous.
  • Use only electric cells.

questions.question: Close the continuous path of electric current is called
questions.explanation: The close and continuous path of electric current is called electric circuit. It consists of switch, connector and an electric appliance. In a continuous and close path of electric current the electron gets a path to flow to form an electric circuit.

questions.question: Distilled water bad conductor of electricity but vinegar is good conductor as it contain
questions.explanation: Distilled water is bad conductor of electricity because it do not contain salts but vinegar contain acetic acid that give hydrogen ions to conduct electricity.

questions.question: The water from taps, hand pumps, wells, and ponds is a good conductor of electricity because of
questions.explanation: Small amount of salts are present in water from taps, hand pumps, wells, and ponds. The salt present in these waters make them good conductor of electricity.

questions.question: Which of the following substance should not be added to distilled water to make it good conductor?
questions.explanation: Addition of acids, bases, and salts in distilled water makes it good conductor of electricity due to presence of ions in these substances but alcohol does not produce ions and it does not make any change in the pH value of distilled water.

questions.question: A device that converts the electrical energy into heat energy is
questions.explanation: Electrical energy is a form of energy that occurs as a result of either stored or moving charged particles. A toaster draws electric current (electrical energy) from a wall outlet and converts these moving electric charges into heat (thermal energy) in the filaments that turn red hot to cook your toast. The same has happened with Heater and geyser. As they contain coils having high resistivity.

questions.question: Jewellery maker electroplate silver and gold on less expensive metals to make it

options.option: To have the appearance of silver and gold

questions.explanation: Silver and Gold jewelry are electroplated with another less expensive metals to have appearance of gold or Silver as these metals are more expensive than other metals. Gold or Silver electroplating, when used in jewelry manufacturing, allows the manufacturer to provide the market with jewelry that appears to be made of pure gold or Silver at a cheaper price as the jewelry is only plated with small amount of gold or Silver.

questions.question: What serves as anode in a common dry cell?
questions.explanation: A common dry-cell battery is a zinc-carbon battery, which uses a cell that is sometimes called the Leclanché cell. The cell is made up of an outer zinc container, which acts as the anode. The cathode is a central carbon rod, surrounded by a mixture of carbon and manganese(IV) dioxide (MnO2).

questions.question: Boojho's uncle has set up an electroplating factory near his village. He should dispose off the waste of the factory:

options.option: according to the disposal guidelines of the local authority.

questions.explanation: In electroplating factory, the disposal of the used conducting solution is a major concern because it is a polluting waste, therefore it should be disposed off according to the disposal guidelines of the local authority.

questions.question: What is the common name for manganese-dioxide-alkaline- zinc cell?
questions.explanation: Alkaline cell is commonly called as manganese dioxide alkaline zinc cell.These batteries are the most commonly used worldwide in flashlights, toys, radios, compact disc players, and digital cameras. There are three variations: the zinc-carbon battery, the zinc chloride battery, and the alkaline battery. All provide an initial voltage of 1.55 to 1.7 volts, which declines with use to an end point of about 0.8 volt.

questions.question: The two leads of LED is are of
questions.explanation: LED stands for the light emitting diode. It consists of two lead one longer and other shorter. The longer lead is attached to the positive terminal while shorter with the negative terminal. A light-emitting diode (LED) is a two-lead semiconductor light source. It is a p–n junction diode that emits light when activated. When a suitable voltage is applied to the leads, electrons are able to recombine with electron holes within the device, releasing energy in the form of photons.

questions.question: During electrolytic refining of metal M, what would you take as cathode?
questions.explanation: During electrolytic refining, a thin strip of pure metal is used as a cathode and thick impure metal as the anode. So, during refining of metal M, the electrode joined at the cathode is made up of a thin sheet of pure metal M.

questions.question: When iron nail is placed in Blue colored solution of copper sulphate it change to green due to
questions.explanation:
It changes to green due to displacement of copper by iron. This happens because a highly reactive metal can displace a less reactive metal from its salt solution such a reaction is known as displacement reaction.
Copper Sulphate(blue) + Iron {tex}\to{/tex} Iron Sulphate(green) + Copper 
CuSO4 + Fe {tex}\to{/tex} FeSO4 + Cu
While,
Iron Sulphate + Copper {tex}\to{/tex} No Reaction

questions.question: It is not safe to carry out electrical repair works outdoor during rain because

options.option: Rainwater is a good conductor of electricity
It become difficult to grips the tools

questions.explanation: Rainwater contains dissolved salts, therefore rainwater can conduct electricity and electrician may get electric shock. Hence electric repair work can not take place for safety precautions.

questions.question: Which property of LED makes it more popular as source of light?

options.option: It consumes less electricity and lasts longer
It is available in many colours and shapes
It is available in market at very low cost

questions.explanation: An LED lamp is a light-emitting diode (LED) product that is assembled into a lamp (or light bulb) for use in lighting fixtures. LED lamps have a lifespan and electrical efficiency which are several times greater than incandescent lamps, and are significantly more efficient than most fluorescent lamps, with some chips able to emit more than 300 lumens per watt. Like incandescent lamps and unlike most fluorescent lamps (e.g. tubes and compact fluorescent lamps or CFLs), LEDs come to full brightness without need for a warm-up time; the life of fluorescent lighting is also reduced by frequent switching on and off.

questions.question: Which of the following is concern over the use of CFL
questions.explanation: CFLs (compact fluorescent lamps )  consume less electricity and lead to less emissions from power plants, they do contain mercury – a hazardous material. It is a highly toxic substance. Overall, in terms of mercury emissions,  compact fluorescent lamps are beneficial to the environment compared to other lamps.

questions.question: Rainwater and distilled water are bad conductors of electricity but rainwater sometimes conduct electricity due to
questions.explanation: Rainwater sometimes conduct electricity due to presence of acids in it. Distilled water which is a pure form of water is neither acidic nor basic in nature so distilled water does not dissolve into ions since conduction of electricity requires free ions, so distilled water does not conduct electricity. Rainwater being an impure form of water, contains many ionic species. These ions are responsible for electrical coductivity of rainwater.

questions.question: Which type of tester is most suitable for testing flow of very low current?
questions.explanation: As it cause deflection when current is very low and it cannot be tested by LED type and other kinds of testers.

questions.question: Which type of effect is produced by electric current passes through a conducting solution?
questions.explanation: The passage of an electric current through a conducting solution causes chemical reactions. As result bubbles of gas are formed, deposits of metal on electrodes may be seen and changes of color of solution may occur depending on what solution and electrode are used, these are some of the chemical effects of electric current.

questions.question: When electric current is passed through a conducting solution, there is a change of colour of the solution. This indicates
questions.explanation: The passage of an electric current through a conducting solution causes chemical reactions. As a result, the change of color of solution occurs. This indicates the chemical effect of the current.

questions.question: Which one of the following solutions will not conduct electricity?
questions.explanation: Vegetable oil will not conduct electricity because it does not make ions easily.

questions.question: Which of the following metals is used in electroplating to make objects appear shining?
questions.explanation: Chromium is used for electroplating to make objects appear shining because chromium has a shiny appearance and it resists scratches.

questions.question: The vessel used for electric decomposition-
questions.explanation: A voltmeter is an instrument used for measuring electric potential difference between two points in an electric circuit. It is connected in parallel. It usually has a high resistance so that it takes negligible current from the circuit.

questions.question: The positive plates in voltage cells are of
questions.explanation: A simple voltaic cell consists of Copper and Zinc rods partially immersed in a liquid electrolyte. The Copper electrode is the positive terminal of the cell, and the Zinc is the negative terminal.

questions.question: A 55 Watt lamp is combined with the main wire of 220 volt, The current in the lamp will be:
questions.explanation: I = {tex}\frac PV{/tex} = {tex}\frac {55}{220}{/tex} = 0.25 ampere

questions.question: By which apparatus or machine an electric current is measured from the following?
questions.explanation: Ammeter, instrument for measuring either direct or alternating electric current, in amperes.

questions.question: Inverter is a machine, which

options.option: Converts AC voltage in DC voltage and Converts DC voltage in AC voltage

questions.explanation: An inverter converts the DC electricity from sources such as batteries or fuel cells to AC electricity. The electricity can be at any required voltage; in particular it can operate AC equipment designed for mains operation, or rectified to produce DC at any desired voltage.

questions.question: Chemical effect of current is not used in
questions.explanation: Chemical effect of current is not used in electrotyping.

questions.question: It is used to make permanent magnet
questions.explanation: Steel is used to make the permanent magnet because when steel bar is magnetised the domains change their shape due to the realignment of the dipoles having their axes parallel and in the same direction and this realignment of the dipoles within the domains is permanent. Hence, the steel possess permanent magnetism when magnetised. Whereas in iron bar also, the same thing happens when magnetised but when the cause of the induced magnetism is removed, the mutual repulsive force between the like poles of the parallel dipoles disturbs and disarranges them. Hence, iron bar lost magnetism.

questions.question: Which one does not involve chemical effect of electric current?

options.option: Movement of needle of magnet compass when placed near an electric wire carrying current

questions.question: Two thermocol balls held close to each other move away because of ________ force.
questions.explanation: Two Thermocol balls held close to each other move away from each other, which is because of electrostatic force. The balls having similar charges move away due to repulsion between similar charges.

Tap water solution is a good conductor of electricity due to the presence of dissolved salt in it. Vinegar when dissolved in water, it breaks into acetate ions which conduct electricity.

questions.question:

options.option: Both Copper depositing at cathode and Copper going into solution at anode

questions.explanation: At anode, {tex}{SO}_4^{2-}{/tex} is collected which reacts with copper anode to form copper sulphate compound. It gets dissolved in the solution and copper deposits at cathode.

questions.question:
Statement 1: The conductivity of an electrolyte is very low as compared to a metal at room temperature.
Statement 2: The density of free ions in electrolyte is much smaller than density of free electrons in metals.

options.option: Both statements 1 and 2 are true and statement 2 is the correct explanation of statement 1.
Statement 1 is true but statement 2 is false.
Both statements 1 and 2 are true but statement 2 is not the correct explanation of statement 1.

questions.explanation: In metals, large number of free electrons are present but in solution, number of ions present are much less. So conductivity of solution is much less than that of metals.

questions.question: Impure sample of copper can be purified by keeping the impure sample as
questions.explanation: Impure sample is used as anode. When current passes, copper from anode starts getting dissolved in the solution of electrolyte. The impurity is left behind at anode.

questions.question: What do we get at anode if we pass current through melted NaCl?
questions.explanation: Liquified sodium chloride conducts current. Chloride ion (Cl-) which is negatively charged go to anode (which is linked with positive polarity of the battery).

questions.explanation:
Sodium ion is converted into sodium metal atom by accepting electron at cathode.
Na+ + e- = Na (metal)

questions.question: According to Arrhenius theory of acid and base

Acids are substances which produce hydrogen ions in solution
Acids are substances which produce hydroxide ions in solution
Neither Acids are substances which produce hydrogen ions in solution nor Acids are substances which produce hydroxide ions in solution.

questions.question: In the process of electroplating, the sample which is to be electroplated is used as

questions.question: Which of the following is the application of electroplating?

options.option: Increasing the shelf life of food kept in electroplated tin cans

questions.explanation: Tin is less reactive than iron, so keeps the food safe for longer time.

questions.question: In electrolysis, the amount of mass deposited or liberated at an electrode is directly proportional to
questions.explanation: Amount of metal deposited at cathode is directly proportional to the amount of charge.

questions.question: Galvanisation protects metallic objects (iron) because
questions.explanation: Zinc is more reactive than iron. Zinc forms zinc oxide with molecules of oxygen in air. The thin layer of zinc oxide on galvanized object protects it from further corrosion.

questions.question:
Statement I- Electrolysis of an object is possible only when it is in liquid state.
Statement II- Electrolysis of an object is possible only when it conducts electricity.

options.option: Statement I is true, while II is false.

questions.explanation: Positive charge

questions.question: Electrolysis converts

options.option: Heat energy is converted into chemical energy
There is no conversion of energy in it

questions.explanation: In electrolysis, electrical energy is converted into chemical energy.

  4. Anode is joined with positive terminal
questions.explanation: A cathode is connected to negative terminal of battery, whereas the electrode connected to positive terminal is known as anode.

questions.question: Which of the following is an application of electrolysis?
questions.explanation: The process of electrolysis can be used for purification as well as extraction of metals.

questions.question: What is the use of electroscope?

To detect and test small electric charges
To calculate the amount of electric charge flowing through the conductor in the given interval of time
To test the presence of magnetic field

Either Solution of metal salt on which plating is done or Metal salt solution of the metal to be electroplated

If the North Pole of a bar magnet is taken close to the South Pole of another bar magnet, it attracts and clings. But if the North Pole of a bar magnet is brought near a the North Pole of another bar magnet it repels or moves away.

questions.question: The area where earthquake are likely to occur are called
questions.explanation: Seismic zone is area where chances of occurring earthquake are more than the others. It is the area from where earthquake arises as they are the weak zones of the earth's crust. Since earthquakes are caused by the movement of earth's plates, the boundaries of the plates are the weak zones where earthquakes are most likely to occur.

questions.question: Lightening strike could destroy
questions.explanation: Lightening strike could destroy life and property if it falls on building or tree because a flash of lighting carries a lot of electric energy which occurs due to interaction of free flowing ions causing high temperature and which is sufficient to cause fire.

questions.question: The outermost layer of earth is called
questions.explanation: The outermost layer of earth is called crust. It is that layer which is compartively made of lighter rocks than the mantle and supports life on earth. The middle layer is called mantle and innermost layer is called core.

questions.explanation: Losing static electricity as charges move off an object is called electric discharge. Discharged object is electrically neutral and doesn't allow electricity to pass.

questions.question: An object becomes charged when the atoms in the object gain or lose
questions.explanation: An object becomes charged when the atoms in the object gain or lose electron. The object that gains electron becomes negatively charged and one who loose electron becomes positively charged.

questions.question: A device used to test whether an object is carrying charge or not is called
questions.explanation: Electroscope is a device for detecting electric on an object. By using an electroscope, we can tell whether an object is electrically charged or not.

questions.question: During thunderstorm which action may be done?

options.option: Switching ON and OFF of electrical switches

questions.explanation: During lightning and thunderstorm we should avoid using telephone, switching electrical switches as all these are connected through wires from where free electrons can easily pass. Mobile phone can be used.

questions.question: To protect tall building from the damage of lightning, what should be done?

options.option: Have a roof top garden with tall tree

questions.explanation: To protect the tall building from the damage of lightning , lightning conductor should be installed at the top of the building so that it can attract free flowing ions and pass them down to the earth. If the lightning strikes,it will hit the top of the lightning conductor rather than the building and gets discharged safely into the ground through the buried metal plate.

questions.question: During thunderstorm it is safer to
questions.explanation: During thunderstorm it is safer to stand in open field as tall tree and building may attract the lightning towards itself because they carry positive charge.

questions.question: Seismograph is an instrument used to record
questions.explanation: Seismograph is an instrument used to measure and record the magnitude of an earthquake in terms of the shock waves it produces. It helps in locating the epicentre of earthquake.

questions.question: A wind vane is used to find the
questions.explanation: Wind vane is an instrument used to find the direction of wind at a place. It is made of arrows pointing towards different directions and rotates freely. The arrow of the wind vane moves until it points to the direction from which the wind is blowing.

questions.question: A balloon, when taken near candle, burst because
questions.explanation: A candle flame produces heat. When a balloon is taken near candle, the heat causes the air molecules inside the balloon expands that leads to bursting of balloon due to high pressure inside it. The heated air exerts pressure on the walls of the balloon and due to uneven expansion the ballon bursts.

questions.question: In Japan cyclone is known as
questions.explanation: Cyclones a huge revolving storm caused by very  high speed winds blowing around a central area of very low pressure in the atmosphere along with rainfall. A cyclone is called "typhoon" in Japan and Philippines.

questions.question: Dark funnel shaped cloud reaches to earth from sky are called
questions.explanation: A tornado is a violent storm with a column of rapidly rotating winds, having the appearance of a dark, funnel-shaped cloud that reaches from the sky to the ground. It causes much damage from the sheer force of its high speed winds.

questions.question: Rapid volume of air due to lightning cause
questions.explanation: During lightning, the air gets heated up and expands suddenly. This rapid expansion in the volume of air sends a disturbance through the air producing loud sound. This loud sound is heard as thunder.

questions.question: Natural disaster can not be predicted as
questions.explanation: Natural disaster cannot be predicted in advance because natural disaster occurs suddenly although a warning or predication of its occurrence is nowdays is given with the help of the pictures sent by weather satellites.

questions.question: It is easier to row a boat in the direction of wind because

options.option: Fresh air help the sail in easy breathing
Air exerts pressure in direction of sailing

questions.explanation: It is easier to row a boat in direction of wind because the wind exerts a force on us in the same direction in which our boat is moving and makes our boat to move easily and faster.

questions.question: Which among the following is not a natural phenomenon?
questions.explanation: Earthquake, cyclone and lightning are natural phenomenon as it occurs naturally but cooking is a man-made process.

questions.question: Wind blows faster when

options.option: Pressure difference between two places is less
Pressure difference between two places is more

questions.explanation: Moving air is called wind. Wind blows faster when pressure difference between two places is more. Air moves from the regions of high pressure to regions of low pressure in the atmosphere.

questions.question: Which one of the following is most destructive?
questions.explanation: Earthquake, Tornado, Typhoon, and storm all are destructive but an earthquake of more than 7 magnitudes measured on the Richter scale is most destructive, causing huge losses to life and property.

questions.question: Increase in level of carbon dioxide in air causes
questions.explanation: Carbon dioxide gas is a greenhouse gas. Increase in the level of carbon dioxide gas in the atmosphere leads to greenhouse effect that causes rise in earth's temperature, which leads to global warming.

questions.question: The boundaries of earth’s plates, where earthquake tends to occurs are called
questions.explanation: The boundaries of earth’s plates where earthquake tends to occurs are called fault zone. In this zone there are about a dozen large,irregularly shaped plates which slide over, under and past each other. Movement of these plates occurs in this zone causing earthquakes.

questions.question: Lightening occurs due to
questions.explanation: Lightening is a discharge of electricity. A single stroke of lightning can heat the air around it to 30,000°C (54,000°F)! This extreme heating causes the air to expand explosively fast. The expansion creates a shock wave that turns into a booming sound wave, known as thunder.

questions.question: Windmill is used to
questions.explanation: Windmill is used to generate electricity when the vanes of the windmill moves with the speed more than 15km/hr. The energy made by windmills can be used in many ways like it can be used to grind grains,pump water and sawing wood.

questions.question: Which state in India is badly affected by cyclone recently?
questions.explanation: Orissa is a coastal state in India that is badly affected by cyclone. Bihar, Jharkhand and Karnataka is not affected by cyclone as they are not situated near the coastline but far away from the sea.

questions.question: High speed winds are accompanied by
questions.explanation: High speed winds are accompanied by reduced air pressure. Winds blows faster if pressure difference between the two regions is more. Air moves from the regions of high pressure to the regions of low air pressure.

questions.question: Which of the following is not due to pressure change?
questions.explanation: Earthquake occurs due to movement in tectonic plates of earth crust, volcanic eruptions, or by explosions. Earthquake is not due to pressure change.

questions.question: If a storm is accompanied by lightning, we should not take shelter under a high tree because

options.option: It has more chance of falling thunder

questions.explanation: If a storm is accompanied by lightning, we should not take shelter under a high tree because When a cloud passes over a tall tree, it induces an opposite charge on them. If the charge built is large, it leads to an electrical discharge in the form of a lightning strike, which may shatter the tree or set it on fire causing severe burns and death of the person standing under it.

questions.question: Earthquake activities are monitored by using
questions.explanation: Earthquake activities cannot be monitored as it occurs suddenly without any sign. Satellite can monitor wind and cloud activities only.

questions.question: Instrument to measure waves of the earthquake is called
questions.explanation: Seismograph is an instrument which detects and records the intensity of seismic waves generated by an earthquake. The graphical record of the intensity of seismic waves is called seismograms.

questions.question: Rearranging charges in uncharged metal object without contact with charged object is called
questions.explanation: Rearranging charges in uncharged metal object without contact with charged object is called induction because it does not required any contact but charge jumps from high end to the lower end.

questions.question: The like charges

options.option: In contrast to the attractive force between two objects with opposite charges, two objects that are of like charge will repel each other. That is, a positively charged object will exert a repulsive force upon a second positively charged object. This repulsive force will push the two objects apart.

questions.explanation: Repel each other

questions.question: Which natural disaster is very rare in India?
questions.explanation: Thunderstorm, cyclone, and earthquake are common natural disaster in India but tornado is common in Japan.

questions.question: Two objects repel each other. What charges might have?

options.option: Both may be having either negative or both positive charges.

questions.explanation: Objects with same charges repel each other i.e. Like charges repel each other. A positive charge repels another positive charge and a negative charge repels another negative charge.

questions.question: What makes the balloon inflated when placed in hot water?
questions.explanation: When a balloon is placed in hot water it gets inflated as an increase in temperature inside the balloon leads to an increase in air pressure inside the balloon and the volume of air on heating and it occupies a bigger space which leads to expansion and the balloon inflates.

questions.question: Which of the following is negatively charged?
questions.explanation: The electron is a subatomic particle that carries a negative electric charge. An electron has a mass that is approximately 1/1836 that of the proton.

questions.question: Electrical charges produced by rubbing are called
questions.explanation: When two insulators are rubbed vigorously over each other, electrical charge is produced. This kind of charge is called as static charge which gets activated due to rubbing.

questions.question: What happen when glass rod is rubbed with silk and attain positive charge?
questions.explanation: When glass rod is rubbed with silk, glass rod becomes positively charged due to movement of electron from glass rod to silk. The materials like glass lose electrons more easily and hence gets positively charged on rubbing . On the other hand materials like silk gain electrons more easily and hence gets negatively charged during rubbing. Whether an object will lose or gain electrons during rubbing depends on the nature of the material of the object.

questions.question: Flash point term is related to
questions.explanation: Flash point, the lowest temperature at which a liquid (usually a petroleum product) will form a vapour in the air near its surface that will “flash,” or briefly ignite, on exposure to an open flame. The flash point is a general indication of the flammability or combustibility of a liquid. Flash point term is related to study of climate.

questions.question: Lightning is seen little earlier than thunder sound because
questions.explanation: Light travels at the speed of 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 108 m/s while sound travels at a speed of 332 m/s i.e., light travels much faster than sound that is why lightning is seen earlier and thunder is heard later.

questions.question: Which of the following is not caused by an earthquake?
questions.explanation: Earthquake may cause floods, landslide and Tsunami but not the thunder because earthquake occurs due to movement of the earth plates whereas thunder is caused due to striking of clouds.

questions.question: The point at which a movement occurred which triggered an earthquake is called
questions.explanation: Epicentre is the point at which a movement occurred which triggered an earthquake. It is the point on earth's surface directly above the focus. Focus is the place inside the earth's crust where the earthquake is generated.

questions.question: Electrons and protons attracted each other because

options.option: They are negatively and positively charged and opposite charges attract each other

questions.explanation: Electron have negative charge and proton have positive charge, opposite charges attract each other, so Electrons and protons attracted each other

questions.question: Waves produced on the surface of earth by tremors are called
questions.explanation: The waves generated on the surface of earth due to sudden movement in a part of earth' s crust are called seismic waves or shock waves. Seismograph is an instrument used to detect and record obtain the intensity of seismic waves generated by an earthquake.

questions.question: In ancient times, people thought that earthquake and lightning is due to
questions.explanation: In ancient time, people thought that earthquake and lightning g is due to wrath of God visiting them. Later it gets clear that it is due to moving plates of the earth's crust which slide past or collide with one another earthquakes occur and lightning is an electric spark in the sky between oppositely charged clouds.

questions.question: Which of the following cannot be charged easily by friction?
questions.explanation: Only non-conducting materials can be easily charged by friction. Copper is a highly conducting material. Therefore, a copper rod cannot be charged easily by friction.

questions.question: When a glass rod is rubbed with a piece of silk cloth the rod

options.option: becomes negatively charged while the cloth has a positive charge.
and the cloth both acquire positive charge
and the cloth both acquire negative charge.
becomes positively charged while the cloth has a negative charge.

questions.explanation: When an object is charged by rubbing it against another object, the two objects get oppositely charged. By convention, it is considered that the charged acquired by the glass rod is positive and charged acquired by the cloth is negative. Therefore, the rod becomes positively charged and the cloth becomes negatively charged.

questions.question: An electroscope is a device that is used to find if an object is
questions.explanation: An electroscope is a device, which is used to test whether an object is charged or not.lt consists of closely placed two metallic strips. When both the strips are charged with similar charges, they repel each other and become wide open.

questions.question: Electric current is to be passed from one body to another. For this purpose, the two bodies must be joined by
questions.explanation: Two bodies must be joined by any conducting wire, i.e. copper wire for the flow of current.

questions.question: The movement of the earth’s plates causes
questions.explanation: When the earth's plates brush past one another or undergo collision, causes an earthquake. An earthquake can cause huge damage to buildings, dams, etc.

questions.question: Two charged objects are brought close to each other. Choose the most appropriate statement from the following options:

options.option: they may attract or repel depending on the type of charges they carry

questions.explanation: Two charged objects may attract, if they carry opposite charges, while they repel if they carry similar charges.

questions.question: Which of the following is not likely to cause Tsunami?
questions.explanation: Tsunami refers to the powerful sea waves generated due to the disturbance under the sea. It is not likely to occur in the case of lightning, because lightning is an electric spark caused by the accumulation of charges in clouds.

questions.question: The earth’s plate responsible for causing earthquakes is
questions.explanation: An earthquake is a sudden shaking or trembling of the earth which lasts for a very short time. It is caused by the disturbance deep down inside the uppermost layer of the earth called the crust.

questions.question:

  1. Seismic Zone
  2. Mantle
  3. Fault Zone
  4. Inner Core

The boundaries of the earth’s plate are known as
questions.explanation: The boundaries of the Earth's plate are associated with areas of seismic activity and fault lines. A 'Seismic Zone' refers to regions prone to earthquakes, which commonly occur at plate boundaries. A 'Fault Zone' is an area with multiple faults, which are directly linked to plate movement. The 'Mantle' and 'Inner Core' are layers within the Earth's interior and not related to plate boundaries. Therefore, the correct terms are (i) Seismic Zone and (iii) Fault Zone.

questions.explanation: The richter scale is scale of numbers used to tell the power (or magnitude) of earthquakes. The richter scale calculates an earthquake's magnitude from the amplitude of the earthquake's largest seismic wave recorded by a seismograph.

Neither Process of transferring charge from a negatively charged body to the earth nor Process of transferring charge from a positively charged body to the earth.

questions.explanation: The process of transferring an electric charge from a charged object to the earth is called earthing.

If both Assertion and Reason are correct, but Reason is not the correct explanation of Assertion.
If both Assertion and Reason are correct and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion.
If Assertion is correct but Reason in incorrect.

questions.explanation: Earthquake occurs due to the movement of tectonic plates. Then pressure builds up. When pressure is released, it results in earthquake.
