questions.question: Lens used in spectacles for correction of defects of vision is:
questions.explanation: The convex and concave lenses are used to correct the defective vision. Myopia can be corrected by placing a suitable concave lens in the line of sight. Hypermetropia can be corrected by placing a suitable convex lens in the line of sight. To correct presbyopia, a bifocal lens is used.

questions.question: In regular reflection, the angle of incidence is always equal to:
questions.explanation: The Law of Reflection states that the angle of the incident light ray is equal to the angle of the reflected light ray.

questions.question: For a plane mirror angle between the incident ray and normal is 45o than the angle of reflection will be:
questions.explanation: The angle of incidence is always equal to the angle of reflection of light. If the angle between mirror and incident ray is 45o than angle of reflection will be 45o.

questions.question: The angle of incidence is equal to the angle of incidence:
questions.explanation: The angle of incidence is always equal to the angle of reflection of light. If the angle between mirror and incident ray is 90o than the angle of reflection will be 90o.

questions.question: A ray of light is incident on a plane mirror making an angle of 30 degrees with the mirror surface. The angle of reflection for this ray of light will be:
questions.explanation:
The ray of light strikes the mirror at an angle of 30, which means it will make an angle of 60o (90o – 30o) with the normal.
So the angle of incidence = 60o
According to the law of reflection, the angle of incidence is equal to the angle of reflection. 
Angle of reflection = 60o

questions.question: The rays of light that fall on the mirror surface is called ________.
questions.explanation: The ray of light which falls on the surface of a body is called an incident ray. The angle of incidence is always equal to the angle of reflection of light.

questions.question: The angle between mirror and incident ray is 50° than the angle of reflection will be:
questions.explanation: The angle of incidence is always equal to the angle of reflection of light. The angle between mirror and incident ray is 50o than the angle of reflection will be 50o.

questions.question: The lens present in the human eye is made up of:
questions.explanation: Lens transparent body enclosed in an elastic capsule. The lens present in the human eye is made up of proteins and water.

questions.question: The perpendicular to the reflecting surface is called ________.
questions.explanation: Normal is a line drawn perpendicular to the surface of the mirror from the point where incident ray meets the surface of the mirror.

questions.question: Rainbow is formed due to
questions.explanation: Rainbow is formed due to the dispersion of light by water droplets after the shower in the opposite direction of Sun. A rainbow is a meteorological phenomenon that is caused by reflection, refraction and dispersion of light in water droplets resulting in a spectrum of light appearing in the sky. Rainbows caused by sunlight always appear in the section of sky directly opposite the sun.

questions.question: Splitting of white light into seven colours is called
questions.explanation: The phenomenon of splitting of white light into seven colours is called dispersion of light to obtain spectrum. These colors are often observed as light passes through a triangular prism. Upon passage through the prism, the white light is separated into its component colors - red, orange, yellow, green, blue, and violet.

questions.question: Splitting of white rays of light into seven colours is called
questions.explanation: Splitting of white ray of light into seven colours when passes through a prism is called dispersion of light. Different colours of light bend through different angles with respect to the incident ray as they pass through the prism, thus emerge along different paths and seen as dispersed

questions.question: ___________ help us to see the objects.
questions.explanation: Light enable us to see the objects around us. During day sun generates sufficient amount of light to illuminate the objects. Reflected light after incidenting on the surface enters the eye to form image on retina which helps to see the objects.

questions.question: In human eyes, image is formed on
questions.explanation: In human eyes, image is formed on retina. Retina acts as a screen in human eye on which inverted image is formed which is passed to brain through optical nerve and it interprets the image.

questions.question: Object that emit light is called
questions.explanation: The objects that emit light of their own are called luminous object. A luminous object is one that gives off light or that glows of its own. Sun, stars, bulb, tube, etc are example of luminous object.

questions.question: Which type of lens is present in our eyes?
questions.explanation: Human eye contains transparent biconvex lens made up of crystalline protein which is elastic and flexible through which ray of light enters and focussed on the retina to form real image

questions.question: Speed of light is fastest in
questions.explanation: Speed of light is fastest in air and vacuum. Speed of light is almost 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 108m/s in vacuum /air. Speed of light decreases with increase in density of the medium. Light travels slowest in diamond.

questions.question: Band of seven colours obtained on screen after dispersion is called
questions.explanation: The band of seven colours obtained on screen when white light passes through a prism after dispersion is called spectrum of white light. These colours are arranged as Violet, Indigo, Blue, Green, Yellow, Orange, and Red

questions.question: What is the name of object through which light can pass?
questions.explanation: The object through which light can pass easily is called transparent object. Glass, water, and thin plastic sheets are transparent object. When light encounters transparent material almost all of it passes directly through them.

questions.question: Rainbow is a
questions.explanation: Rainbow is a natural spectrum that is caused by reflection, refraction, and dispersion of light in water droplets suspended in air to form spectrum of light appearing in the sky. It always appear in the sky directly opposite to the sun.

questions.question: Braille system is used by
questions.explanation: Braille system is used by blind people to read and write. It consists of raised dots or bumps that are felt by blind people with a finger to read and write . It is named after Louis Braille, the French man who invented it.

questions.question: _________ refers to collection of rays.
questions.explanation: Beam is a collection of rays It is a directional projection of light energy radiating from a light source. A beam of light is visible only when part of it is scattered by the objects

questions.question: When a ray of incident light strikes the surface of a plane mirror at an angle of 60o with the mirror. The angle of reflection is:
questions.explanation:
Here, the angle to the mirror surface (the angle between the incident ray and the mirror) = 60o
Therefore, the angle of incidence (the angle between the incident ray and the normal) = 90o - 60o = 30o
Now, we know that according to the law of reflection, the angle of incidence is equal to the angle of reflection.
Therefore, the angle of reflection will be 30o.

questions.question: What makes the object visible?
questions.explanation: Light makes the object visible. After reflection of a light from the surface of object enters the human eye and form image on the retina which helps to see the object. In absence of light, we are not able to view the object present in our surrounding.

questions.question: Angle of incidence is equal to the angle of reflection.
questions.explanation: During reflection of light, the angle of reflection is always equal to the angle of incidence according to the law of light.

questions.question: The colour of eyes is due to colour of
questions.explanation: The colour of eyes is due to colour pigmentation present in the diaphragm of iris present around the pupil that controls the amount of light entering the eye ball.

questions.question: The object which emits their own light are called
questions.explanation: The object which emits their own light is called luminous bodies. They may be natural or manmade sources of light.

questions.question: Which of the following works on the concept of multiple reflections?
questions.explanation: A kaleidoscope is an optical instrument with two or more reflecting surfaces tilted to each other in an angle, so that one or more objects on one end of the mirrors are seen as a regular symmetrical pattern when viewed from the other end, due to repeated reflection.

questions.question: Speed of light
questions.explanation: Speed of light changes with medium. Light travels faster in rarer medium and slower in denser medium.

questions.question: A drop of water appears like pearl due to
questions.explanation: total internal reflection of light

questions.question: When you stands in front of a small mirror. The image formed will appear to be of
questions.explanation: Plane mirror always forms virtual, erect,  image of same size as object. So, when you stand in front of small mirror image will be of same size.

questions.question: Which property of light enables us to see our face in mirror?
questions.explanation: Reflection of light enables us to see our face in mirror as mirror forms image behind the mirror which is virtual and erect.

questions.question: Which of these explain the image of building or tree in river or pond?
questions.explanation: The image of building or tree in river or pond is formed by reflection of light form the water surface of ponds or river which acts as a reflector.

questions.question: A red rose and a white rose are placed in front of a source of light. The colour of the shadow will be

options.option: Red for red rose and white for white rose
Black for red rose and white for white rose

questions.explanation: The colour of the shadow is always black in spite of different object. So, a red rose and white rose always form black colour of shadow.

questions.question: Small patches of sunlight under the tree are the
questions.explanation: The small patches of sunlight under the tree are the image of sun formed by small gaps between tree leaves.

questions.question: When two mirrors are placed parallel to each other
questions.explanation: When two mirrors are placed parallel to each other, large numbers of images are formed due to repeated reflection form opposite side placed mirror.

questions.question: When an object is placed at a distance of 15 cm from a plane mirror. The image formed is at distance of

options.option: 15 cm in front of the mirror

questions.explanation: Plane mirror form virtual, erect and image of same size behind the mirror. The object distance is equal to image distance. Object placed at a distance of 15 cm form image at distance of 15 cm behind the mirror.

questions.question: A narrow beam of light is made up of
questions.explanation: A light ray is a line (straight or curved) that is perpendicular to the light's wavefronts; its tangent is collinear with the wave vector

questions.question: We can see an object when it

options.option: Refract or absorb the rays of light

questions.explanation: When we see an object when it reflects or emit rays of light. Reflected light enters the eyes and image is formed on the retina.

questions.question: The path of the light is visible in
questions.explanation: The path of the light is visible in presence of dispersing medium like dust particle or prism or other substances in the medium.

questions.question: Objects are visible, when reflected light
questions.explanation: Objects visible to us only when ray of light enters our eye after reflection forming an image on the retina

questions.question: Which mirror always forms virtual, erect, and image of the same size. The image is laterally inverted too.
questions.explanation: Plane mirror always forms virtual, erect, and image of the same size. The image is laterally inverted too.

questions.question: The image formed on cinema screen is an example of
questions.explanation: The image formed on cinema screen is an example of real image as it is obtained on screen. The inverted image is re-inverted by the projector to appear erect.

questions.question: Normal is
questions.explanation: Perpendicular at the point of incident of light to the surface is called normal. Normal is an imaginary line.

questions.question: Videography and cinematography is based on principle of
questions.explanation: Videography and cinematography is based on principle of persistence of vision in which image formed on retina remain for 1/16th second. So, picture moving at the speed with 24 frames in a second appear to be connected with each other.

questions.question: Different colours are due to ability of the object to
questions.explanation: Different colours are due to ability of the object to reflect the rays of light. The pigments present in objects absorb some light of the required wavelength and reflect the others.

questions.question: Danger signals are made red coloured because red light
questions.explanation: Danger signals are made red coloured because red light have longer wavelength that scatter less. Red coloured light can be seen from distance due to less scattering.

questions.question: Sky appear blue coloured during day because

options.option: Blue coloured light can be scattered by small particles
Blue colour of light is visible in sunlight only
Sun light is made up of blue light only

questions.explanation: Sky appear blue coloured during day because blue coloured light can be scattered by small particles present in atmosphere due to its shorter wavelength.

questions.question: Which of the following phenomenon is based on the persistence of vision principle?
questions.explanation: Cinematography is based on the principle of persistence of vision. Colour blindness, hemophilia, and myopia are defects of vision.

questions.question: A person is not able to view the nearby object clearly but can see far object distinctly. He is suffering from
questions.explanation: A person suffering from Hypermetropia or farsightedness is not able to view the nearby object clearly but can see a far object distinctly. It can be corrected using a convex lens of proper power.

questions.question: The phenomenon of bending of rays of light when passes from one medium to another medium is called
questions.explanation: The phenomenon of bending of rays of light when passes from one medium to another medium where its speed is different is called refraction of light. In Refraction of light when it passes from a rarer medium to a denser medium it bends towards the normal to the boundary between the two mediums.

questions.question: Which type of mirror is used as shop security image?
questions.explanation: Convex mirror is used as shop security mirror because it covers a larger area and forms an erect image of the object to view a larger section of the shop.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of virtual image?
questions.explanation: The image formed by a plane mirror is always virtual, erect, and of the same size as the object. Image formed on retina, pinhole camera, and cinema screen are real images.

questions.question: Diffused reflection of light

options.option: does not obey the laws of reflection
May obey or may not obey laws of reflection

questions.explanation: Diffused reflection of light does not obeys the law of reflection of light as reflected rays are not parallel to each other.

questions.question: The elementary particles that defines light is the
questions.explanation: a particle representing a quantum of light or other electromagnetic radiation. A photon carries energy proportional to the radiation frequency but has zero rest mass.

questions.question: Light consists of electromagnetic waves that

options.option: Do not require material medium for propagation

questions.explanation: Light consists of electromagnetic waves that do not require material medium for its propagation. It can travel in vacuum.

questions.question: The defects of eyes due to absence of cones in retina is
questions.explanation: Colour blindness is the defect of vision due to absence of cones in retina. Cones are responsible to see the colour of the object. Some people do not contain certain cones which enable them to see colour.

questions.question: White light is composed of:
questions.explanation: The cause of dispersion of light is that white light consists of seven different colours, and each colour has a different angle of deviation. Therefore, on passing through the prism different colours deviate through different angles. Hence the seven colours of white light separate and form a spectrum. Out of seven colours, the red colour deviates the least, and hence the red colour is present at the top of the spectrum. On the other hand, the violet colour deviates most that is why violet colour is present at the lower end of the spectrum.

questions.question: Image formed by a plane mirror is

options.option: Real at the surface of the mirror and enlarged.
Real, behind the mirror and of the same size as the object.
Virtual, behind the mirror and of the same size as the object.

questions.explanation: Image formed by a plane mirror is virtual, behind the mirror and of the same size as the object.The image formed by a plane mirror is of the same size as the object. The image is formed behind the mirror. The image cannot be obtained on a screen. Hence, it is a virtual image.

questions.question: Which one shows lateral inversion?
questions.explanation: "Lateral inversion" means the apparent reversal of the mirror image's left and right when compared with the object. If you stand in front of a plane mirror and raise your right hand, your mirror image shows your left hand. The size of the image is equal to the size of the object. Plane mirror, concave mirror, and convex mirror show a lateral inversion of light.

questions.question: Part of the eye which controls the light entering is called

questions.explanation: We can see a non-luminous object (the object that does not produce its own light) when light reflected by the object enters our eyes.

questions.question: A person effected by colour blindners feels problem to identify which two colours?

questions.question: A dark muscular structure of human eye which gives it its distinctive colour is
questions.explanation: The iris is the area of the eye that contains the pigment which gives the eye its colour,

questions.question: The angle between incident ray and reflected ray is 120°, then angle of incidence is
questions.explanation:
According to the question,
Given,

{tex}\angle A O N+\angle N O B=120^{\circ}{/tex}
According to law of reflection,
Angle of incidence = Angle of reflection
Therefore, {tex}\angle A O N=\angle N O B{/tex}
{tex}\angle A O N=\frac{120}{2}=60^{\circ}{/tex}
Therefore, the angle of incidence = 60o

questions.question: Which of the following has maximum energy?
questions.explanation: Blue light has shorter waves, with wavelengths between about 450 and 495 nanometers. Red light has longer waves, with wavelengths around 620 to 750 nm. Blue light has a higher frequency and carries more energy than red light.

questions.question: In eye donation, which part of donor’s eye is transplanted?

questions.explanation: The reflected ray will make an angle of 45° with IInd mirror

questions.explanation: For a normal person, the least distance of distinct vision, that is, the minimum distance for viewing an object comfortably and clearly is 25 cm.

questions.explanation: The image of running man in the mirror will appear to move at the same rate i.e. at 2 ms-1. So in 5 seconds, the image will be nearer by distance of 10 m.

questions.explanation: In both types of reflections, either regular or irregular, laws of reflection are obeyed.

questions.question: In periscope, we use mirrors inclined at
questions.explanation: In periscope, two reflecting plane mirrors both of which are inclined at 45° with the vertical are used. Each of the mirrors, produces a deviation of 90° in the direction of ray making net deviation equal to zero. The incident ray and emergent ray become parallel to each other.

questions.question: Power of accommodation of an eye is the

questions.explanation: Power of accommodation is the ability of eye to change the focal length of eye lens, so that both near or far off objects can be seen comfortably.

Both Viewing continuously something and Effect of vision remaining in action in brain for a short time.

questions.explanation: When we view an object, the impression of viewing lasts for some time in our brain. During that period we cannot view another object even if another object comes before our eyes. This is called persistence of vision.

questions.explanation: In case of regular reflection, all the reflected rays are parallel, so light acquires high intensity, producing glare in the eye.

The incident ray after reflection from second mirror returns back on its previous path. That means ray of light is falling on the second mirror perpendicular to it. Here {tex}\angle{/tex}OCB = 90°;
{tex}\angle{/tex}CBO = 40°. So {tex}\angle{/tex}{tex}\theta{/tex} - 90° - 40° = 50°

Here, {tex}\theta{/tex} = 120o
So, number of image formed = {tex}=\frac{360^{\circ}}{\theta^{\circ}}=\frac{360^{\circ}}{120^{\circ}}{/tex} = 3

Since Number of images formed = {tex}\frac{360}{\theta}-1{/tex}
with the decrease in angle between two mirrors, the number of images formed increases.

Let the point of reflection on the mirror be at a distance x from A.

{tex}\Delta{/tex}OAC and {tex}\Delta{/tex}O'BC are similar triangle so. {tex}\frac{O A}{A C}=\frac{O^{\prime} B}{B C} ; \frac{8}{x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2}{(10-x)}{/tex}
2x = 80 - 8x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 10x = 80 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 8 cm

For a plane mirror the distance between the image and mirror is same as between the object and mirror.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} For a distance of 5 m between Mahima and her image, she should move 2.5 m towards the mirror.

Cover the bowl’s outer side with aluminium foil

questions.question: The Sun,other stars, Moon, planets and other distant objects in space are called
questions.explanation: The Sun, other stars, Moon, planets, and other distant objects in space are called Celestial bodies. Solar system is the sun and planets, Galaxy is group of stars only and Universe is the vast space including galaxies, dust, gas, and all forms of matter.

questions.question: Which is the farthest planet of solar system?
questions.explanation: Neptune

questions.question: Size of moon keeps changing when viewed from earth. This change is called
questions.explanation: The size of moon keeps changing when viewed from earth. This change is called phases of moon. The part of moon on which sunlight falls is visible to us.

questions.question: Sky is not clear in cities due to
questions.explanation: In cities a number of industries and vehicle emits large amount of dust and smoke that makes sky not so clear.

questions.question: What is the time-period of revolution of Halley's Comet:
questions.explanation: Halley’s Comet is visible from earth after about 76 years. This is the time-period of revolution of this comet.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a celestial body?
questions.explanation: Star, moon, and planets are celestial bodies as they are located in sky but artificial satellite made by human beings revolves around the earth in fix orbit for fix interval of time.

questions.question: Star appear small because
questions.explanation: Stars appear small because stars are very far from earth. Star appears as small balls as light from the star reaches to us in very small amount due to distance.

questions.question: ISRO stands for
questions.explanation: ISRO stands for Indian space research organisation. It is the space agency of the Indian government headquartered in Bengaluru, Karnataka.

questions.question: The sun and other stars are huge bodies made up of
questions.explanation: Sun and other stars are huge bodies made up of hot gases. They emit light due to nuclear fusion of hydrogen into Helium.

questions.question: The sun makes life possible by providing us with
questions.explanation: The Sun is the ultimate source of light and heat that supports life. Life is possible on earth due to Sun. The energy required for all kinds of living organism come from Sun.

questions.question: Water in liquid form is found only on
questions.explanation: Water in liquid form is found only on the earth. Water is essential for survival of life. On earth water is present as solid, liquid and gaseous state.

questions.question: The path of planet around the sun is called its
questions.explanation: Planets revolve around the Sun on a fixed path. The path of the planet around the Sun is called its orbits.

questions.question: Sun is
questions.explanation: Sun is a huge ball of gases emitting heat and light due to nuclear fusion. The size of Sun is very much larger than earth

questions.question: The brightest object in clear night sky is
questions.explanation: Moon is the brightest object in clear night sky. Moon reflects the light of sun as it does not have its own light.

questions.question: Moon is the
questions.explanation: Moon is the natural satellite of earth as moon revolves around the sun in a fixed orbit. Moon reflects the light of the Sun.

questions.question: The various shapes of bright part of moon is called
questions.explanation: The various shapes of bright part of the moon is called phases of moon. The shape of the moon keeps changing every day.

questions.question: Which planets has huge red spots
questions.explanation: Jupiter is called red planet due to presence of a huge red spot over it. This planet is easily visible from the earth.

questions.question: The celestial body nearest to the earth is
questions.explanation: Moon is the natural planet of the earth and distance between them is 384,400 kms.

questions.question: Neptune have very low temperature due to
questions.explanation: Neptune is the eighth planet  from the Sun. Due to far distance Neptune have very low temperature as very less light of sun reaches there.

questions.question: A piece of rock which burns as it enters through the Earth's atmosphere is
questions.explanation: A piece of rock which burns as it enters through the earth's atmosphere due to friction is called meteoroids.

questions.question: The first outside orbit of the earth planet is
questions.explanation: Mars

questions.question: Millions of stars together form a
questions.explanation: Million of stars together make a galaxy. Our solar system falls under the milky way galaxy.

questions.question: Small celestial body revolving around a planet is called
questions.explanation: Satellites are small celestial body revolving around a planet. Moon is the natural satellite of earth.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a planet?
questions.explanation: Mars, Saturn and Mercury are planets that revolve around the Sun. Moon is the natural satellite of Earth.

questions.question: Day and night occurs on the earth due to
questions.explanation: Day and night occurs on the earth due to rotation of earth on its own axis. The part facing the sun is day and other part is night.

questions.question: In a solar system, the planets between which the earth lies are
questions.explanation: In solar system, planets revolve around the Sun in a fixed orbit. Venus is the second planet in the solar system, Earth is third and Mars is fourth.

questions.question: Heavenly bodies that emit heat and light throughout the year are called
questions.explanation: Heavenly bodies that emit heat and light throughout the year are called stars. Stars are a huge ball of gases that emit heat and light.

questions.question: The distance travelled by light in one year is called
questions.explanation: The distance travelled by light in one year (around 9,460,000million km) is called light year. Light year is unit of large distance.

questions.question: The number of planets in our solar system is
questions.explanation: There are eight planets in our solar system. They are Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune.

questions.question: The path along which celestial bodies revolve around the Sun is called
questions.explanation: Orbit is a gravitationally curved path of an object about a point in space.

questions.question: Small celestial bodies characterised by small head and a long tail is called
questions.explanation: The comets appear as a body with a small head and long tail because as it approaches the Sun, it warms up and gases evaporate from its surface which forms a glowing tail that streams away from the direction of the Sun. Eg: Halley's Comet.

questions.question: Which property of the pole star makes it useful for sailors?

options.option: It appears to be in a  fixed position.
It is visible during day and night.
It appear to be the closest star to Sun.

questions.explanation: Pole star appears to be a fixed position and is seen as a faint star located directly above the North Pole. Thus pole star is useful for sailors to know the direction.

questions.question: Which of the following is a larger unit of distance?
questions.explanation: Light year is larger unit of distance. One light year is equal to distance travelled by light in one year which is almost 9,460,000  million km.

questions.question: The first Indian satellite to the Moon.
questions.explanation: India's first satellite was launched in 1975 and named  Aryabhata, the prominent Indian astronomer and mathematician of the 5th century bearing the same name. The 360-kilogram satellite built by ISRO was launched by Russia from their Kapustin Yar rocket launch and development site using a Kosmos-3M launch vehicle.

questions.question: Indian astrology is based on the relative position of the
questions.explanation: Indian astrology is based on the relative position of planets in the sky at a particular point of time. In Astrology, there are nine planets or Grahas. These include Mercury, Venus, Mars, Jupiter, Sun, Moon, Saturn Rahu, and Ketu, which are astronomical points formed where the moon's orbit intersects the apparent path of the Sun around the earth.

questions.question: Which planet is called blue planet?
questions.explanation: Earth is called blue planet because earth appear blue from sky as 70%  Earth's surface is covered with liquid water.

questions.question: Rotation of earth on its own axis causes
questions.explanation: We have day and night because the Earth rotates. It spins on its own axis, which is an imaginary line passing through the North and South Poles. The Earth completes one rotation in 24 hours. The Earth's side facing Sun will have day and other having night.

questions.question: Earth appear blue from the sky due to presence of
questions.explanation: Earth appear blue from the sky due to presence of water on earth. About 2/3rd part of earth is surrounded by water which gives blue color.

questions.question: The atmosphere of Uranus contains gases like
questions.explanation: Uranus has hydrogen and helium in the upper layer like the other gas giants. It also has an icy mantle that surrounds its rock and iron core. The upper atmosphere of water, ammonia and methane ice crystals gives Uranus, its distinctive pale blue color.

questions.question: The Sun is a
questions.explanation: The Sun is a star that emits heat and light. Sun appear larger than other stars due to its closeness from earth.

questions.question: Ozone layer around the earth protects it from
questions.explanation: Ozone layer around the earth in the atmosphere prevents us from harmful UV rays. Ozone prevents the harmful ultraviolet rays from entering the atmosphere of earth.

questions.question: Shooting star is actually
questions.explanation: Meteorite is also called shooting star because due to friction of air in atmosphere meteorite starts burning that appear as streak of light in the sky.

questions.question: The number of days between full moon to new moon is
questions.explanation: There is an interval of 15 days between full moon and new moon. After full moon, the size of moon start decreasing and after 15 days on new moon, moon is not visible. A complete cycle from one full moon to the next one is 29.5 days and referred as lunar cycle.

questions.question: Phases of moon occurs due to
questions.explanation: Phases of moon occurs due to shadow of Earth on Moon. As Moon revolves around the Earth and Earth revolves around the Sun, the shape of the Moon appears to change when viewed from the Earth. Different phases of Moon are Full Moon, New Moon, Gibbous Moon, and Crescent Moon.

questions.question: Orbit is the

options.option: Path along which a celestial body revolves

questions.explanation: Orbit is the path along which a celestial body revolves around the sun. Different planets revolve around the Sun in a fixed orbit.

questions.question: The only source of light and heat for all the planets is
questions.explanation: The Sun is a ball of gases (mostly Helium and Hydrogen) that contains 99.8% of the mass of the Solar system. Due to this, massive pressure leading to a nuclear reaction (fusing of helium and hydrogen atoms) which results in the production of intensive heat and light, which is the source of energy for the whole solar system. The temperature at the core of the Sun is around 15,000,0000C and the surface temperature is around 55000C.

questions.question: Venus is the brightest and hottest planets due to
questions.explanation: The presence of carbon dioxide (97%), nitrogen, and clouds of sulphuric acid in Venus's atmosphere trap heat. Due to the greenhouse effect, the temperature on the surface of Venus is around 4600C, making it the hottest planet in the solar system. The thick cover clouds reflect light making its brightest planet too.

questions.question: Which of the following gas is called greenhouse gas?
questions.explanation: A greenhouse gas (sometimes abbreviated GHG) is a gas in an atmosphere that absorbs and emits radiation within the thermal infrared range. The primary greenhouse gases in Earth's atmosphere are water vapor, carbon dioxide, methane, nitrous oxide, and ozone. Carbon dioxide (CO2) and other greenhouse gases act like a blanket, absorbing IR radiation and preventing it from escaping into outer space. The net effect is the gradual heating of the Earth's atmosphere and surface, a process known as global warming.

questions.question: Gravitational force of the heavenly body like Earth and Moon depends upon
questions.explanation: Gravitational force of the heavenly bodies like Earth and Moon depends upon the mass and radius of the object. The pull of gravitational force is directed towards the centre of the Earth. The size of gravitational force depends on the mass of the object being pulled by the Earth. The Moon is a smaller object than the Earth and of lower mass. As a consequence, its gravitational field only pulls with a force of about a fifth of that of the Earth.

questions.question: Which planet shows the slowest rotation around its axis?
questions.explanation: Venus has the slowest rotation around its axis, over 243 Earth days for one rotation. It rotates from east to west unlike other planets.

questions.question: NASA stands for
questions.explanation: NASA stands for National aeronautics and space administration. It is an independent agency of the USA government responsible for the civilian space program, as well as aeronautics and aerospace research.

questions.question: Which of the following planets takes the longest time to complete one revolution?
questions.explanation: Neptune takes longest time to complete one revolution as it has longest orbit. Neptune is farthest planet from the sun(4498 million km away)and it takes 165 years to complete one revolution.

questions.question: In the orbit between Jupiter and Mars, a large number of rock and metal pieces are found. They are called
questions.explanation: Asteroids revolve around the Sun and are present between orbit of Jupiter and Mars as large number of rock and metal pieces.This region where most asteroids are found are called the Asteroid belt which is about 550 million km.

questions.question: The pieces of rock which revolve around the Sun, sometimes enter the Earth's atmosphere and start burning due to
questions.explanation: Pieces of rock revolving around the sun that enter the earth’s atmosphere and start burning due to friction from air of atmosphere and appear as streak of light in the sky.

questions.question: The streak of light in the night sky are commonly called
questions.explanation: Shooting star is a common name for the visible path of a meteoroid as it enters the atmosphere, usually burns up due to friction with the atmosphere becoming a meteor.

questions.question: INSAT 3B or Kalpana is
questions.explanation: Artificial satellites are man made satellites revolving around the earth. INSAT-3B is an Indian communications satellite which was built and is operated by the Indian Space Research Organisation. INSAT-3A, a multipurpose satellite was launched by Ariane in April 2003 and it provides communication, weather, and search and rescue services.

questions.question: In which constellation, pole star is present ?
questions.explanation: Pole star is at the end of tail in Ursa minor constellation. Other stars of this constellation appear to revolve but pole star which remain fixed. It is located directly above the North Pole so used for centuries to locate North Pole.

questions.question: Which one is called the red planet?
questions.explanation: Mars planet is called red planet due to the presence of iron oxide (rust) in its soil which gives a reddish appearance. It is situated between Earth and Jupiter.

questions.question: The study of the objects in the sky is known as
questions.explanation: The study of celestial bodies and the space that contains them is known as Astronomy and the scientist who studies astronomy are called astronomers.

questions.question: Which of the following planet have the greatest radius?
questions.explanation: Jupiter is the largest and fifth planet of solar system. So, Jupiter planets have greatest radius of about 69,911 km. It has the most number of moons (63 moons) too.

questions.question: An astronaut standing on the surface of the moon throws a ball upwards. The ball would

options.option: Keep going up never to come back.
Go up and then come back to the surface of the moon.
Directly fall down from the point it is released.

questions.explanation: The ball will go up and then come down because at moon,acceleration due to gravity is less than of Earth.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a member of the solar system.
questions.explanation: A constellation is not a member of the solar system. These are the group of stars that forms a recognizable shape is known as Constellation.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a planet of the sun?
questions.explanation: Sirius is a star and not a planet of the sun.

questions.question: Phases of the moon occur because:

options.option: the thickness of the moon's atmosphere is not constant.
we can see only that part of the moon which reflects light towards us.
our distance from the moon keeps changing.
the shadow of the Earth covers only a part of the moon's surface.

questions.explanation: Phases of the moon occur because we can see only that part of the moon which reflects light towards us. Moon does not produce its own light. We are able to see the moon because the sunlight falling on it gets reflected towards us. Thus, we see only that part of the moon from which the light of the sun gets reflected towards us.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a star?
questions.explanation: Shooting stars look like stars that quickly shoot across the sky, but they are not stars. A shooting star is really a small piece of rock or dust that hits Earth's atmosphere from space. It moves so fast that it heats up and glows as it moves through the atmosphere. Shooting stars are actually what astronomers call meteors.

questions.question: Which of the following is a star?
questions.explanation: Alpha Centauri is the nearest star system to the Sun, located at a distance of only 4.37 light years or 1.34 parsecs from Earth. Alpha Centauri is the brightest star in Centaurus constellation and the third brightest star in sky.

questions.question: Phase of Moon are observed because

options.option: only some parts of the Moon can emit light.
the shadow of Earth falls on the Moon.
the sunlight reflected by some parts of Moon reaches the Earth.

questions.explanation: Moon does not produce its own light. We are able to see the moon because the sunlight falling on it gets reflected towards us. Thus, we see only that part of the moon from which the light of the sun gets reflected towards us.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a member of the solar system?
questions.explanation: A constellation is not a member of the solar system. Constellations are groups of stars that form recognisable shapes.

questions.question: Morning star is the name given to
questions.explanation: Planet Venus is dazzling than any other stars and it is visible in the eastern sky before sunrise.

questions.question: Sun appears to move from east to west around the earth. This means that the earth rotates from
questions.explanation: Sun appears to move from east to west around the earth which means the earth is rotating in the opposite direction.

questions.question: Suppose a new planet is discovered between Uranus and Neptune. Its time period would be

options.option: equal to that of Neptune or Uranus.

questions.explanation: The period of time would be more than the Uranus but lesser than the Neptune because period of time is directly proportional to the distance from the sun.

questions.question: The change in seasons on the earth occurs because

options.option: the distance between the earth and the sun is not constant.
the axis of rotation of the earth is perpendicular to the plane of its orbit.
the axis of rotation of the earth is parallel to the plane of its orbit.
the axis of rotation of the earth is tilted with respect to the plane of its orbit.

questions.explanation: The axis of rotation of the earth is not perpendicular to the plane of its orbit. The tilt is responsible for the chang of seasons on earth.

questions.question: The stars twinkle in the sky because of-
questions.explanation: As light from star races through our atmosphere, it bounces and bumps through the different layers, bending the light before you see it. Since the hot and cold layers of air keep moving, the bending of the light changes too, which causes the star’s appearance to wobble or twinkle.

questions.question: The planet venus appears in the ________ sky before sunrise
questions.explanation: The planet venus appears in the eastern sky before sunrise.

questions.question: Hari saw full moon on a particular day. After ________ days he will be able to see full moon again.
questions.explanation: The time interval between a full moon and next repetition of full moon is 29 days.

questions.question: How much time is taken by sunlight to reach to the earth from sun?
questions.explanation: It takes sunlight an average of 8 minutes and 20 seconds to travel from the Sun to the Earth.

questions.question: Venus is a planet of our solar system but it is often called a morning or an evening star (although it is not a star) Venus appears in the

options.option: eastern sky as well as western sky as a morning star

questions.explanation: Venus appears in the eastern sky before sunrise and in the western sky while sunset.

questions.question: The density of water is 1000 kg/m3. A planet of our solar system has density lesser than this value. The name of this planet is
questions.explanation: The density of saturn is lesser as compare to other planet.

questions.question: A star is 60 light years away from the Earth. How much time does it take for the light from the star to reach the earth?
questions.explanation:
Given,
distance between star and earth = 20 light year 
As we know that,
1 light year = Distance travelled by light in 1 year
20 light year = Distance travelled by light in 20 year.

questions.question: Which constellation appears to move around the pole star from east to west and looks like a question mark?
questions.explanation: Great bear constellation appears to move around the pole star from East to West and looks like a question mark.

questions.question: Which two planets of our solar system rotate from East to West?
questions.explanation: Venus and Uranus planets of ours solar system rotate from East to West.

questions.question: In terms of increasing distance from Sun, the correct sequence of the first the inner planets of the solar system is
questions.explanation: In terms of increasing distance from Sun the correct sequence of planets is Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mass, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune.

questions.question: A star is 20 light years from the Earth.How much time does it take for the light from the star to reach the Earth?
questions.explanation: The light from the stars reaches the earth in 20 yrs.

questions.question: In ecosystem the source of energy is-
questions.explanation: The Sun is the major source of energy for organisms and the ecosystems of which they are a part. Producers such as plants, algae, and cyanobacteria use the energy from sunlight to make organic matter from carbon dioxide and water.

questions.question: Planet venus is also known as ________
questions.explanation: Planet Venus is also known as morning star or an evening star because sometimes it appears in the eastern sky before sunrise. Sometimes, it appears in Western sky just after sunset. It is the bright est planet in the night sky.

questions.question: A star is fifteen light years away from earth. The light emitted by it today will be seen after ________ years.
questions.explanation: Light year is the unit of measurement of distance. One light year is equal to the distance travelled by light in one year. Therefore, if a star is fifteen light year away from earth, then the light emitted by it today will be seen after 15 years.

questions.question: Our universe is expanding. This speculation is propounded by
questions.explanation: Edwin Hubble

questions.question: Which one of the following star is the nearest to the Earth?
questions.explanation: Sun

questions.question: The distance between the sun and the Earth is
questions.explanation: 1.49 {tex}\times{/tex} 108 km

questions.question: The moon completes one revolution around Earth in
questions.explanation: 27 days 7 hrs 43 min

questions.question: Which year man firstly landed on the Moon?
