The area of the moon's surface where there is no evidence of the water is called
questions.explanation: ocean of storms

questions.question: The calm and dark back part of the moon is called

options.option: Both sea of tranquility and oceans of storms

questions.explanation: sea of tranquility

questions.question:
Which of the following statement is correct?

  1. Moon is the only natural satellite of Earth
  2. The time period of the moon is approx 27.3 days
  3. The time period of moon is roughly equal to the rotational period of moon about its own axis
  4. All of these
questions.explanation: All of these

questions.question: Aakash Ganga is the common name given to
questions.explanation: Milky way galaxy

questions.question: The smallest planet in our solar system is
questions.explanation: Mercury

questions.question: Which of planets is known as outer planets?
questions.explanation: Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune

questions.question: The small groups of planetary pieces which are confined and revolving between Mars and Jupiter are called
questions.explanation: asteroids

questions.question: The Earth has the size among all the planets
questions.explanation: fifth

questions.question: The planet whose relevancy as a planet has been ended at Prague Summit of IAU in
questions.explanation: Pluto

questions.question: The planet which has the largest number of satellites is
questions.explanation: Jupiter

questions.question: The planet which completes one revolution in 88 days around the sun is
questions.explanation: Mercury

questions.question: The largest satellite of the planet Saturn is
questions.explanation: Titan

questions.question:
Which of the following are true?

  1. A polar satellite goes around the Earth's pole in North-South direction
  2. A geostationary satellite goes around the Earth in East-West direction
  3. A geostationary satellite goes around the Earth in West-East direction
  4. Both (a) and (c)
questions.explanation: Both (a) and (c)

questions.question: From the space the sky appears to an astronaut
questions.explanation: black

questions.question: Which of the following planets can float in water?
questions.explanation: Saturn has less density than that of water so, it can float in water.

questions.question: Which is the brightest star in the sky?
questions.explanation: Sirius is the brightest star in the sky, though it is not the nearest star to the earth.

questions.question: Which constellation resembles a hunter?
questions.explanation: The constellation Orion resembles the figure of a hunter.

questions.question:
Arrange the following planets in decreasing order of time required to complete one revolution around the sun.

  1. Earth
  2. Uranus
  3. Mars
  4. Jupiter
questions.explanation:
Planets Time
Earth 1 year
Jupiter 11.9 years
Mars 1.88 years
Uranus 84 years

questions.question:
According to the table, on which planet would a ball fall the fastest?

Planet Earth Jupiter Neptune Saturn
Acceleration due to gravity (ms-2) 10 26 14 12
questions.explanation: Force of gravity is maximum on Jupiter due to large acceleration due to gravity. Hence, ball will fall fastest on Jupiter.

questions.question: Planets in our solar system are classified as inner and outer planets. Which of the following is incorrect about them?

options.option: Outer planets have rings orbiting them while inner planets do not have.
Most of the outer planets are larger in size whereas inner planets are comparatively smaller.
Outer planets spin quicker than inner planets.
Outer planets do not have moons orbiting them while inner planets have.

questions.explanation: Outer planets also have moons, in great numbers orbiting them.

questions.question: The given diagram shows the night sky in which stars are visible. Identify P, Q, R and S.

options.option: (P) - Big dipper (Q) - Little dipper (R) - Pole star (S) - Cassiopeia

questions.explanation: P represents Little dipper, Q is Big dipper, R is Pole star and S is Cassiopeia.

questions.question: Which of the following planets has no satellite?
questions.explanation: There are no satellites for mercury and Venus.

questions.question: Which of the following options correctly represents the relative positions of the Sun (S), Moon (M) and Earth (E) on the day of full moon and new moon?

options.option:
Full Moon New Moon
MES MSE

Full Moon New Moon
ESM EMS

Full Moon New Moon
SME ESM

Full Moon New Moon
SEM SME

questions.explanation: The position of the earth is between sun and the moon for full moon day and for new moon day, the position of the moon is between sun and the earth.

questions.question: Geostationary satellites appear stationary because

options.option: Their orbit of rotation lies at great height

questions.explanation: The period of rotation of geostationary satellite is equal to the period of rotation of the earth around its axis so they look stationary from the earth's surface.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements is/are true?

  1. A constellation has only 5-10 stars.
  2. Aryabhatta was the first Indian satellite.
  3. Artificial satellites are used for weather forecasting and remote sensing only.
  4. Venus is the brightest planet in the night sky.
questions.explanation: The first Indian satellite was named after famous astro as Aryabhatta.

questions.question: Which of the following planet is visible only in the morning and evening time?
questions.explanation: Mercury and Venus are situated on the side of earth's orbit which is nearer to the sun so they are not visible during night. They are visible either in the evening or in the early morning.

questions.question: We see the pole star stationary in the sky while other stars move in the group from east to west because,

options.option: Pole star is situated on axis of the earth directly above Earth’s north pole.
Pole star is situated on the axis of the earth directly above Earth’s south pole.
Pole star is situated above the equator of earth
Pole star is situated on axis of the earth directly below Earth’s north pole.

questions.explanation: Pole star lies on the extended axial line of the earth towards the north direction.

questions.question: Ursa major is visible throughout the year in
questions.explanation: The great bear is visible throughout the year in northern hemisphere and is the northern parts of Southern hemisphere.

questions.question: How star differ from planet?

options.option: Distance between two stars appears constant but distance between a star and a planet keep on changing with time.
Stars have their own light but planet does not have their own light and Distance between two stars appears constant but distance between a star and a planet keep on changing with time.
Planets have their own light but stars does not have their own light.
Stars have their own light but planet does not have their own light.

questions.explanation: Star produce their own light.

questions.question: Why do we always observe the same face of the moon, though it keeps rotating on its axis?

options.option: Time period of rotation of the moon on its axis is very small
Because the time period of rotation of the earth on its axis and time period of rotation of the moon on its axis are same.
Time period of rotation of the moon on its axis is very large
The time period of rotation of the moon and the time period of revolution of the moon around the earth are the same.

questions.explanation: As the period of rotation on the axis of moon being equal to its period of revolution around the earth, so we can only see one face of the moon.

questions.question: Which of the following is wrong about outer planets?

options.option: They all are formed by rocks and They all contain rings of gaseous material around them
They all are visible only during night
They all contain rings of gaseous material around them

questions.explanation: All the outer planets are gaseous, and are visible only during night.

questions.question: Which of the following is wrong about pole star?

options.option: It is visible from the southern hemisphere of the earth.
It is situated in the axial line of earth
It is visible from the northern hemisphere of the earth.

questions.explanation: Pole star is situated on the axial line towards north so it appears stationary from northern hemisphere.

questions.question: Venus is the brightest planet because

options.option: The index of reflection of its surface is very high.
It is very close to the sun
Its atmosphere reflects most of the light falling on it.
The index of reflection of its surface is very low.

questions.explanation: Venus has very dense atmosphere composed mainly of carbon dioxide which reflects most of the sun rays falling on it.

questions.question: Which of the following planets can see rising sun in the west?
questions.explanation: Venus and Uranus rotate on their axes from East to West.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements about comet is not true?

  1. As it moves close to the sun, its tail orients towards the sun.
  2. It moves on a very large elliptical orbit around the sun
  3. Halley's comet appears in the sky after every 76 years.
  4. They are mostly made up of ice.
questions.explanation: The tail orients itself away from the sun as comet moves towards the sun.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements about meteorites is incorrect?

  1. Meteor is bigger than meteorite
  2. Meteorite is bigger than meteor
  3. Meteorite reaches the earth's surface
  4. meteorite provides rich source of information regarding space.
questions.explanation: Meteorites are bigger in size so a part of them manage to reach the surface of the earth even after burning partially in atmosphere.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements about artificial satellite is incorrect?

  1. Most of them revolve around the earth in orbits, very close to the earth.
  2. Ultimately they fall to the earth's surface.
  3. Some of them fly off to outer space
  4. They help in weather forecasting.
questions.explanation: Artificial satellites are destined to fall to the earth at the end of their utility period.

questions.question: Which of the following activities is not possible if done on the moon?
questions.explanation: Sound can not propagate on the moon because there is no atmosphere.

questions.question: Which of the following planets has the most tilted rotational axis?
questions.explanation: Uranus has the most tilted axis of rotation among all the given planets.

questions.question:
Match column I with column II and select the correct option from the given codes.

Column I Column II 
P. A girl pulling the leash of a running dog (i) Impact of force can make stationary object to move
Q. A driver turning the steering wheel of a car (ii) Gravity acts upon an object
R. When you hit a nail with a hammer (iii) Force can make an object go slower
S. A ball released from the top of a tower (iv) Force can change the direction of a moving object
questions.explanation: P-(iii), Q-(iv), R-(i), S-(ii)

questions.question:
Which of the following statements about Jupiter and Saturn is incorrect?

  1. Both are gaseous planets.
  2. Both have rings.
  3. Jupiter is bigger than Saturn.
  4. Saturn is denser than Jupiter.
questions.explanation: The Saturn is the least dense planet of all the planets

questions.question: Which of the following planets is called sister planet of the earth?
questions.explanation: The composition of Venus and the Earth is very similar, that is why Venus is known for sister planet of earth.

questions.question: Stars appear moving from east to west in the sky because

options.option: Of movement of stars in the sky
Both (Both are gaseous planets) and (Both have rings)
Movement of the earth around the sun

questions.explanation: Just as the sun appears rotating about the earth from the east to the west, similarly other stars too appear moving in the same direction in the night due to rotation of earth on its own axis.

questions.question: Which of the following planets is not visible during midnight?
questions.explanation: Venus is called "morning star". It is visible only in early morning or late evening.

subtopics.name: Some Other Members of the Solar System

questions.question: Our solar system is unique in the universe in the sense that

options.option: The whole solar system is moving in the universe
No other star has system of planets around them like our solar system.
All planets revolve round the sun which is a star, in elliptical orbits
It has a member planet having life on it

questions.explanation: Planet earth is the only celestial body which is inhabited.

questions.question: Next to the Sun our nearest star in the space is situated at a distance of
questions.explanation: Our next star Alpha Centauri is at a distance of 4.37 light years from the earth.

questions.question: Stars appear to move from ________.
questions.explanation: east to west

questions.question: Shooting star is actually ________.
questions.explanation: a big star

questions.question: Pole star cannot be seen in which one of the following countries?
questions.explanation: New Zealand

questions.question: Which is the brightest star in the night sky?
questions.explanation: Sirius

questions.question: The Moon revolves around the Earth in about ________ days.
Small rocky objects found in large numbers between Mars and Jupiter are called ________.
questions.explanation: asteroid

questions.question: Name the constellation which looks like distorted letter W or M.
questions.explanation: Sirius

questions.question: Artificial satellites are used for ________.
questions.explanation: all of these

questions.question: Ozone layer in troposphere protect us from
questions.explanation: Ultraviolet radiation is a form of energy traveling through space. Some of the most frequently recognized types of energy are heat and light. These, along with others enter the earth, can be classified as a phenomenon known as electromagnetic radiations radiates by sun.

questions.question: Which disease is caused by drinking of contaminated water?
questions.explanation: River or lake water may contain harmful microbes that cause water-borne diseases like jaundice, cholera, dysentery, typhoid, diarrhea, etc.

questions.question: Addition of harmful substances in water to make it unfit for human consumption is called?
questions.explanation: Whenever harmful substances such as sewage, toxic chemicals, silt, etc., get mixed with water, the water becomes polluted and unfit for human consumption. This is known as water pollution.

questions.question: The substance that pollutes the natural substance is called
questions.explanation: The substances that pollute natural substances like air, water and soil are called pollutants. Exhaust and sewage from industries, vehicular emission, non-biodegradable materials, silt, etc. are some common pollutants.

questions.question: Water pollution is caused by

options.option: Addition of untreated sewage in water bodies
Excess drawing of water from water bodies

questions.explanation: Water pollution is caused by addition of untreated domestic sewage and industrial wastage in water bodies. They make water unfit for human consumption.

questions.question: Which of the following area is likely to be most air polluted?
questions.explanation: Residential area, park, and crop fields are very less polluted areas while industrial areas have a large number of factories that emit harmful gases to cause air pollution.

questions.question: Which one is not a natural source of water
questions.explanation: Ponds, rivers, and lakes are a natural source of water as they are present naturally on earth. On the other hand, Canals are a man-made sources of water, especially for irrigation purposes.

questions.question: Which of the following is not responsible for air pollution?
questions.explanation: Air pollution is caused by combustion of fossil fuels, waste(gases) from factories and burning of coal. Freezing of ice is not responsible for air pollution.

questions.question: Which is not produced by burning of fossils fuel?
questions.explanation: Burning of fossil fuels produce carbon dioxide gas, Sulphur dioxide gas, and Nitrogen oxide gas. Oxygen gas is not produced by burning of fossil fuels.

questions.question: The purest form of water is
questions.explanation: Rain water is purest form of water as during cloud formation, evaporation of water takes place in which impurities are left in water bodies. Spring water, subsoil water, and hand pump water contains some salt dissolved in it.

questions.question: Which one is not an organic waste?
questions.explanation: Vegetable peels, cotton cloths, and paper are organic wastes as all of them are obtained from plants. DDT is an insecticide, used to kill insects and pests, and is prepared in laboratory.

questions.question: Which one is a common source of drinking water?
questions.explanation: River and lake are common sources of drinking water. Sea and ocean water are salty and cannot be used for drinking purpose.

questions.question: The process of waste treatment is called
questions.explanation: The process of waste treatment is called sewage treatment. During this process impurities are removed by filtration, sedimentation, and chlorination, thus converting the sewage to drinkable water.

questions.question: Bacteria that decompose organic wastes are called
questions.explanation: Bacteria that decompose organic wastes are called anaerobic bacteria. Oxygen is not required by these bacteria for respiration.

questions.question: Percentage of nitrogen gas in air is
questions.explanation: Nitrogen gas is present in atmosphere in maximum amount that is about 78% followed by oxygen and carbon dioxide.

questions.question: After the treatment, the treated water is released into
questions.explanation: Sewage treatment is done to remove different kinds of wastes present in water. After the treatment, the treated water is released into water bodies.

questions.question: Which gas is produced on burning of fossil fuels
questions.explanation: On burning of fossil fuels carbon dioxide gas is mainly produced. Some amount of sulphur dioxide and nitrogen oxide is also present in it.

questions.question: Air is a mixture of various gases. One of the gases is 21% part of the air and is essential for the survival of human beings. This gas is:
questions.explanation: Oxygen makes up about 21% of air by volume. It is essential for the survival of human beings.

questions.question: Boojho wishes to contribute in reducing air pollution. Which vehicle should he use for going to school?
questions.explanation: Boojho, if wishes to contribute in reducing air pollution, should use school bus for going to school. A school bus carry many children together, thus vehicular emissions are reduced.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a way to conserve water?
questions.explanation: Replace is not a way to conserve water. At our individual level, we should consciously save water and not to waste it. Reduce, reuse and recycle should be our mantra.

questions.question: Water-wise habits includes

options.option: Wash the flour one time in a day

questions.explanation: Water-wise habits include measures to save portable water from its misuse. Turning off tap while brushing and repairing of leaky taps are water-wise habits.

questions.question: Algal bloom causes
questions.explanation: Growth of large number of alga in water bodies due to presence of excess of nutrients causes low dissolved oxygen level in water bodies. It becomes difficult to respire in such water.

questions.question: Incomplete combustion of fuel such as petrol and diesel gives?
questions.explanation: Carbon monoxide is produced by the incomplete combustion of fuels like wood, coal, kerosene, petrol and diesel.

questions.question: The type of pollution which is likely to affect Taj Mahal in Agra to a greater extent is:
questions.explanation: Air pollution is responsible for the yellowing of Taj Mahal in Agra. Polluted air contains oxides of sulphur and nitrogen that mix with water during rain to cause acid rain that acts on marbles to make it yellow.

questions.question: Anaerobic bacteria is used for
questions.explanation: Fermentation bacteria are anaerobic, but use organic molecules as their final electron acceptor to produce fermentation end-products. Streptococcus, Lactobacillus, and Bacillus are some of the examples of anaerobic bacteria.

questions.question: Water from bathroom, kitchen and toilet are called
questions.explanation: Water from bathroom, kitchen and toilet are called waste water. These water together with other household wastage forms the sewage.

questions.question: Inorganic substance present in sewage includes
questions.explanation: Sewage includes organic as well as inorganic substances. The main inorganic substance present in sewage includes nitrate and phosphates.

questions.question: Network of big and small pipes called sewers to join together to form
questions.explanation: In cities, there is a network of big and small pipes connected together to remove sewage from domestic and industrial area and is called as sewerage.

questions.question: Which gas is responsible for acid rain?
questions.explanation: Fossil fuel contains small amount of sulphur that combine with oxygen during combustion to form oxide of sulphur (SO2). SO2 reacts with rain water to form Sulphuric acid to cause acid rain.

questions.question: Chemicals used for disinfecting water are
questions.explanation: Disinfection is the process of killing harmful microbes present in water. Chlorine and Ozone is used as disinfect in drinking water.

questions.question: Waste water should be used after
questions.explanation: Waste water should be used after removing pollutants present in waste water. Sewage treatment is used to remove harmful pollutants involving filtration, sedimentation and chlorination.

questions.question: Which of the following gas is most harmful to us?
questions.explanation: Out of helium, nitrogen, carbon monoxide, and carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide is most harmful to us as it combines with hemoglobin to reduce oxygen carrying capacity.

questions.question: ___________ Plants are planted along the sewage treatment plant.
questions.explanation: Eucalyptus plants are generally planted along the sewage treatment plant because it grows very fast(also in contaminated water) and cover large area. They absorbs waste water and releases pure water vapour.

questions.question: Aeration of water during water treatment removes the
questions.explanation: Aeration is the addition of oxygen to water. Aeration of water during water treatment removes the anaerobic bacteria that grow only in absence of oxygen.

questions.question: The process during waste water treatment, in which germs are killed is
questions.explanation: During tertiary treatment chlorination is done that kills the germs present in water to make it fit for human consumption.

questions.question: In a network of sewer system, Manhole are provided at
questions.explanation: In Sewer network system, manhole is provided at junction of two or more sewers. Manhole is a deep, generally cylindrical structure.

questions.question: WWTP stands for
questions.explanation: WWTP stands for Waste water treatment plant. It is the physical infrastructure used for treating waste water. The treated water can be used for consumption, household chores, and recharging water table.

questions.question: Treatment of waste water do not involves
questions.explanation: Treatment of waste water involves physical treatment, chemical treatment and biological treatment. Hormonal treatment is not a part of sewage treatment.

questions.question: Absence of safe drinking water may lead to
questions.explanation: Waterborne diseases are caused by pathogen present in water. Absence of safe drinking water may lead to water born disease as people start using unsafe water.

questions.question: The water that is purified and fit for drinking is called
questions.explanation: The water that is purified and fit for drinking is called as portable water. Portable water contains small amount of useful salts.

questions.question: Water in a river is cleaned
questions.explanation: In natural water bodies, there is a biological system of water cleaning. But addition of large amount of pollutants in water makes this natural system fail to clean the water.

questions.question: River Yamuna is polluted near Delhi due to
questions.explanation: River Yamuna is polluted near Delhi due to addition of untreated sewage in large quantities. All the sewage enters the Yamuna and pollutes it.

questions.question: Water is chlorinated to
questions.explanation: During water works, water is chlorinated to kill the germs present in it to make it fit for human consumption.

questions.question: Water can be conserved by
questions.explanation: Rainwater harvesting is a technique of collection and storage of rainwater into natural reservoirs or tanks, or the infiltration of surface water into subsurface aquifers (before it is lost as surface runoff)

questions.question: Sea water is unfit for drinking because it contains
questions.explanation: Seawater is unfit for drinking because it contain a large amount of dissolved salts. Seawater cannot be used for even washing the clothes as the foam is not formed in such water.

questions.question: Which of the following is a respiratory disease caused by polluted air?
questions.explanation: Asthma is a respiratory disease caused by polluted air. Blood pressure, jaundice, and goiter is not caused by polluted air.

questions.question: In winter, smoke and fog together mix to form
questions.explanation: In winter, smoke and fog together mix to form smog that decrease the visibility and affect the air and road transportation.

questions.question: Gaseous pollutant like Sulpher dioxide and nitrogen oxide is mainly released from
questions.explanation: Small amounts of sulphur dioxide and nitrogen oxide is released from petroleum refinery. These gases combine with water during rain to cause acid rain.

questions.question: Which of the following gas is responsible for ozone layer depletion?
questions.explanation: Chlorofluorocarbon gas is responsible for ozone layer depletion. Ozone layer present in stratosphere combine with CFC,s to release free oxygen.

questions.question: Ozone layer protects us from harmful
questions.explanation: Ozone layer present in the stratosphere protects us from harmful ultraviolet rays. Ultraviolet rays may cause skin cancer and other diseases in human beings.

questions.question: Chlorofluorocarbon gas is used in
questions.explanation: Chlorofluorocarbon gas which is mainly responsible for depletion of ozone layer is used in refrigerator and air conditioners.

questions.question: Mathura oil refinery and leather industry around Agra are mainly responsible for
questions.explanation: Oil refinery release sulphur dioxide and nitrogen oxide gas which is carried by wind to other places. These gases combine with rain water to form Sulphuric acid and nitric acid and cause acid rain. This rain corrodes marble of Taj Mahal.

questions.question: Sulpher dioxide combine with water to form
questions.explanation: Sulphur dioxide combines with water to form Sulphurous acid.  SO2(g) + H2O(l) {tex}\to{/tex} H2SO3(aq)

questions.question: CNG is called clean fuel because

options.option: Combustion of it produces less Carbon dioxide

questions.explanation: Compressed natural gas is called clean fuel because combustion of CNG produce less amount of CO2 without any solid residues. It can be used easily too.

questions.question: We cannot survive a few minutes without
questions.explanation: Oxygen is crucial to sustaining life. Though it varies from person to person, on average, 3 minutes is the maximum time where the person can stay alive without oxygen.

questions.question: Green house effect is responsible for
questions.explanation: Greenhouse effect is responsible for global warming. Greenhouse gases form a blanket in atmosphere that prevents the escape of heat to cause increase in temperature.

questions.question: In which process of plant carbon dioxide is converted into oxygen?
questions.explanation: During photosynthesis, plants convert carbon dioxide gas into oxygen along with the formation of glucose.

questions.question: The average temperature of earth is increasing slowly due to
questions.explanation: The average temperature of the earth is increasing slowly due to the greenhouse effect that leads to global warming.

questions.question: Which of the following is most polluted stretch of river Ganga?
questions.explanation: Due to the presence of large number of leather industries near Kanpur, large amount of wastes are released in river Ganga that make most polluted stretch of river Ganga.

questions.question: Kyoto protocol is about the

options.option: Increase in the emission of greenhouse gas

questions.explanation: Kyoto Protocol is an international treaty that extends the 1992 united nations framework convention on climate change to reduce the emission of greenhouse gases.

questions.question: An ambitious plan to save the river Ganga, launched in 1985 was
questions.explanation: An ambitious plan to save the river Ganga, launched in 1985 is the Ganga Action Plan. This multicrore project although failed to clean the river Ganga.

questions.question: World largest eco-friendly, CNG bus service is
questions.explanation: World largest eco-friendly, CNG bus service is Delhi Transport Corporation. This service is started to reduce the air pollution in Delhi.

questions.question: WWF stands for
questions.explanation: The World Wide Fund for Nature is an international non-governmental organization founded on April 29, 1961, working in the field of the biodiversity conservation, and the reduction of humanity's footprint on the environment.

questions.question: Which of the following is not an alternative source of energy?
questions.explanation: Solar energy, wind energy, and hydropower are alternative sources of energy. Petrol is a conventional source of energy along with coal.

questions.question: The substance like sewage, toxic chemicals, and silt forms
questions.explanation: The substance like sewage, toxic chemicals, and silts form water pollutants. These pollutants are present in domestic and industrial sewage along with hospital wastes.

questions.question: Van Mahotsav is celebrated in month of
questions.explanation: Van Mahotsav is an annual tree-planting festival in India. This movement was initiated in the year 1950 by India's Union Minister for Agriculture, Kulapati Dr.K M Munshi. It is now a week-long festival, celebrated on different days in different parts of India, but usually between 1 July to 7 July.

questions.question: Adding some material to water that spoils its quality and change the smell and colour is called
questions.explanation: Addition of materials to water that spoils its quality and changes the smell and colour is called water pollution. The changes may be biological, chemical, or physical.

questions.question: Which of the following is common toxic chemical that get accumulated by biomagnifications?
questions.explanation: DDT is common toxic chemical that get accumulated by biomagnifications. DDT is non-biodegradable insecticide that accumulates at each trophic level.

questions.question: Which of the following substance is present in least amount in air?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen gas is present in maximum percentage in air followed by oxygen and Carbon dioxide. Helium gas is present in very trace amount.

questions.question: Amount of water recommended by United Nation per person per day is
questions.explanation: Amount of water recommended by United Nation per person per day is 40 litres. The recommendation includes water on the basis of everyday use like bathing, sanitation, consumption, cooking, etc.

questions.question: In water works, first step is
questions.explanation: In water works, first step or primary treatment is sedimentation in which solid wastes are settled at bottom.

questions.question: If a person consumes contaminated water, he may suffer from a disease called
questions.explanation: a medical condition with yellowing of the skin or whitening of the eyes, arising from excess of the pigment bilirubin and typically caused by obstruction of the bile duct, by liver disease, or by excessive breakdown of red blood cells.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a greenhouse gas?
questions.explanation: The greenhouse gases include methane, CO2, SO2, nitrous oxide, ozone and water vapour. Oxygen is the only clean gas out there because we need it to survive. Therefore, the only gas that is not a greenhouse gas is (c) oxygen.

questions.question: Which of the following affects the oxygen carrying capacity of blood?
questions.explanation: Carbon Monoxide Reduces the oxygen-carrying capacity of the blood. It combines with haemoglobin in red blood cells, preventing oxygen combining with the haemoglobin. This causes an increase in heart rate to compensate for the reduced amount of oxygen carried in the blood.

questions.question: Air contains the highest percentage of
questions.explanation: Air is a mixture of gases. About 78% of air is composed of nitrogen, 21% is composed of oxygen, and the rest 1% is composed of carbon dioxide, argon, methane, ozone, water vapour, etc.

questions.question: Most of the atmospheric air is contained in the atmospheric layer called
questions.explanation: The troposphere is the atmospheric layer closest to the planet and contains the largest percentage (around 80%) of the mass of the total atmosphere.

questions.question: An odd combination of smoke and fog is called
questions.explanation: Smog is a mixture of smoke and fog. Smog reduces visibility, and thus can create problems for drivers at highways. Pilots face problems while landing or taking off; due to smog.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a source of air pollution?
questions.explanation: Windmill is not a source of air pollution. It is regarded as an alternative source of energy.

questions.question: The phenomenon of marble cancer is due to
questions.explanation: The phenomenon of marble cancer Is due to acid rain. Marble cancer is the corrosion of buildings or monuments by acid rain.

questions.question: Potable water is the water which is
questions.explanation: Potable water is the water which is pure and fit for drinking. Water can be made potable by various methods such as boiling, etc.

questions.question: Which of the following procedures will give you water free from all impurities?
questions.explanation: When water is heated, it boils at a temperature of 100o C. At this high temperature, all the harmful microorganisms (or germs) present in water are killed and it becomes absolutely safe for drinking.

questions.question: A pond contains clean water. Which of the following activities will produce least pollution of water?
questions.explanation: Swimming in the pond is the activity which will produce the least pollution of water.

questions.question: Trees help in reducing the pollution of our environment. Lakhs of trees are planted by people in the month of July every year. The occasion is called
questions.explanation: Van Mahotsav is a occasion when lakhs of trees are planted in July every year.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a green-house gas?
questions.explanation: Carbon dioxide, methane, and water vapour are greenhouse gases. But the main constituents of the atmosphere nitrogen and oxygen are not greenhouse gases.

questions.question: The main cause of pollution in metrocities is
questions.explanation: burning of fossil fuels

questions.question: CFCs are responsible for
questions.explanation: ozone layer depletion

questions.question: Acid rain contains
questions.explanation: SO2 and NO2

questions.question: Which of the following gases does not cause acid rain?
questions.explanation: Methane

questions.question: Ozone hole rain is due to
questions.explanation: CFCS

questions.question: Greenhouse effect is due to the presence of
questions.explanation: CO2 layer in the atmosphere

questions.question: A gradual increase in the average temperature of earth's atmosphere is known as
questions.explanation: global warming

questions.question: Which one of the following is mainly responsible for greenhouse effect?
questions.explanation: CO2

questions.question: Which one of the following pairs of gases are the major cause of greenhouse effect?
questions.explanation: CO2 and N2O

questions.question: Greenhouse effect with respect to global climate refers to
questions.explanation: warming effect

questions.question: Biological pollution in water is caused by
questions.explanation: domestic sewage and animal excreta

questions.question: The Ganga Action Plan was launched in the year
The cause(s) of water pollution is/are througling of
questions.explanation: All of these

questions.question: Smoke is-
questions.explanation: Smoke is an aerosol. The dispersed phase in the aerosol is solid and the dispersion medium is gas. The aerosol is a type of colloidal solution. Smoke is a colloidal solution of solid in gas.

questions.question: Which of the following is a greenhouse gas?
questions.explanation: Water vapours are considered as greenhouse gases along with CH4, CO2, etc. These gases absorb solar radiation and are responsible Mr raising earth's temperature due to greenhouse effect.

questions.question: The acid rain contains ________ in more quantity.
questions.explanation: The acid rain contains SO2 in more quantily. It reacts with rain droplets and form sulphuric acid (H2SO4). When it falls on surface of earth, it damages plant and, animal life alongwith monuments.

questions.question: Water gas is a mixture of-
questions.explanation: Water gas is a mixture of carbon monoxide (CO) and hydrogen (H2) produced by passing steam over red-hot coke using the endothermic reaction C + H2O {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} CO + H2.

questions.question: Which gas is responsible for Global warming?
questions.explanation: Carbon dioxide (COA is the primary greenhouse gas emitted through human activities.

questions.question: Acid-rain is related to
questions.explanation: Acid rain is caused by a chemical reaction that begins when compounds like sulfur dioxide (SO2) and nitrogen oxides (NO2) are released into the air. These substances can rise very high into the atmosphere, where they mix and react with water, oxygen, and other chemicals to form more acidic pollutants, known as acid rain.

questions.question: The apparatus used to measure humidity of atmosphere is-
questions.explanation: Hygrometer is the instrument used in meteorological science to measure the humidity or amount of water vapour in the air. Several types of hygrometers are used to measure humidity.

questions.question: The gas that does not cause green house effect is
questions.explanation: Nitrogen gas (N2) does not cause greenhouse effect. The greenhouse effect is the way in which heat is trapped close to earth surface by greenhouse gases. Main greenhouse gases are carbon dioxide, methane, nitrous oxide and water vapour and fluorinated gases.

questions.question: Air is-
questions.explanation: Air is considered as a mixture because of the following reasons: It is possible to separate air into its constituent gases by means of physical methods like fractional distillation. The boiling point of nitrogen (77.3 K) is less than that of oxygen (90 K).

questions.question: Which are natural sources of air pollution?

options.option: Smoke and dust arising from forest fires and volcanic eruption

questions.explanation: Natural sources of air pollution are smoke and dust arising from forest fires and volcanic eruption. Air pollution is contamination of the indoor or outdoor environment by any chemical, physical or biological agent that modifies the natural characteristics of the atmosphere.

questions.question:
Choose the correct statement from the following for the pure matter.

  1. Pure matters can be compound or mixtures.
  2. The composition of pure matters is same everywhere.
  3. In pure matters there are same type of particles.
  4. Except hydrogen and oxygen, all elements are the example of pure matters.
questions.explanation:
Statements that are correct with reference to pure matter are:

  1. The composition of pure matters is same everywhere.
  2. In pure matters there are same types of particles.

questions.question: In which form the carbon is found in the atmosphere of earth?

options.option: CO2 in less quantity with Carbon Monoxide

questions.explanation: Carbon is the foundation of all life on Earth, required to form complex molecules like proteins and DNA. This element is also found in our atmosphere in the form of carbon dioxide (CO2).

questions.question: Which of the following gas does not create air pollution?
questions.explanation: CNG produces the fewest emissions of all other fuels and contains significantly less pollutants than gasoline. It produces 20-30% fewer greenhouse gas emissions and 95% fewer tailpipe emissions than petroleum products and because CNG fuel systems are completely sealed, CNG vehicles produce no evaporative emissions.

questions.question: Which of the following is a non-polluting fuel for vehicles?
questions.explanation: CNG is a natural gas stored under high pressure. Burning of natural gas produces nitrogen oxides, sulphur dioxide, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide in low quantities thus, it is a non-polluting fuel.

questions.question:
Choose the correct statement related to soaps/ detergents-

  1. The usage of detergents is environment friendly
  2. The cleaning process of soaps is more better than detergents because they do better with hard water, while detergents don’t
  3. Soaps are biodegradable, while detergents are not biodegradables
  4. detergents do not pollutes the water
questions.explanation: Soaps are formed by the reaction of alkaline, fats, and oils. These are the sodium salts of fats and oils. Fats and oils can easily be broken down into simple molecules by microbes and hence soaps are biodegradable. Detergents are synthetic compounds generally ammonium or sulfate salts of long-chain carboxylic acids. These synthetic compounds cannot be broken down into simple molecules by microbes and hence detergents are non-biodegradable.

questions.question: Which of the following gas in atmosphere is responsible for acidic rain?
questions.explanation: Acid rain is caused by a chemical reaction that begins when compounds like sulfur dioxide and nitrogen oxides are released into the air. These substances can rise very high into the atmosphere, where they mix and react with water, oxygen, and other chemicals to form more acidic pollutants, known as acid rain.

questions.question: Which one of the following air pollutants produced due to incomplete combustion of petrol and diesel reduces the oxygen carrying capacity of blood?
questions.explanation: Carbon monoxides produced due to incomplete combustion of petrol and diesel reduces the oxygen carrying capacity of blood.

questions.question: Which of the following gases are responsible for acid rain?
questions.explanation: Acid rain results when sulphur dioxide (SO2) and nitrogen oxides (NO2) are emitted into atmosphere and transported by wind and air currents. The SO2 and NO2 react with water, oxygen and other chemicals to form sulphuric and nitric acids.

questions.question: What will happen, if all the oxygen present in atmosphere is converted into ozone?

options.option: It will become poisonous and will destroy living beings
It will help harmful solar radiation to reach to earth and will damage many types of creatures
Ozone is not permanent, that’s why it will become poisonous

questions.explanation: Ozone is a poisonous gas and is only present in a thin layer in the stratosphere. If all the oxygen is converted to ozone, the environment will become poisonois and kill all living forms.

questions.question: Choose that which is not a mixture-
questions.explanation: Distilled water is a type of purified water that has had both contaminants and minerals removed. Purified water has had chemicals and contaminants removed, but it may still contain minerals. Distillation boils the water and then condenses the steam back into a liquid to remove impurities and minerals.

questions.question: Which of the following processes does not increase CO2 in air?
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis the process by which green plants and certain other organisms use the energy of light to convert carbon dioxide and water into the simple sugar glucose. In doing so, photosynthesis provides the basic energy source for almost all organisms.

questions.question: ________ is responsible for acid rain.
questions.explanation: Sulphur dioxide (SO2) and nitrogen oxide (NO2) are responsible for acid rain. Acid rain results when sulphur dioxide (SO2) and nitrogen oxide (NO2) are emitted into the atmosphere and transported by wind and air currents. SO2 and NO2 react with water, oxygen and other chemicals to form sulphuric acid and nitric acids, then mix with water and oilier materials before falling to the ground.

questions.question: Major pollution is caused by
questions.explanation: man

questions.question: At present, the concentration of CO2 in the atmosphere is about

Read the given paragraph.
Gas X and Gas Y are produced by burning of fossil fuels. They both contribute to the formation of smog. Gas X forms sulphuric acid and Gas Y forms nitric acid on combining with moisture present in air. Hence, they are also responsible for acidic precipitation or acid rain. Identify X and Y and select the incorrect option regarding them.

options.option: A person living in area where air contains large amount of Y can develop skin problems as well as respiratory problems.
X combines with haemoglobin of our blood and impacts its oxygen carrying capacity.
If a person is continuously exposed to air polluted by X then he may develop certain respiratory problems.
Y Combines with Oxygen develop lungs problem.

questions.explanation: In the given paragraph, gas X is SO2 and gas Y is NO2. They combine with water vapour present in air to form acids that come down along with precipitation which is referred to as acid rain. These gases are also components of smog. These gases when inhaled cause respiratory problem. Oxides of nitrogen in addition to this, also cause various skin problems.

questions.question:
Which of the following will help control air pollution?

  1. Use of smokeless fuels like LPG, CNG etc.
  2. Burning garbage in open
  3. Use of bicycle instead of scooters or cars for covering short distance.
  4. Spraying pesticides in agricultural fields to control pests
  5. Use of solar cookers to cook food and solar water heaters.
questions.explanation: Burning of garbage in open and spraying of pesticides in agricultural fields to control pests will cause pollution.

questions.question: Refer to the given figure and select the incorrect option regarding it.

options.option: The heat that enters the glass house escape out.
It is a house that is constructed to allow sun-rays and heat to pass into the glass house
It helps in growth of green plants in cold weather by providing them optimum heat.

questions.explanation: The given figure shows a green house that is based on the heat trapping effect of some gases like CO2, methane, nitrous oxide, etc. present in air. The heat that enters the glass house cannot escape out because the glass pane radiate back the heat to the inside of the glass house.

questions.question:
Refer to the given Venn diagram.

What can be placed at X, Y and Z respectively?

options.option: X - Smoking  Y - Lead processing units, Z - Use of pesticides in agricultural fields
X - Untreated sewage  Y - Burning of garbage in open, Z - Construction sites
X - construction sites,  Y - Use of chlorofluoro carbons in cooling devises, Z - Discharge of hot water from factories into water bodies
X - Use of fertilisers in agricultural fields  Y - Oil refineries, Z - Gases emitted by motor vehicles

questions.explanation: According to the given Venn diagram, X is an activity that leads to both air and water pollution like construction sites, Y is an activity that causes air pollution but not water pollution like use of chlorofluorocarbons in cooling devices and Z is an activity that causes only water pollution so it could be discharge of hot water from factories into water bodies.

questions.question:
Refer to the given classification.

Select the correct statement regarding X, Y and Z.

options.option: Y and Z are produced by burning of fossil fuels.
X, Y and Z leads to various respiratory problems in humans.
X is dust produced by blowing wind, heavy traffic on roads, stone crushers and construction activities.

questions.explanation: In the given classification chart X is dust, Y is smoke and Z is fly ash.

questions.question: Potable water is ________.

options.option: Hot water that is discharged from factories into river
Polluted water that is unfit for drinking
Water that contains salts in high amounts making it healthier and tastier
Water that is free from impurities and microorganisms

questions.explanation: Water that is free from impurities and microorganisms

questions.question: Which of the following is a method of conserving water?

options.option: Bathing under running tap water or using a shower
Using full flush from cistern in the toilet after every use
Reusing water used for washing vegetables for watering plants

questions.explanation: Reusing water used for washing vegetables for watering plants

questions.question: Which of the following activities cause water pollution?

options.option: Discharging untreated industrial waste into rivers and lakes
Dumping idols of god and goddess and worship waste in river water on the name of rituals
Use of fertilisers and pesticides in agricultural fields

questions.explanation: All of these

questions.question:
Read the given paragraph and answer the question that follows.
In a remote village, pesticides were supplied by water transport. In an unfortunate accident, one of the ships get drowned in the water body of the village and sacks carrying pesticides opened up in water. Pesticides dissolved in water body. After a month following the incident large number of aquatic birds visiting the area were found dead and people consuming aquatic animals developed various toxicities. What could be the possible reason for this?

options.option: Pesticide started accumulating in bodies of aquatic plants and animals either developed diseases or got killed gradually.
Pesticide used up all dissolved oxygen there by all aquatic plants and animals either developed diseases or got killed gradually.
Pesticide started accumulating in bodies of aquatic plants and animals and kept biomagnifying in higher order consumers.

Large amount of pesticide in water body caused its nutrient enrichment which lead to algal bloom which lead to death of submerged plants and all animals, when dead animals were consumed by birds and humans, they also developed toxicity

questions.explanation: Pesticides are non-biodegradable and non-metabolizable within the bodies of living organisms. If they are present in water bodies then they enter the bodies of aquatic organisms and keep on magnifying at each higher trophic level of food chain so mostly the top consumers are highly impacted by the toxicity.

questions.question: An electric water filter like aquaguard is based on which of the following principles?

options.option: Chlorine is added to water and it kills harmful microorganisms present in it
Water filled in a container having porcelain candles gets filtered thoroughly on passing through tiny pores of porcelain candles
Resin filters are used to remove solid particles present in water and ultraviolet radiations are used to kill harmful microorganisms

questions.explanation: Resin filters are used to remove solid particles present in water and ultraviolet radiations are used to kill harmful microorganisms

questions.question: Refer to the given graph and select the correct statement.

options.option: Air of city 1, 2 and 3 is free from pollution.
Air of city 2 is most polluted. It could be due to excessive use of fossil fuels.
Smog formed in city 3 is most intense and dangerous.
City 1 is most likely to receive acidic precipitation, which is otherwise negligible in city 2 and city 3.

questions.explanation: The air is more polluted if more amount of oxides of sulphur and nitrogen are present in atmospheric air. Density of smog will also be more in such air. According to the given graph, city 2 is most polluted. Followed by city 1 which is less polluted than city 2 and city 3 is least polluted.

questions.question:
Refer to the given flow chart.

Which of the following holds true for P, Q, R and S?

options.option: P could be a non-biodegradable toxic chemical that keeps on biomagnifying at each higher trophic level in a food chain.
R and S are components of smog and also cause acid rain.
Q could be a gas produced by incomplete combustion of fossil fuels and affects the oxygen carrying capacity of blood.

questions.explanation: In the given flow chart, P could be a liquid or a solid pollutant (biodegradable or non-biodegradable) Q could be carbon monoxide gas. R could be oxide of sulphur and S could be oxide of nitrogen.

questions.question:
Refer to the given Venn diagram. 

Select the correct option regarding X

options.option: In the absence of X, the temperature of earth will become very low such that it will no longer be able to support life.
Forests help in regulating the amount of X in atmospheric air by maintaining balance between processes of respiration and photosynthesis.
The amount of X in the atmospheric air is increasing steadily due to rapid burning of fossil fuels.

questions.explanation: : In the given Venn diagram X refers to carbon dioxide.

questions.question:
Refer to the given paragraph where few words have been italicised.
Certain gases like carbon dioxide, water vapour, etc., are able to trap long wavelength ultraviolet rays of the sun. They are called green house gases. They contribute towards global warming. Carbon monoxide is released by cooling devices and destroy ozone in the atmosphere which prevents infra red radiations of sun to reach earth. Oxides of sulphur and nitrogen react with methane in the atmosphere and result in acid rain

options.option: The positions of carbon monoxide should be changed with chlorofluorocarbons
Water vapour should not be replaced as it is correctly mentioned whereas carbon dioxide should be replaced by oxygen.
Long should be replaced by short and positions of ultraviolet and infra red should not be interchanged as they are correctly mentioned.
The positions of ozone and methane should be interchanged.

questions.explanation: The positions of carbon monoxide should be changed with chlorofluorocarbons

questions.explanation: All of these

questions.question:
Select the incorrect statement.

  1. Sulphur dioxide gas combines with haemoglobin of blood and prevents transport of oxygen by it.
  2. Methane, nitrous oxide and water vapour present in atmospheric air contribute to global warming.
  3. Lead emitted by automobile exhaust could cause brain damage.
  4. Oxides of sulphur and nitrogen present in air could affect lungs.
questions.explanation: Carbon monoxide gas combines with haemoglobin of blood and prevents transport of oxygen by it.

questions.question: Which of the following is suspended particulate matter?
questions.explanation: Smoke

questions.explanation: As the population of people goes on increasing, pollution also goes on increasing

Select incorrect match.

  2. Oxygen-21%
  3. Carbon dioxide - 0.3%
  4. Other gases - 1%
questions.explanation: Percentage of carbon dioxide is 0.03% in air.

questions.question: Which of the given containers will continue to have healthy fish and plants at the end of a week?
questions.explanation: Container in option (A) will continue to have healthy fish and plants at the end of a week as the water is clean and will let proper exchange of gases. While the container in options (B) and (C) have layer of oil and suspended matter above the water respectively. These will hinder the process of photosynthesis and exchange of gases.

questions.question:
The given table describes the amount of pollutants in body of aquatic organisms in a river.

Organism Amount of lead in the body
of the organism  (ppm)
P 1.6
Q 4.3
R 0.025
S 2.4
T 0.16

Select the option that correctly arranges these organisms in a food chain.
questions.explanation: The concentration of non-biodegradable toxic chemicals increases with each trophic level in a food chain. This phenomenon is called as biomagnification.

questions.question:
Match column I with column II and select the correct option from the given codes.

Column I Column II
(i) Fluoride (a) Delays reflexes
(ii) Carbon monoxide (b) Anaemia
(iii) Copper (c) Delay in mental development
(iv) Lead (d) Bone disorders
(v) SPM (e) Respiratory disorders

questions.question: What could be the consequence of global warming?
questions.explanation: All of these

questions.question: Untreated sewage is discharged into a river from nearby homes. Within few weeks, the number of fish in the river significantly falls. Which of the following could not have caused this phenomenon?
questions.explanation: Due to addition of untreated sewage waste into the river, the fish population will decrease. Hence, over crowding due to increased fish population is not possible

questions.question:
The given graphs show the change in global sea levels with respect to time.
Which of these graphs correctly depicts that deforestation has taken place over time?
Which of the following is the impact of acid rain?
questions.explanation: Acids present in acid rain reacts with carbonates present in marble and limestone of statue and building and corrode it slowly.

questions.question:
Read the given statements and select the correct option.
Statement 1: Pesticides like DDT enter the food chains and their concentration goes on increasing from one trophic level to the next.
Statement 2: Pesticides like DDT are non-biodegradable organic compounds which are not metabolised within the organisms’ body and get stored in their body tissues.

options.option: Both statements 1 and 2 are true and statement 2 is the correct explanation of statement 1.
Statement 1 is true and statement 2 is false.
Both statements 1 and 2 are true but statement 2 is not the correct explanation of statement 1.

questions.explanation: Certain pollutants, e.g., DDT, etc. get accumulated in the tissues of organisms in increasing concentration along the food chain. This phenomenon is called biomagnification.

questions.question: Acid rain is caused by
questions.explanation: Oxides of sulphur and nitrogen react with water vapour present in atmospheric air to form sulphuric acid and nitric acid. These acids come down with precipitation, it is known as acid rain.

questions.question: Which of the following can help reduce air pollution?

options.option: Use of manure in place of fertiliser

questions.explanation: CNG is a clean fuel and does not contain sulphur.

questions.question: Potable water is
questions.explanation: Potable water is free from impurities and harmful microorganisms. It contains dissolved salts in appropriate amount and is absolutely fit for drinking.

questions.question: Fertilisers run off from agricultural fields into nearby water bodies leads to

options.option: Immediate killing of aquatic plants and animals

questions.explanation: Fertilisers run off from agricultural field into nearby water bodies causes their nutrient enrichment due to which dense growth of algae takes place known as algal bloom. The process is called eutrophication.

questions.question: Which of the following does not help in purification of water?
questions.explanation: Using solar cookers help to reduce air pollution.

questions.question: If some amount of pesticide is present in a water body then its highest amount will be accumulated in
questions.explanation: Pesticides are non-biodegradable chemicals and cannot be metabolised within the bodies of living organisms. If it is present in water bodies then its amount keep on magnifying at each trophic level of a food chain and is found to be maximum in top consumers.

questions.question:
Refer to the given Venn diagram.

Select the correct option regarding X and Y.

options.option: Y could corrode monuments and buildings made up of marble stone.
Y could destroy vegetation and decrease pH of water bodies making them unsuitable for survival of aquatic animals.
Gas X could be a gas produced by burning of fossil fuels.

questions.explanation: In the given Venn diagram, X could be CO2 and Y could be SO2 or NO2.

questions.question: Refer to the process (shown in the given figure) and select the incorrect option regarding it

options.option: Excessive burning of fossil fuels and deforestation will enhance the process leading to global warming.
Practical application of process can be seen in green houses built in cold countries which provide optimum heat for plant growth when actual temperature is very low.
The given process (in a limit) is essential for continuity of life on earth as it keeps earth warm and hospitable.
Gases like SO2, NO2, methane and water vapour are responsible for the given process.

questions.explanation: The given figure illustrates the greenhouse effect shown by some gases e.g., CO2, CH4, N2O, CFCs present in earth’s atmosphere. These gases are responsible for greenhouse effect.

questions.question:
Refer to the given arrow diagram.

Which of the following holds true for X

options.option: It may lead to rise in average temperature of earth thereby affecting polar regions where it can cause melting of polar ice caps.
It may cause rise in sea level dramatically and drowning of coastal areas consequently
It may cause changes in weather and precipitation patterns.

questions.explanation: In the given flow chart, X refers to global warming.

questions.question:
Read the given passage.
P, Q, R, and S are various greenhouse gases. P contributes 60%, Q contributes 6%, R contributes 14% and S contributes 20% to greenhouse effect.
Identify P, Q, R and S and select the incorrect statement regarding them.

options.option: Q is produced due to combustion of nitrogen rich fuels and nylon production.
S is produced by incomplete combustion of biomass.
R is odourless, and flammable gas used in jet fuels and refrigerators.
P increases in the atmosphere due to large scale deforestation.

questions.explanation: In the given passage, P is CO2, Q is nitrous oxide, R is CFCs and S is CH4. CFCs are odourless and non-inflammable gases used in jet fuels and refrigerators.
